## Page 15

Preface

Presented herewith is Book III of Deva Keralam (Chandra Kala Nadi) from sloka No. 1998 to
5774 (ie. 3796 slokas) - the portion compiled by Venkatesa.

‘The arrangement of the book goes thus:

Sloka No. 1 to 2718 by Achyuta in Book I

Sloka No. 2719 to 3407 of Achyuta, and sloka No.1 to 1997 of Venkatesa in Book II.

Sloka No. 1998 to 5774 by Venkatesa in Book III.

These 3 volumes put together have a total of 9181 slokas in all (3407 by Achyuta and 5774 by
Venkatesa) for which complete English translation has been provided by me.

{As the discerning reader understands, the work covers two areas of delineation. A particular
space of rising degree called Nadiamsa is used to depict a series of events to occur in the native’s life,
one of the subtle techniques of handling a horoscope. On the other hand, countless general principles
are enumerated on the lines done by popular series of text-books like Parasara Hora, Phala Deepika,
Saravali, Jataka Parijata and Sarvartha Chintamani dealing with natal horoscopy including dasa
effects, transits and the like. In some cases, general principles that do not consider Nadiamsa
technique are incorporated in the Nadiamsa reading. To avoid confusion to the reader, these have
been separated by me in the translation, as far as possible. Further, these have been more clearly
sorted out in the index given at the end of the present volume. The index now given embraces all the
3 parts.

‘As the reader knows, each sign has 150 Nadiamsas and the 12 signs have a total of 1800
Nadiamsas. It is not humanly possible to bring out a work dealing with all the 1800 Nadiamsas. To
the extent possible, the authors Achyuta and Venkatesa, gathered a‘large amount of information on
this most subtle division. An intelligent reader can multiply the use of a Nadiamsa four-fold keeping
in mind the three classifications of the 12 zodiacal signs into movable, fixed and common signs.

For example, a rule given for a certain Nadiamsa for Aries can be suitably extended to the other
three movable signs, viz. Cancer, Libra and Capricorn. Similarly the four fixed signs Taurus, Leo,
Scorpio and Aquarius can be brought under the second category. The three dual signs Gemini, Virgo,
Sagittarius and Pisces form part of the 3rd group. While doing so, the involvement of planets should
be judiciously understood. This is because the same planet plays a different role for each sign
ascending.

The text excels in merits in tactfully combining the usage of Vimsottari dasa and Kala Chakra
dasa. So also in applying transits with reference to the signs caused by certain Navamsa positions.
Many such rare techniques are available in this wonderful work. One who uses such techniques
frequently will be able to issue successful predictions

I sincerely believe that the reader will find our present efforts useful and forgive me for my
shortcomings if any.

Mr. Narinder Sagar of Sagar Publications deserves special thanks for undertaking the task of
bringing out this voluminous work considering the huge amount of money that the production of a
book of this size requires.

R Santhanam,
9/1040 Govindpuri,
Kalkaji, New Delhi 110 019
Telephone 6462777
Dated January 14, 1994

2000 - 20087. First Half: (a) The one belong-
ing to the first half will have no older co-born. He
would be either born, or will live, in a town
surrounded by-forests. The street of his birth will

be south-north spread, and the house will be west-
facing. Or the housé could be in the eastern part.
Ifthe birth be in the middle of the former half of the
said Nadiamsa, it will be in astreet spread from east,
to west, either in his own house (i.e. parental house)
or in others’ house.

(b) He will be entangled in umbilical cord atthe
time of birth. But the subject of second half of the
said Nadiamsa will not have such a birth.

(©) Thenative of former half of Kutila Nadiamsa
of Leo ascendant will be good-looking, intelligent,
eloquent, and will have charming face and eyes.
He will be fortunate, charitable and be a Soodra by
birth. His complexion wiltbe dark-bluish. He will
have a stout body, with imedium build, will be well
educated, expedient, eloquent and skilful in writ-
ing and learning. He will be a poet in his mother
tongue, be of sacrificial and charitable disposition,
will enjoy pleasures and be the best of the givers.
He will be endowed with one co-born, be wealthy,
and fond of eating flesh. He will have many kinds
oflearning and be devoted toLord Vishnuand Lord
Siva. Due to his devotion to Lord Vishnu, he will
notlisten to the words of his motherand father (i.e.
he will be so devoted to divine rituals that he will
not care much for his parents). He will be passion-
ate in disposition.

2009 - 20102. Lord of Ascendant: (a) In the
above case, if the lord of the ascendant, Sun, is
associated with a benefic, the native instead of
being passionate will be Satvic in disposition. (b)
Related to Mars: Should Mars aspect the Sun, the
subject will be irritable, be somewhat wicked and
be endowed with happiness concerning paternal
grand-father and fortunes through father. He will
also be happy in respect of mother and be endowed
with brothers and sisters.

2021 - 202672. The native of first half of Kutila
Nadiamsa will be devoted to Lords Vishnu and
Siva, be troubled by grief in the middle of his life,
and will after some time enjoy physical felicity. He
will be healthy (or happy) during childhood and
will enjoy paternal happiness. He will be devoted
to gods and preceptors and will be fortunate in
terms of agriculture and quadrupeds. He will have
two conveyances, be passionate and will have a
body with warm and windy temperament. In
between he will be troubled by windy disorders. He
will have a happy childhood, The middle part of his
life will cause grief. (This is a repetition.) He will
understand the minds of others; be skilful (in
general), will patronise his kinsfolk, be long-lived,
be a satisfied person, will secretly indulge in sins,
be fond of sexual union, will befriend persons

prostitutes, be miserly, fickle-minded, timid, sin-
ful, free from diseases but will at the end incur
windy disorders.

2031 - 2039%. Father: Mars ruling the 9th,
placed in own sign (Aries or Scorpio), and being
conjunct Saturn would cause a physique to father
which will be. bluish and humpbacked (or back
resembling that of bull). Further, father would be
a cause of difficulties to his (father’s) brothers, be
honourable and would be poor during childhood.
He would prosper following his headship of a
temple and would be honoured by aking. During
his childhood he would be poor, endangered and
would live by servitude. After the birth of ason, his
prosperity would commence. He would be liked by
aking ruling a forest-surrounded region and would
have headship of a place under the king. He would
also earn the favours of aminister through the good
offices of the king. He would have a prosperous
calling under a king ruling a forest-surrounded
region, would be endowed with royal attributes, be
skilful in amassing wealth, would earn fame in
royal assembly and would have wealth acquired by
questionable means. He would be fond of the
virtuous, be passionate, will befriend fallen people
and others and will thereby make financial gains.
He would take interest in deeds prescribed for men
of Lord Siva’s sect. He would construct tanks and
gardens for a Siva temple near Mahanadi (or great
river), would install a temple and worship gods and
Brahmins. His charities would be through ill-
acquired wealth.

2040-20431, Saturn-Marsin Aries: Should
Saturn be with Mars in Aries in aspectto Jupiter, the
following effects will accrue in respect of the father
of the subject belonging to Kutila Nadiamsa, first
half, in Leo ascendant. He would be secretively
sinful, would turn religious (i.e. be sinless) in old
age, would be munificent but without the knowl-
edge of others, be religiousin old age andbe happy.
He would be grieved on account of loss of brothers.
After the native’s 5th year of age, the father would
be happy, after the 8th year he would be wealthy,
after the 10th year he would acquire much wealth
and in the native’s 2nd dasa he would acquire a
conveyance. After the 12th year he would achieve
prosperity and wealth.

2044 - 2052. (a) Between the 22nd and 25th

years of the native’s life, (or) in hisascendantlord’s -

dasa, his paternal grand-father will pass away.
(b) Irrelevant: During the native’s first dasa,
with. Satum moving in Taurus, destruction of a
Yavana king will occur. In the region between
Kanchi and Pinakini, there will bea change of king.
There will be quarrels in the north-western direc-
tion. The king of the said region will be happy. In
the Pandya region, the king will quit the world; his

brother will become stronger. In the west of
Pandya quarrels and anarchy will be marked;
Mlecchas will take over after which the original
king will recover his lostkingdom and wealth. The
rulers of Chola and Pandya will be in the grip of fear
and destruction.

(©) The native’s father will gain elephants,
horses and robes through the king. His Raja yoga
effects will be on the increase and he will acquire
happiness after the 10th year of the native.

(@) Irrelevant: Ministers and Brahmins will be
in grief through the evil influences of Mleccha
kings. There will be strife and drought (poverty)
caused by enemies.

ing in Aries, the country will reel under droughtand
strife. The extent of drought will correspond to
placements of Jupiter and Saturn in the end of
Pisces/Scorpio, or in Gemini or in the middle of
Leo.

(b) Saturn in the 9th from the ascendant in the
company of Mars denotes that the subject will show
anger against his king (i.e. will misbehave with the
employer).

inkingdom. After strife, peace will be restored and

2062-2071. (a) Paternal Uncles: The number
of paternal uncles will correspond to the number of
Bindus in the Ashtaka Varga of the Sun.

(b) Co-born: The subject of Kutila Nadiamsa,

first half, in Leo ascendant will have delayed
obtainment of after-born. One of them will have
large testicles. The native will have one older
brother whom he'will be fond of.

(c) Saturn in the 3rd house in the company of
Mars and in aspect to Jupiter will cause happiness
through co-born. There will be three or five of
them.

@ (Without reference to Saturn & C) the
native will have one brother and one sister. The
said sister will be endowed with golden ornaments,
will marry into a prosperous family and will be
endowed with (long-living) husband and sons.

(© Mars ruling the house of sister’s husband
(ie. the 9th house which is the 7th from the 3rd)
should be in own sign in association with Saturn,
Then the sister of the native will be involved in
quarrels with her husband.

(e) The said Mars joining a debilitated planet
will denote that the sister's older brother-in-law
(ie. husband’s brother) will have enmity with her
husband. Z

(®) An ever truthful older brother-in-law is
denoted for the sister of the native of Kutila
Nadiamsa, Leo ascendant.

(g) He will gain fortunes by betraying his
employer and will also incur destruction. In the
native's 4th dasa fortunes will revive.

() Inthe 3rd dasa, orin the junctions of 2nd and
3rd dasas, the native will incur grief on account of
aco-born. Results about brother-in-law should be
told in detail with the help of operating dasa
periods.

2073- 2083¥. Curse of Previous Birth: Jupi-
ter in Libra in aspect to Saturn and Mars, and
begetting Pisces Navamsa would denote one with
the curse of a Brahmin in the previous birth (and
consequent delay in obtainment of progeny in the
current birth). To overcome this blemish, one
should take sacred dips in the Setu for a period of
3 weeks. Or else, it should be done with purity in
the company of family members fora period of one
week, months, or one year. A Soodra should take
dips after sun-rise. For a Brahmin dips for three
months are considered the best. To attain final
emancipation, things that are dear to the native
should be abandoned in the meal. To obtain a son,
recitation of “Hari Vamsa Kavya” should be done.
A Soodra should listen to this account through a
Brahmin, At the end of the month, Vyasa should be
worshipped as perrules. For3 months, on Pradosha
days, Lord Siva should be worshipped, particularly
on Mondays as stated in Kalpa. After these rituals,
the native of Kutila Nadiamsa will obtain progeny.
Orelse, he will obtain dead children. His first wife
will give birth to (only) female children. Due to the
performance of these remedies, she will, obtain

limited number of sons. In the absence of these
remedies, his first wife will never obtain sons. He
will however beget sons through second wife after
the age of 40, -Because of the blemish of Saturn -
Marstelationship as stated earlier, the sacred Linga
should be worshipped for 96 days and Brahmins
should be honoured with gifts of over a hundred
coins.

2084 - 2088. Marriage: (a) The 7th lord
Satur in the company of Mars denotes acquisition
of two wives, the second one being in the 4th dasa.
‘The first marriage will occur in the first dasa, in the
sub period of Jupiter, or in the 2nd dasa during the
first sub period or Venus's sub period, in spring
during abright lunarhalf. This applies tothenative
of first half of Kutila Nadiamsa. (Also see point “c”
below.)

(b) The native of second half of the subject
Nadiamsa will beget married in the second dasa,
with the Sun moving in Leo or Gemini.

(c) The person belonging to first half will have
his marriage in the month of Gemini. (See point “a”
above.)

2095 - 2102. First Half - Kutila Nadiamsa
(upto “e”).

(a) Thenative of formerhalf of Kutila Nadiamsa,
Leo ascendant will beget a dark-bodied spouse who
will be quite attached tohim, She will bea virtuous
woman, endowed with brothers and sisters. Out of
these co-born, one will be a brother and the lady
will incur obstacles in giving birth to children.
Other authorities prescribe two brothers for her.
One of them is said to be long-lived.

(b) The subject will have a re-marriage in
which case he will have two or three brothers-in-
law. The actual number of such brothers-in-law
should be deduced from the Ashtaka Varga.

(©) The said native will have two long-living
wives.

(d) According to another view, after the na-
tive’s 50th year, the first wife will quit the world,

(©) Inthe end of his second dasa, children born
to his first wife will face destruction.

(© Second half - Kutila Nadiamsa: One born in
this part in Leo ascendant will lose his wife in the
20th year.

(g) The native of Kutila Nadiamsa-(irrespec-

tive of first or second half) will incur enmity with
his spouse.

2103. The native of Kutila Nadiamsa, Leo

2104. Scorpio Ascendant - Sama Nadiamsa:
The native in question will be a Brahmin and be the
first issue of the family.

Notes: “Brahmamsa” in this part of the text
relates to birth in Brahmin family. Brahmin or
Brahmana in Vedic ages means - relating to or
given by Brahman who was supposed to be re-
quired to know the three Vedas to supervise sacri-
fices and set right the mistakes; aman belonging to
the Ist of the 4 original divisions of the Hindu body
but often in the present day a layman engaged in
non-priestly occupations although the name is
strictly only applicable to one who knows and
repeats the Veda. Thus the text implies birth in a
Brahmin family by words such as “Brahmamsa
yoga jatake”.

The words “Brahmamsa” need not be confused
with Brahma Nadiamsa as such.

For Sama Nadiamsa, please see page 141,
Book 1.

2105 - 2105¥, Sama Nadiamsa - Mars: (a)
Birth in Sama Nadiamsa in Scorpio with ascendant
lord Mars in an angle or ina trine in aspect to Jupiter
will be that of a Brahmin.

(b) The same effect will come to pass with Mars
in Leo getting Aries Navamsa.

Notes: (a) For birth in a Brahmin’ s family, this
yoga requires Jupiter's aspect on Mars who should
be in Ist, 4th, 7th, 10th, Sth or 9th from the
ascendant Scorpio.

(b) In this case Mars in Leo should get Aries
‘Navamsa, but need not be connected with Jupiter.

In modem ages, such a combination should be
interpreted as capable of giving knowledge related
to the Supreme Being.

2111 - 212% Sama Nadiamsa - Co-born:
One belonging to Scorpio ascendant will have
three brothers and two sisters (as against none in
case of second half). One of the brothers will have
instant death at the time of birth. One will be long-
lived. The middle of the brothers will have mental
derangement.

Notes: There is some confusion about events

## Page 24

provided eliminating such a confusion, Instant
death and long life are apparently applicable, in
order, to the first and third of the three brothers.
The middle one is destined to suffer from maladies
of the mind.

2113 -2117¥. (a) Ketu in 3rd: The wife of the
younger brother of the said native will be blessed
with sons but will be crafty if Ketu is in the 3rd.

(b) The Moon in an Angle: If the 9th lord
Moon is posited in an angle along with Rahu, the
sister-in-law will be somewhat disposed to be fond
of questionable history.

(©) VirgoNavamsa: Shouldbirth be in Scorpio
ascendant begetting Virgo Navamsa as Rahu is in
the 9th from ascendant (i.e. in Cancer), the native’s
sister-in-law will blame her parents-in-law (i.e. she
will be disobedient to them). One of the sisters-in-

_ law will further be unable to bear progeny and will
not care for her husband. Yet another will have
some physical defect, will have two sons and two
daughters, be well honoured, and will be weak-
bodied due to some abortions. She will be fond of
her brothers and sisters and will occasionally be
subjected to troubles.

2118 - 2126. Sama Nadiamsa - Scorpio As-
cendant: (a) The subject will be sinful being in the
company of whores if Ketu is in the 3rd in Capri-
com as the 7th lord (Venus) is aspected by Mars.

(b) Brothers: A brother of the native of first
half of this Nadiamsa will be versed in the Vedas.

Another will beget a spouse who will never be
able to give birth to a child.

The last one will have three wives who will be
domineering. He will bea fool, a beggar, given to
anger, will have a troubled life which he will eke
out through the use of almanacs, will lead self-
eamed wealth apart from some riches throught
brothers, will also incur grief through a division
with brothers, will migrate to foreign lands and will
enjoy limited happiness. Afterwards, there will be
some troubles to his brothers while he will shift to
live in a coly of Brahmins. He will be attached to
his profession, will live through Vedic calling, will
commit sinful acts, will be the husband of a wicked
woman, be irritable and be adventurous in his
deeds, will indulge in arrogant acts, will go without
progeny due to the sins of betraying his brothers,
will not be grateful to his brothers, be inwardly
cunning and will go begging from house to house
belonging to men of various castes.

‘The middle one of the brothers will be mad.

2139 - 2142%. Rahu in 9th: The subject of
second half of Satna Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant,
with Rabu in the 9th house will beget the following
effects. He will have two brothers, be long-lived,
will live in a colony of Brahmins and will have
knowledge of Puranas, philosophy and justice. He
will be fond of his elder brother, be devoted to Lord
Siva, will have much hair on his physique, be
learned, of pure consciousness, highly munificent,
fond ofascetics, devoted to gods, firm in prosperity
and endowed with justice and expedients. He will
be widely famous, will enjoy pleasures, be indo-
lent, will have good qualities, will also indulge in
sinful deeds, be emaciated and endowed with
auspicious marks.

2143-2144¥4, Saturn in 2nd: If Saturnisin the
2nd house, declare the following effects. The
person will be of crooked disposition, be fond of
cating things that are eatable as well as uneatable,
be untruthful, libidinous, pure in mind and be
fortunate. If Saturn is in a malefic’s Navamsa and
acquires malefic aspects, the subject will take to
bad path. He will be devoid of truth and purity.

in Scorpio ascendant, with Mercury's Navamsa

Arudha Lagna in aspect to or in association with
Rahu while the 8th from the Arudha Lagna is
occupied by amalefic will cause a specific kind of
Kemadruma Yoga. Asaresult, the native will be in

various countries.

2175 - 2181: (a) Jupiter ruling the Sth house
should join Saturn and occupy his own sign in
Vargottama. As a result the native of Scorpio
ascendant willbe opulent, be aking’s preceptor but
will attain hell after his death. He may attain
heavens (only) due to the good deeds of his grand-
sons.
(b) Father: The lord of the 9th, the Moon,

being in exaltation without malefic association
will cause following effects concerning the na-
tive’s father. He will be liberal, saintly, will be
attached to his wife (without any illicit associa-
tions) and be happy.

(©) Father: Rabu in the 9th house will give
following effects-in respect of the native’s father.
He will have self-earned wealth, be a famous
astrologer and will have a moderate living. At the
time of his mother’s death he will have two sisters.
She will die on a Friday during which time, the
native’ paternal great grand-mother will be alive.
‘The native’ sfather will be happy, will prosper after
marriage, be lonely, will earn favours from kings
and ministers. Following the age of 42, he will live
inashrine. After this, he will migrate to forests and
enjoy much happiness through his sons.

In Surya Nadi, effects concerning father have
been narrated in full for birth in the former half of
Sama Nadi, Scorpio ascendant.

2182 - 2184. In case of birth in the latter half
of Sama Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant, the father
will live by beggary. The native’s paternal grand-
father would live in the region of Kerala, by
carrying loads. After some time he will return to his
native place and die before his son. His paternal
grand-mother will be long lived and would have
three sons and three daughters.

2187 - 2188. Special Rule - Kali Yuga:
According to sage Garga, one bom (even) with
three exalted planets will not beget Rajayoga ef-
fects unless there is planet in simultaneous debil-
ity. This applies to the Kali Yuga. In the said Yuga,
without a debilitated planet, an exalted planet will
become ineffective. Only with simultaneous pres-
ence of exalted and debilitated planets, Raja Yogas
will fructify. Without debilitated planet, one with
only an exalted planet will incur poverty.

Notes: This seems to be a general rule applica-
ble to various ascendants.

2195-2197. Marsin Aspect to Jupiter: Mars
ruling the house of maternal uncles (ie. the 6th -
Aries) should be in aspect to Jupiter and be in Aries
Navamsa, in case of birth in Sama Nadiamsa,
Scorpio ascendant. Then a maternal uncle of the
subject will be endowed with longevity. The
subject will be emaciated due to early loss of
mother. Two other maternal uncles will be blessed
with male issues. They will lose their daughters
and be in grief. One maternal uncle will lead family
life asa married man and be a student of the Vedas.
He will be displaced in a political calamity and will
live in a town surrounded by forests by trading
activities with Soodras. He will be hypocritical and
will earn wealth.

in former half of Sama Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascend-
ant with Rabu in the 9th house, as the 12th lord
Venus is aspected by Mars will give the following
effects. One’s maternal uncle’s wife will be of
questionable history. She will be in troubles when
foreigners attack her country; her modesty will be
outraged. Later on she will leave the country in
grief, She will be sad losing her son and husband.

2202 -2204¥2, Rahu - Moon - Venus: Rabu in
the 9th, the Moon in the 7th in exaltation, and
‘Venus associated with Mars by aspect or company
will produce the following results.

(a) The native will have three wives. He will
also be involved with others’ women.

(b) His wife’s sister will be molested, will
become a widow and will have a questionable
history. In the absence of these effects occurring to
assister of the person's wife, these can happen toa
daughter of his maternal uncle.

(c) The native will beget married in the last one
third part of the 3rd dasa. The first one third of the

while the Moon is in the 7th house will give the
following effects for a person born in Sama
Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant. He will nearly die
due to delirium (in the early years). He will be
patronized bya Soodraking. In the 3rd dasa, he will
join auspicious men and will gain conveyances,
robes, wealth and ornaments. For two years during
the said dasa he will be in extreme difficulties. The
period following his 14th year of age will produce
much distress. He will live in a different country.
He will gather self-made wealth.

2210 - 2214. Venus in a Trine: If Venus is in '

atrine (Pisces or Cancer) while Marsis in the 10th

(Leo) and Rahu in 9th (Cancer) will probably cause
three marriages to the co-bom of the native. In the
sub period of the Sun during the fifth dasa, the
native’ brother will be endangered. In Venus sub
period, his marriage will occur. He will have some
troubles on account of fever. The sub period of
Mercury (the 8th lord) in any dasa will cause fever
(or soine illness) but instant recovery.

Sama Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant had (useful)
friends in the past birth and will have such friends
in the presentand future births. He will be relieved
from all kinds of debts.

ofthe Moon or the Sun. Both of them (i.e. the native

2230 - 2236. During the 54th year or the 61st
year, the mother of the native of former half of
Sama Nadiamsa will be endangered. Saturn
transiting the sign identical with the Navamsa of
the I thlord, ora trine thereof, could also endanger
the said native’s mother. During Saturn’s move-
ment in Libra, much difficulties will follow. In
Saturn dasa, with Saturn moving in Virgo (the
11th), the native will earn much fortunes. There
will be thread ceremony for his son. His friends
circles will enjoy great happiness. He will obtain
good results through a king. In the 6th dasa,
following 56th year, with Jupiter in Taurus, during
the sub period of Venus in the dasa of Jupiter, he

will take to ascetic order due to some family
calamity. He will quit the world ina shrine located
on the banks of ariver, ona Thursday afternoon and
go to Brahma Loka. He will be instantly re-born.

2237 - 2239Y2: Gemini Ascendant - Indra Na-
diamsa: The native of second half of Indra
‘Nadiamsa, Gemini ascendant will be a Soodra. He
will be either the 7th, 6th or the 8th issue of the
family. He will have younger co-born but be
endowed with only elder brothers and elder sisters.
His birth will be in the afternoon, in the region of
a shrine with a river. He will lose his father in
childhood. His mother will be long-lived.

2241 - 2247, (a) Saturn in an angle or ina trine,
‘endowed with full strength and occupying his own
Navamsa will cause a bluish physique to the native
of former half of the subject Nadiamsa. Such a
native will not enjoy happiness in his childhood.

(b) If Saturn isin the Navamsa of the Moon, the
said native will enjoy Rajayoga effects. He will
experience happiness through after-born.

(©) One belonging to the second half of the
subject Nadiamsa will earn money through trading
activities.

(@) The subject of former half will earn wealth
through royal service, as a minister or a justice.
After the age of ten, he will be happy and his home.
will abound in quadrupeds and wealth.

(€) One of second half of the subject Nadiamsa
will enjoy the blessings of goddess Lakshmi, i.e.
will be opulent.

(f) The person belonging to former half will
beget Rajayoga effects after the age of 30.

(g) One belonging to second half of this
Nadiamsa will gather wealth after his second mar-
riage and be happy throughout his life. He will
have three sons and abundant fortunes and wealth.
After 30, he will enjoy greathappiness, quadrupeds
and wealth.

2261 - 2263. Results for Father-in-law: If the
Sun ruling the father-in-law (3rdiis the 9th from the
7th) joins Mercury and begets a friendly Navamsa
while Venusis in the 3rd, the native’s father-in-law
would be highly charitable. He would have many
sons, be honourable, and would have limited number
of brothers. He would be endowed with much
wealth, be virtuous and dear to the king. He would
follow Vedic codes and would face troubles in

childhood. His prosperity would increase in the
middle part of his life following which he would
turn orthodox.

rius Navamsa will cause many brothers-in-law to

enjoy much prosperity and happiness. He will have
plenty of wealth and grains (food comforts). His

Indra Nadiamsa, Gemini ascendant, if Rahu is in
the Sth house from the ascendant, one daughter will
be endowed with a long-living husband. Another
will undergo many ordeals. Long-living husband
will be possible only when she is childless. If she
acquires a child, it will cause her widowhood as
well.

she will join other men.”

case of the said native, the 11th lord (Mars) is in
aspect to a benefic, while the 5th from the 11th (i.e.
the 3rd from ascendant) is occupied by a malefic,
will cause enmity with older brother.

(b) Without reference to Mars & C above, two
of the subject's elder brothers will have progeny.

(©) The middle part of Venus dasa, or the sub
periods of Mars and Rahu will cause loss of co-

will incur penury. Another will enjoy happiness.

2303 -2306. (a) Ashtaka Varga: Inthe chapter
on Ashtaka Varga, effects for Mars Ashtaka Varga,
have been stated in connection with results for
father’s brothers. The number of such uncles
endowed with longevity will correspond to the
number of final Bindus after reductions in Mars
Ashtaka Varga.

(b) The number of brothers his father will have
will correspond to the sign position or Navamsa
position of Mars (counted from Aries).

span should be known.

Nadiamsa, Gemini ascendant.

2307 -2309Y%. (a) The native will obtain prog-
eny after the age of 40.

(b) Venus in Arudha Lagna: If the Sth lord
Venus isin the ArudhaLagna, orin a trine from the
ascendant, his Dasa will cause desirable effects.
Year after year, auspicious effects will increase.
The subject will gain in his business activities. In
his old age he will be honoured by the king and
enjoy happiness through his sons. He will have
self-earned wealth and will destroy penury.

Notes: (a) The term “Chandramsa Yogaja”
adds to confusion.

(b) Venusbeing a favourable planet for Gemini
ascendant, his occupancy of a trine or the Arudha
Pada of the ascendant, will be capable of causing
good effects in his dasa.

2312 - 2313. Leo Ascendant - Mudgara Na-
diamsa: (a) The Sun in Taurus in Sarva Nadiamsa,
being in the company of Rahu and in aspect to Mars
will cause diseases to the father of the subject
native.

(b) His father will perform charitable acts like
construction of tanks and gardens (for public ben-
efit) if the Sun in Sarva Nadiamsa is in aspect to
Mars.
Notes: (a) Details of Mudgara Nadiamsa could
be found in the notes under sloka 1379, page 125,
Book 1.

Sarva Nadiamsa is not found in our list. This
may be another name for Mudgara Nadiamsa, as it
is clubbed together under one heading in the fol-
lowing verses.

(b) The placement for Mars in “Durbala
Nadiamsa” is to be ignored.

2314 - 2315. Brothers: (a) Should Mars be in
his own sign with benefic divisions while the Sun
in strength occupies Sarva Nadiamsa, the subject
native will have no brothers.

(b) Mars in Scorpio with Sagittarius Navamsa,
or Pisces Drekkana, will also cause the same effect.

Notes: (b) Mars in Scorpio 16° 40' to 20°
acquires Sagittarius Navamsa. In the same sign,
the space from 10° to 20° gives Pisces decanate.
ForLeo ascendant, Mars in the 4th with Sagittarius
Navamsa isa powerful hindrance for acquisition of
co-born. The rule concerning decanate should be
used only after giving attention to Venus as well.

similar transit with reference to the Moon-occu-
pied Navamsa will kill the mother of the native in
the 2nd dasa. The corresponding months will be
denoted by the Sun's transit in Virgo through

io.

+ (b) Danger to Father: During the 4th dasa
when Saturn moves in the end of Sagittarius or in
Capricom, his father will die in a shrine,

(©) The second line of verse 2320 is abruptly

(@) Prosperity: The native of Sarva Nadiamsa,
Leo ascendant, will prosper after the age of thirty.
The middle of Saturn’ sDasa will cause distress and
loss of the first issue.

(©) Father-in-law: The 3rd lord Venus in
aspect to Saturn and Mars will endanger the na-
tive’s mother-in-law during the third dasa, His
father-in-law will live asa widower fora long time.
He will incur a lot of grief in his old age due to loss

() The Native: Under the same combination,

(g) Father: The Sun in the 10th in aspect to
Jupiter denotes that the father of the person will
perform good deeds. During his childhood, he

diseases. He would be fond of liquor, and be
virtuous and happy.

‘Taurus with Aquarius Navamsa while Venus is in

belonging to a fixed sign ascending will worship

houses and lands to others. He will believe in Gods

Should Mercury be in Harina Nadiamsa, the person
will involve himself in deeds related to gods (or

acts and will patronise gods and Brahmins.

(b) Mars in Harina Nadiamsa will usher pros-
perity and growth after the age of 28. Particularly
after 35, his fortunes will incur much progress.

or 500 gold coins after his 35th year.

2348-2351. Immovable Ascendant: One will
perform fire sacrifices as stipulated in the Vedas
and be fond of donating money if the 11th lord is in
aspect to Saturn while Mercury is in Harina Nadi-
amsa. In the context of Sacrifice, donation should
be given with Resolution. At the end of the rituals,
donation of a cow will bring forth more auspicious
effects. The donation should be given after proper
spiritual initiation, with a full heart. (Even) beg-
ging for the sake of these three deeds (viz. Sacrifice
etc.) is nota blemish. In Kali Yuga, a Brahmin in
the vicinity will ward off blemishes.

One with Jupiter in Harina Nadiamsa will have one
son. He will have four excellent sons if...... is ina

issues as well, and be endowed with both sons and
daughters. Another sister will be childless. Yet
another will be in grief. The views on Harina
Nadiamsa are as per the school of sage Garga.

2365 - 2366%. General: Now stated are the
effects of Saturn in the 12 signs in order, counted
from the ascendant or the Moon as the case may be.

(7) Great courage (8) Death (9) Dehydration of the
physique (10) Troubles (11) Gains (12) Losses.

currency of Scorpio - Mars (KCD), when Jupiter is
in his 4th round of transit, the native’s prosperity
will gradually increase.

(b) Libra Dasa: The Dasaof_Libra- Venus will
produce much happiness. 40th year will give
wealth.. The middle of Libra Dasa will cause
limited number of children.

(©) Scorpio Dasa: During the Dasa of Scorpio
- Mars, the native’s daughter will obtain progeny.

@) Libra Dasa: In his Libra - Venus Dasa,
much happiness will occur to his daughter. This

quadrupeds and wealth in the native’s family.

Dasa, the native will die, with the transit Sun in
Aquarius, Pisces or Aries.

2397 - 2399. Aries Ascendant - Kutila Nadi-
amsa: (a) The native will be a valorous Brahmin.
He will be learned in Vedas and other Sastras, will
patronise his brothers and will live in a colony of
Brahmins. His profession will be in a village. He
will obtain fame in royal service.

(b) One born in Aries ascendant with Asvini
(4th quarter) with Cancer Navamsa will bea Soodra
and be the eldest issue. There is another authority
to state that he will be the 2nd issue, will not have
(living) elder co-born but will have younger ones.

current verses and the following ones deal with
Aries ascendant.

notes under sloka 130 of Book 1.

2400 - 2402. Aries Ascendant - Kutila Nadi-
amsa: (a) He will be a Soodra, happy in disposition,
will not have an elder co-born, be handsome in
appearance, be born in a remote village or a town,
and will have one younger co-born. His father will
die in a distant land.

(b) The Sun ina Navamsaruled by Satu while
the Moon is in Kutila Nadiamsa denotes that the
person is born to his parents after a great deal of
delay (or longing).

subject Nadiamsa extend upto sloka 2425 infra.

ticable. Thus it should only relate to Kutila

2404 - 2405. The native with the Moon in the
4th (Cancer) in joint aspect to Saturn and Mars will
be indolent, intelligent, passionate and disposed to
damage others’ wealth. He will be libidinous, will
join whores, be a servant and be after ministers (or
powerful people). He will suffer from leprosy and
other diseases and be eloquentin speech. His body
will be affected by heat and urinary disorders.
Notes: In sloka 2405, "Amatya Preshya
Lolupah” is another reading which means the'na-
tive will be a minister's servant and will possess
destructive instincts. The word "lolupa" has two
meanings, thus: destructive, longing.

be in his exaltation sign (in the 10th, Capricorn).

courageous and fond of good deeds.

2409 - 2422's, Aries Ascendant: (a) The Na-
tive: Aries ascendant, with solar Hora and Leo
decanate rising (i.e. the ascendant being anywhere
between 10° and 15°) while Saturn is in the 2nd
from the ascendant (i.e. in Taurus) will deprive one
of happiness irr boyhood days. He will be devoted
to Lord Siva.

(b) Mother: The 4th lord Moon in Cancer in
Kuntala Nadiamsa will cause widowhood to mother
who will live for a long time. She will be grieved

‘on account of loss of progeny and emaciated due to
loss of co-born. Her co-bom will live in distant
## Page 46

Chandra Kala Nadi 31

lands and she would be brought up by other rela-
tives.

(© Brother: One will obtain the. following

results in respect of a brother if Mars is in the 10th
with the Sun, while Venus is in Pisces with a
Navamsa of Mars (i.e. Scorpio). The brother will
be happy for many days due to dips in the sacred
Ganges (i.e. he will go on a long time pilgrimage).
His sons will be destroyed due to tuberculosis. He
will incur grief on account of mother in a foreign
country and will live with his kinsfolk. He will
cause perpetual distress to his spouse and will be
separated from elder co-bornand will incur various
kinds of grief, These are the effects concerning a
brother.
(d) Father: If the 9th lord Jupiter is in Vargot-
tama in Aquarius, the native’s father will serve
Brahmins, will have brothers and sisters. He will
eke out his livelihood by trading, and also by
serving Soodras. He will leave his country and die
a bad death,

(©) Paternal Uncles & Aunts: The Moon in
Kuntala Nadiamsa will give the following effects.
One of the paternal uncles of the native will be
childless. Another will be a chief and a famous
person receiving royal honours, He will be devoted
Lord Siva, will have two wives and good sons. One
will be somewhat sinful and will have three or four
sons or will have many sons and daughters. Two of
the paternal.uncles will be endowed with long
lives. One paternal aunt will obtain progeny after
along time and will be in grief. Two or three aunts
will be long-lived. One will have male issues. Two
aunts will be widowed. A paternal uncle’sson will
enjoy authority under a king and will have many
sons, much wealth and step-brothers.

Notes: (a) In sloka 2410, the expression "Siva
Varma Gharah" isa corrupttext. Itshouldbe "Siva
Karma Parah" meaning that the ssubject will be
devoted to Lord Siva's services.

2423 - 2425. Aries Ascendant - Kutila Nadi-
amsa: (a) The beginning and concluding parts of
the second half of the native’ s third Dasa will cause
prosperity of his relatives. Due to association with
a son of paternal uncle, the native’s fortunes will
progress and auspicious results will come forth.

(b) The lord of the ascendant (Mars) in the 10th
in exaltation together with the Sun and in aspect to
the Moon will cause dips in Ganges.

(©) These effects are for the 2nd half of the
subject Nadiamsa.

2426 - 2429. (a) Now specially the effects
‘concerning brother are told. (But the text does not
speak of any such thing in detail but refers to dips
in Ganges and getting rid of sins of the last seven
births. Apparently, there is a loss of text.)

(b) The 9th house aspected by Rabu will cause
grief to a brother on account of loss of spouse. Due
to such grief, the said brother’s life-span will
diminish and he will go to hell.

(c) The 9th-lord (Jupiter) in Kalakoota
Shashtiamsa or in Saturn’s Navamsa while Rahu is,
in the 3rd (in Gemini) will destroy the brother
through a female.

These effects are applicable to birth in 2nd half
of Kutila Nadiamsa, in Aries.

2430 - 243344. Kutila Nadiamsa - First Half:
(a) Birth in this segment denotes that the native’s
brother will indulge in sinful deeds.

(b) If Rahu is in the 8th or the 9th, Satur is in
the 3rd and Venus is in exaltation, the native will
not be able to perform last offices to his father. But
after some time in the end of the year, he will do
related rituals in a shrine with devotion.

@ If the dispositor of the 4th lord is conjunct
Jupiter and Merury in aspect to Rahu, the mother
should worship Lord Siva with excluively the
Pranava Mantra by which she will be rid of trag-
edies like grief concerning progeny and will have
her family grow.

2434 - 2438. (a) Should the Moon be in the 6th
in aspect to Mars, the native’s father’s dynasty will
expand while that of the mother will be impeded.
(b) The lord of the 6th (Mercury) in aspect to
the 8th lord (Mars) while Rahu and Mars are in a
trine from Mercury will bring obstacles in the
marriage of the native’s daughter. She will marry
after delay and be in poverty. By donating the
image of Goddess Lakshmi, her husband will earn
fortunes. She will experience lot of grief and will
begeta daughter after some time. These have been
stated in “Garbha Hora” (read Garga Hora). Due to
the good fortunes of her father (i.e. the native), she
will obtain many daughters and be long-lived.

2439 - 2445. Scriptural Injunctions: In case
of births relating to the 4 castes (ie. Brahmin,
Kshatriya, Vaisya and Soodra), marrriage of a girl
should be done only after her attaining puberty.
Otherwise, following such a marriage, (the merits
of) seven past generations will be burnt. So had
been said by Manu and Parasara. The science of
astrology also declares such effects. A girl of 8 is
known as Kanya, of 9 Rohini, of 10 Gauri and
above 10 Rajasvala (who attained her puberty and
is fit for marriage). For Brahmins, Kshatriyas and
Vaisyas, the giving away of a Kanya is important
(i. e. is recommerided). For Soodra, marrriage is
allowed of a Rohini or Gauri. If there be obstacles
in performing the marriage of a son or daughter,
worship of a Sumangali (a female whose husband
is alive), Maha Lakshmi and Gauri (i.e. Lakshmi
and Parvati) should be done as prescribed by
Sastras. Lakshmi and Parvati should be offered
Abhisheka (ritual bath) with milk and turmeric.
Clothes should be given away to a Simnagali.
These should be done once, and many times (i.e.
again and again), with purity. Then the hurdles will
be removed and marriage will take place.

Notes: An eight year old girl is called Gauri
(and notKanya) anda ten year old girl isKanya (not
Gauri). Apparently, the text in question has been
wrongly copied. ‘Thus the first 3 castes were
permitted to marry off their daughter after 10 while
Soodra can marry off his daughter even at 8 or 9, as
per Sastras. More suh information can be found in
Brihad Daivagna Mano Ranjanam.

2446 - 2449. (a) The Moon in Kuntala Nadi-
amsa in aspect to Satum and Mars will cause
obstacles in the performance of son’s marriage for
which remedies should be done.

(b) The 9th and 3rd lords should be together in
the 11th from the ascendant. When Saturn transits
the 11th thereof (i.e. 9th from the ascendant), the
native’s daughter will get married. Alternatively,
Saturn can also aspect the sign identical with the
Navamsa occupied by the 3rd lord. The corre-
sponding months will be denoted by the Sun
transiting in a sign which is a trine to such Nav-
amsa.

(©) The Dasa of the 7th lord will cause marriage
and other auspicious effects.

2450 - 2451. Aries Ascendant - Kutila Nadi-
amsa 2nd Half: (a) The native will accumulate
wealth, He will be involved in the marriage of his,
brother in his 5th Dasa. Marriage will be settled
after two or three obstacles. So alsoin respect of all
undertakings and acquisition of progeny.

(b) The lord of the 9th should be associated
with a benefic. In that case, the number of Bindus
in his Ashtaka Varga will denote the number of
issues for the native. His daughter will be charm-
ing.

Notes: (a) The relevant text is not clearly
composed. Apparently it suggests that the native’s
brother will incur obstacles in respect of marriage,
progeny and other auspicious events.

2452 - 2454. The lord of the 5th (the Sun) in a
Navamsa of Venus denotes that the subject will
marry a person who will give birth toonly one child
and then become barren. She will be charming,
virtuous, dark in complexion and will have one
brother, The native’s brother-in-law will have one
son. His sister-in-law will have two or three sons
and would be wicked in her childhood. She would
also incur danger to her survival due to planetary
blemishes for which remedies would be sought.

2455 - 2460%. (a) Brother’s Son: If the lord
of the ascendant Mars is in exaltation, the native’s
brother’s son will be happy, will have two wives,
will enjoy pleasures and will inherit paternal un-
cle’s wealth. He will also have self-earned wealth
and will enjoy fortunes through his kinsfolk.

() One born in Aries ascendant with the Sun in
the 10th house with Virgo Navamsa will be skilful
in gathering wealth and will patronize his brother's,
son.

(©) In the Dasa of Venus, when Saturn transits
the 12th, the subject will incur troubles from the
king due to enmity with Vaishnavites.

(d) During the native’s first dasa, his paternal
uncle will lose his spouse and will have second
marriage. The native’ s father will be endowed with
happiness. Later on, the subject will lose a sister
## Page 49

and will acquire a brother. His education will _

commence in Mercury - Saturn periods. The end
of the first dasa will cause distress to father due to
troubles from fever. Also his kinsfolk will be
mentally disturbed.

2461 - 24704, Irrelevant: The head of the
region will die and a war will break in the northern
part of the country. In the periods of Jupiter and
Saturn, the king in the southern part of the country
and the army chief will perish. When Jupiter and
Saturn journey in the end of Sagittarius or in the
beginning of Capricorn, the land of Pandya king-
dom will be in grief and there will be fear of
untimely death. Saturn’s transit in the end of Cap-
ricom ora trine thereof will cause a change of ruler.
Saturn in the ascendant, 2nd, 8th or 10th will bring
sorrow and danger to the king. The Sunin one of the
four immovable signs, with a Navamsa of Venus
.. While the planet Saturn transits a trine ..... the

king of Pandya country will be destroyed. The
king’s son and the army chief will face untimely
death, When Saturn transits Pisces or the sign
identical with the Navamsa occupied by the Sth
lord, the king of the native’s country will incur
diséases and be destroyed. When Saturn comes to
the 8th, or the Navamsa of the 9th lord, there will
occur quarrels among the king’s relatives.

2473 - 2476. (a) The sub period of the 4th lord
in the Dasa of the 5th lord will bring professional
fortunes under a changed ruler.

(b) For about ten years, his paternal uncle’s son
will be in difficulties after which he will prosper
and the kinsfolk will make wealth.

(©) During the 33rd or 27th year of age, in the
3rd dasa, the native’ s father will be endangered. At
that time Saturn will be in transit in Aries or in

2477-2481. (a) Saturn’ s transitin the 7th (read
10th) in Capricorn will cause mother’s death.

(b) Troubles to co-born, mental distress to
relatives and destruction of a patemal uncle will
result the transit of Saturn in the 5th (in Leo).

(©) When Saturn moves in the ascendant or in
the end of the 12th during the Dasa of the 2nd lord,
the person’s paternal uncle will be in grief. If the
Dasa of the 4th lord (the Moon) and the sub period
of the 8th lord (Mars) be in operation, paternal
uncle will be endangered by fear of untimely death.

(@) Saturn in transit in the sign identical with
the Navamsa occupied by the lord of the 8th (Mars),
the native of Kuntala Nadiamsa will be greatly
endangered by fear of untimely death provided the
Dasa is the 5th one.

(©) When Saturn transits in Sagittarius, he will
be troubled by windy diseases. But his daughter
will beget married and he will obtain a grand-son.

2483 - 2501. (a) The learned prescribe difficul-
ties in the junction periods of two Kala Chakra
Dasas.

(b) Physical emaciation is denoted in the 5th
dasa. (The term “Sukra Chakra” is to be excluded
for better understanding.)

(c) Should the Moon be in Kuntala Nadiamsa,
the transit of malefics in Deha Rasi will cause
## Page 51

36 Deva Keralam

diseases of the body, coinciding with adverse solar
movements. To overcome this blemish, remedies
should be resorted to.

(d) Remedies: A vessel made of bell-metal
weighing 128 or 64 or 32 Palas should be obtained
in which an image of the Moon-God made of silver,
weighing 16 Palas should be placed on rice grains
of 16 Prasthas and should be covered with clothes,
in a pavilion and should be worshipped with in-
cense, lamps and flowers. The image of the Sun-
God made of gold should also be placed in a copper
vessel and worshipped as stated earlier for the
Moon-God. The Sun-God should be offered rice
boiled in milk with sugar while the Moon-God
should be offered with rice cooked in milk mixed
with plantains following which the fear of untimely
death will vanish. Reciting the remedial Mantras
as per rules will destroy diseases and increase life-
span. Then there will be no infantile mortality, nor
untimely death. His wealth and fame and dynasty
will grow. His enemies will be humiliated. His
daughter will enjoy long marital life and husband
will ive long. By praying to the Sun and the Moon,
one’s desires will always be fulfilled. By the
donation of the images of the Sun-God and Moon-
God to an astrologer, these Gods will be well-
pleased and will bless the person with longevity.
‘These donations should be given away in the dasa
periods of the luminaries, or during malefic plan-
ets’ periods, or during malefic transits or in bad
times. With the mercy of the Sun and the Moon, the
native will ever enjoy auspicious effects. Longev-
ity, health, fame etc. will prevail in an excellent
manner. This sin-destroying secret has been re-
vealed by sage Narada for the welfare of the world.
These are spoken by the Brahmin Achyuta with
happiness. Those wise men who perform this
worship will live happily. Mrityunjaya Maha
Mantra should be got recited with the help of a
Brahmin, Once upon a time, Markandeya recited
this and got relieved of the noose of death. By
reciting this for 48 or 24 days, death will be
destroyed. Asa result, longevity, health and pros-
perity will grow day by day.

Kutila Nadiamsa, with the Moon in Kuntala Nadi-

to the mercy of Sun God and Moon God. He will
construct a new house. His relatives will celebrate
birth of a child, He will make money with the help
of his paternal uncle’s son. In the sub period of
Pisces or Scorpio, he will incur death. From the age
of 27, he will be in danger for a period of 2 years.

2510-2511. Note the 8th lord from the ascend-
ant or the Moon sign and note in which Navamsa
the said 8th lord is placed. When Saturn transits the
sign identical with that Navamsa or a trine, with
‘Vedha (obstruction in transit) by amalefic, moder
ate results (instead of completely good results) be

of the said transit and those of the birth should be
minutely examined, Strengths of the dasa lord and
sub period lord should also be studied. Otherwise
the effects will be’ futile.

moving in the ascendant, 12th or the 8th. Saturn in
transit should also be exceedingly adverse, and the
Dasa should be one capable of causing difficulties.

undertaken, If this remedy is done with devotion,
one will acquire success, benefits and fame.

2517 - 2520. General: (a) When Jupiter tran-
sits the sign identical with the Navamsa occupied
by the 11th lord or the 9th lord, happiness, employ-
ment and wealth will occur.

(b) Wealth will come to pass if the said transit
is in a sign that is identical with the Navamsa
occupied by the 2nd lord or the 11th lord.

(©) Should Satum replace Jupiter as at “b”
above, the person’s relatives will be greatly endan-
gered.

end of the Sth dasa will produce sickness to the
native’s wife. His relatives will be in great distress
and in-laws will be subjected to evils.

2551 - 2561. (a) During the “sub periods” of the
Moon and Sun (read the sub period of the Sun, in
the Dasa of the Moon), the native’s daughter will
be happy and will obtain a male issue. His so
law will also remain pleased.

(b) The junction period of the Dasas of the 4th
lord (Moon) and Mars will cause auspicious events
in the family and birth of a son will occur. But the
native’s kinsmen will be distressed (for other rea-
sons).

(c) In another text, for birth in Kuntala
Nadiamsa, Aries ascendant, the end of the 4th
lord’s Dasais said to inflictsadness on relatives and
evils in (from) king’s circles.

(@ Saturn moving in Aquarius in the junction
periods of Moon and Mars will cause mental
worries, The native’s paternal uncle will die.

(e) The Dasa of ascendant lord Mars will cause
wealth, grains (food comforts) and progeny. The
person will prosper on account of his association
with the son of his paternal uncle. He will obtain
prosperous employment and a good son.

(f) The sub period of Mars (in the dasa of Mars)
will cause fever during the periods of adverse
movements of the Sun.

(g) Satum’s stay in Aquarius will cause (trou-
blesome) fever to one’s daughter. She will incur
illness due to delivery of a child and also mental
distress. Later on her health will be normal but she
will be emaciated. Malefics’ sub periods} ‘1th
lord's (i.e. Saturn's) Dasa will also cause | se
events. Illness will occur with adverse movements
of the Sun. Suitable remedies be performed.

(h) If a malefic is placed in the 8th, its sub
period in the Dasa of the 8th lord will produce fear
of danger from untimely death.

(i) Financial gains will occur through ministe-
rial favours in the sub period of the 9th lord during
the Dasa of the lord of the ascendant. Further there
willbe an auspicious eventin the form of child birth
in the relatives’ circle.

2565 - 2569: Chandra Kriya, Chandra-
vasthas & Effects: After this, I now state of the
(Avasthas, Kriyas etc. based on the) Moon's longi-
tude at a given time by the knowledge of which
effects for the movable and immovable beings of
the 3 worlds can be obtained. Gain, absence of
gainis (i. €. loss), enjoying happiness, grief, life,
death, success, defeat, coming, going, rains suffi-
cient for the country and pleasures like royal state
et cetera - these and many other things ought to be
known of the Moon. I now tell ways of deciding the
Avasthas of the Moon. In that manner, the viliptikas
(1/3600th part of a degree which means of arc of a
second,) be found out ... deducted .... remainder ...
300, 15, 30 and 5 accompanied by 7000, 30, 182,
7000 and 8 ae
Notes: This portion attempts to instruct us on
the 12 Avasthas and 60 kinds of Kriyas of the
Moon. These and the 36 Chandra Velas are well-
discussed coberently in texts like Phala Deepika
and by Pandit late M. R. K. Bhat in his work,
Essentials of Horary Astrology. But in our present
text, in these slokas there are utter confusion,
copying errors and the like making the clues unin-
telligible.

41

The methods of finding out Chandra Kriyas
and Chandra Avasthas are put forth by me as under
so that my job is completed in a satisfactory
manner.

‘Chandra Kriya (Moon's actions): One Nak-
shatra is 13° 20' or 800' of arc. Divide 800 by 60
and find out in what part of such a portion is the
Moon placed. That will be Chandra Kriya.

Example: Say the Moons 2° 40' of Aswini,
160th minute of longitude in Aswini. 160 divi
by 60 yields the 3rd Chandra Kriya. The result
mentioned for the 3rd Kriya will apply to the,
Moon's particular position.

Also see slokas 2570 - 2578 infra for effects of
Chandra Kriyas. Our text gives another methd of
Chandra Kriya which are five per sign, and 60 in all
for 12 signs, which are found from sloka 2579
onward.

Chandra Avasthas (Moon's states): There
are 12 specific Avasthas for the Moon which
should be found out by dividing 800 by 12. In
which of the 12th part is the Moon located in that
particular Nakshatra should thus be known which
will tell of Chandra Avastha.

(Similarly for Chandra Vela, the divisor is 36
as there are 36 Chandra Velas, or the Moon's
periods.)

‘Thesecan be used in respect of birth horoscope,
query horoscopes, Muhurta etc.

2570 - 2578. (Now stated are effects for sixty
such Chandra Kriyas). (1) Loss of position, (2)
performing penance, (3) attachment to others’
women, (4) covetousness, (5) gambling, (6) riding
on an elephant, (7) occupying a throne, (8) royal
status, (9) destruction of foes, (10) lord of justice,
(11) virtuousness, (12) lack of life (state of being
dead), (13) bereft of upper portion (or being head-
less), (14) being with injured feet and arms, (15)
being in confinement, (16) loss, (17) kingly status,
(18) reader of Vedas, (19) in a state of deep sleep,
(20) having a good history, (21) having knowledge
of Smrithi (or orally transmitted scriptures), (22)
charitability, (23) birth in a good family, (24)
endowed with wealth, (25) birth in a famous fam-
ily, (26) teacher of concepts, (27) destroyer of
enemies, (28) a sickly person, (29) won over by an
enemy, (30) one who goes away from his birth
place, (31) aservant, (31) one who loses his wealth,
(32) employed in the king's assembly, (33) a good
minister or adviser, (34) owner (or usurper) of
others’ lands, (35) has a wife, (36) fear of el-
ephants, (37) cowardice in fights, (38) very timid
in disposition, (39) sportive, (40) giver of food,
(41) maintains sacred domestic ire ("agnipaalaka”
and "Agnirakshaka'", i. e. will perform fire rites
regularly), (42) troubled by hunger, (43) thinks
about food, (44) eats flesh, (45) injured by weap-
ons, (46) blessed with a marriage, (47) holdsa ball
in his hand, (48) a gambler, (49) a minister, (50) a
grieved person, (51) sleeping on a bed, (52) hon-
oured by foes, (53) endowed with friends, (54)
sickly, (55) endowed with wife, (56) eats sweet
food, (57) drinks milk, (58) performs meritorious
deeds, (59) enjoys health and (60) enjoys happi-
ness. The Nakshatra occupied by the Moon should
be divided into sixty parts and the good (and bad)
be known thus (through 60 Chandra Kriyas),

2579 - 2591. Chandra Kriyas (or Chandra
Gunas) Based on Signs: There is another kind of
Chandra Kriya (which are 5 per sign).

(a) Aries: (1) Loss of position, (2) performing
penance, (3) interest in others’ women, (4) courage
("Saurya" is the correct text as per another version)
and (5) riding on an elephant are the five Gunas of
the Moon in Aries.

(b) Taurus: (1) Occupation of a throne, (2)
king, (3) being amidst kings, (4) destroyer of
enemies and (5) an army-man are the five Gunas of
the Moon in Taurus.

(©) Gemini: (1) Loss of life, (2) has broken or
curved head, (3) has injured hands and legs, (4) one
in confinement and (5) destroyed body are the five
Gunas of the Moon in Gemini.

(@) Cancer: (1) Kingship, (2) Study of Vedas,
## Page 58

Chandra Kala Nadi 43

(3) bad name due to consuming liquor, (4) charita-
ble acts, (5) dutiful are the five Gunas of the Moon
in Cancer. é

(©) Leo: (1) Birth in a good family, (2) En-
dowed with wealth, (3) wide-spread fame, (4)
‘commentator and (5) destroyer of adversaries are
the five Gunas of the Moon in Leo.

(6) Virgo: (1) Troubledby fever, (2) destruction
of place, (3) won by others, (4) servant and fond-
ness for joining girls are the five Gunas of the Moon
in Virgo.

(g) Libra: (1) Member of royal assembly, (2) a
good minister, (3) lord of another others' lands, (4)
a married person and (5) one equal to a person
sitting on an elephant are the five Gunas for the
Moon in Libra.

(h) Scorpio: (1) Minister, (2) warrior, (3) fears
battles, (4) state of sleep and (5) maintains sacred
doemstic fire are the five Gunas of the Moon in
Scorpio.

(i Sagittarius: (1) Troubled by hunger (2) eater
of food, (3) eater of flesh, (4) pierced by weapons
and (5) lives in foreign lands are the five Gunas of,
the Moon in Sagittarius.

(@) Capricorn: (1) Plays ball with his wife, (2)
Holds the hands of his wife, (3) a gambler, (4)
wanders for pleasure and (5) a king are the five
Gunas of the Moon in Capricorn.

(&) Aquarius: (1) Cool in dispoition, (2) a
meritorious person, (3) endowed with friends, (4)
devoted to Discus-holder, i.e. Vishnu and (5) being
aking are the five Gunas of the Moon in Aquarius.

(D Pisces: (1) Eats sweet food, (2) drinks milk,
(3) performs meritorious deeds, (4) healthy and (5)
happy are the five Gunas for the Moon in Pisces.

‘These states for the Moon in different signs
should be understood thus.

‘Notes: The Moon's positions for every 6 de-
grees give five various positions as above.

SATE RTST ATTA

2592. Chandra Avasthas: The 12 Avasthas of
the Moon should be variously understood. These

are: (1) Pravasa (going abroad), (2) Nashta (loss),
(3) Mrita (dead), (4) Jaya (success), (5) Haasya
(ridiculous or comical), (6) Rathi (enjoying carnal
pleasures), (7) Kreeda (sportive) (8) Supta (state
of deep sleep or numbness), (9) Bhukti (cating),
(10) Jvara (fever), (11) Kampita (embling) and
(12) Susthira (very firm) are the 12 Avasthas for'
Moon. *

Notes: How these Avasthasare tobe calculated
is not clearly given. Since certain Avasthas are
restricted to Moon's exaltation in the following
verses, the confusion is confounded.

2599 - 2600, (a) The Moon will be in Jvara
Avastha in a malefic's sign along with Mars and
will cause fever and heat. If she is, in the process,
in aspect to abenefic, she will prove auspicious, i.e.
will not cause fever etc. If the Moon as above is,
aspected by another malefic, death of the person
will occur secretively.

sign of Mercury when in the company of Mars and
will cause harm and grief.

2606 - 2608. (a) The Moon in a dual sign in
aspect to Venus and Mars begets Susthira Avastha
whereby success is granted. Ina situation contrary
to this there will be no success.

(b) The Moonin Aries, Scorpio and Taurus will
beget Jaya Avastha which will give success (to the
querist).

Moon undergoes 108 Avasthas. The effects should
‘be understood with the help of Dina Charyas which

2609-2611. Query about Food: If the Moon
isin Pravasa Avastha, one gets luxurious food with
more salt but bereft of six tastes (Shadrasas). If the
Moon is in Nasthavastha, one will not be able to
digest the food served; he will be grieved and will
mentally decline; the food concerned will be dry
and bereft of vegetables etc. all of which should be
known through query horoscope. If the Moon is in
Mrita Avastha, one will get very inferior food, will

incur indigestion and diseases.

2612 - 2613. Add the longitudes of the lords of
the 3rd counted from the three places, viz. ascend-
ant, the Moon and Karaka (?) or the longitudes of
‘Navamsa dispositors of the said three places. When
a benefic transits in the resultant place, Yoga (or
prosperity) will occur.

Notes: The present two slokas are apparently
defective in composition.

2614. One should know of the beneficial places
for transits (viz. Janma, Sampat, Vipat Nakshatras
etc.) with the directions ofa teacher and apply the
effects of planetary transits in Janma Nakshatra
group, Karma Nakshatra group and Adhana Nak-
shatra group.

Notes: There is a popular method of seeing
planetary transits, which in particularis in vogue in
Andbra and Kerala regions. Janma, Sampath,
Vipath, Kshema, Pratyak, Sadhaka, Vadha (or
Naidhana), Maitra and Parama Mitra are the nine
Nakshatras counted from the birth star. These
repeat again twice thus completing 27 stars. How-
ever, the 10th Nakshatra from the one occupied by
the Moon is specially known as Karma Nakshatra
while the 19th one as Adhana Nakshatra. Com-
mencing from Janma Nakshatra, transits are alter-
natively badand good. This is justa briefidea and
the entire scheme cannot be reproduced here.

While on the subject, I must rectify a mistake
of mine committed in the translation of sloka 486,
Book 2, vide p. 131. Karmarksha means the 10th
star (and not the 10th house), Adhana means the
19th from birth star and not Adhana ascendant, and
‘Vainasa is 8th star and not 8th house. The reader
will kindly give forgive me for my lapse.

2615 - 2624. Special Rules for Transit: (a)
‘Add the longitudes of the 5th lords counted from
the Moon and Jupiter. When Saturn transits the
resultant place, birth of a child is to be declared
provided the 5th lord at that time is in aspect to a
benefic.

(b) Add the longitudes of the 4th lord, 11th lord
and ascendant lord. Saturn moving in the resultant
place will cause much happiness and acquisition of
wealth,

(©) Add the longitudes of the 2nd lord, 11th lord
and ascendant lord. Jupiter moving in the resultant
place with another benefic’s aspect will produce
gain of wealth and grains.

(@) Add the longitudes of Mars, 2nd lord and
Sth lord. The time of Satumn’s transit in the
resultant place will harm wealth, grains and quad-
rupeds. ;

_ (© Add the longitudes of lords of Ist, Sth and

7th. When Saturn’s transit occurs in the resultant
space, the native will have professional fulfilment.
Transit Saturn should then be in aspect to 10th lord.

(© Satumn’s movement, in aspect to Jupiter, in
## Page 61

46 Deva Keralam

the space resulting the addition of the longitudes of
the lords of the 2nd, 10th and 111th will give gain of
a woman (i.e. marriage). Or else one will enjoy
pleasures.

(g) Add the longitudes of the lords of the 2nd,
11th and 10th. When Jupiter in transit occupies this
space, with the aspect of the Moon, the native will
have abundant financial gains.

(b) Add the longitudes of (the lords of) 4th, Ist,
2ndand 11th. Saturn's transit in the resultant space
will cause marriage, gain of lands or men-borne
conveyances.

(® Add the longitudes of the lords of the 2nd,
9th and 12th. Loss of wealth, and great grief will
result the transit of Saturn in the resultant space.
There should, however, be aspect of a benefic on
the said Saturn.

(j) Satum moving in the space caused by add-
ing the longitudes of the lords of ascendant, Sth and
12th will cause evils in respect of progeny.

2625 - 2627. Special Rules for Transit (con-
tinued): (a) Add the longitudes of lords of ascend-
ant, 3rd and 12th. The transit of Saturn in the
resultant space will cause harm to co-born.

(b) The same effect as at “a” will come to pass
through the transit of Saturn in the space resulting
the addition of lords of the 6th, 3rd and 12th.

(©) Add the longitudes of the rulers of 12th, 7th
and 2nd. Saturn’s transit in the resultant space will
bring about loss of wealth and great grief provided
he is aspected by a benefic.

(@) Add the longitudes of the lords of ascend-
ant, Sth and 12th. Declare evils to one’s progeny
out of Saturn’smovement in the resulting space. If
transit Rahu joins Saturn in the said context (i.e.
during the said transit), evil to wife be feared.

2629 - 2635. Special Rules for Transit (Con-
tinued): (a) Jupiter, the Moon or Mercury in transit
in the space denoted by addition of longitudes of
lords of Ist, 10th and 11th will cause pilgrimage.

(b) Add the longitudes of lords of the 3rd, 11th
and 12th. When Rahu movesin the resultant space
with the aspect of a planet that is inimical to him,
the person will incur loss of co-born and a great
disease.

(c) Add the longitudes of Mars, Satum and
Rahu as seen at birth. When Rabu transits the 8th
from the resultant space, great disease and great
distress will come to pass.

(@ Evils to progeny and incurring great grief
will be experienced with the transit of Mars in the
space denoted by the addition of the longitudes of
the lords of 12th, 5th and 11th.

(©) Add the longitudes of the lords of Ist, 2nd
and 11th counted from the ascendant, the Moon or
Jupiteras the case may be. Saturn journeying in the
resultant space or a trine thereof will bring finan-
## Page 62

Chandra Kala Nadi 47

cial gains. The Moon, Jupiter and (or) Venus
should then be aspected by a benefic.

(© Ketu transiting the place caused by adding
the longitudes of the two planets, viz. lords of 3rd.
and 8th, will cause professional obstacles apart
from much difficulties and change of place.

(g) Should Saturn (instead of Ketu) move in the
space as mentioned at “f” above, death will strike
the person.

respect of effects. “Rahu aspecting the lords of 3rd.
and 10th ......”

of the 6th, 8th and 12th. When Saturn moves in the
resultant space, death will occur to a day-born
person. For anight-bom, Saturn should be moving
in the 7th from the resultant space.

be the result when Mars moves in the space indi-
cated by adding the longitudes of the 4th lords from
Mars, the Moon or the ascendant as the case may
be.

(b) Troubles frem weapons will come to pass
when the Sun passes on the space denoted by the
addition of longitudes of the 6th lords counted from
the ascendant, the Moon and the Sun.

(c) Add the longitudes of the 2nd lords from
Jupiter, the Moon and the Sun. Astrologers declare

2652 - 2653. Sagittarius Ascendant -
Champaka Nadiamsa: The native of 2nd half of
‘Champaka Nadiamsa in Sagittarius ascendant will
bea Brahmin and be the fifth or the second issue of
his family. He will be blood-red in complexion, be
auspicious in appearance and be born in a remote
village or in a town in a river region.

Notes: For Champaka Nadiamsa, please see
notes under sloka 1412, p.128, Book 1.

rlus Decanate: The native of Sagittarius ascendant
with Gemini Navamsa (or) Sagittarius decanate
will lose his father early in his childhood if the 9th
lord (the Sun) is with Jupiter (the lord of the
ascendant), He will have no after-born but will
have older co-born and be liked by them. His
childhood days will be marked by poverty and
danger. He will live in hismaterna grand-mother's

“home. His mother will be somewhat happy.

2662 - 2663 (a) The maternal family of one
bom in the former half of the subject Nadiamsa will
progress. The subject will obtain younger co-born.

(b) One of 2nd half of this Nadiamsa will live
with his matemal kinsfolk during his childhood
experiencing difficulties. He will be happy through
a Soodra king.

2667 - 2669. He will receive education but be
bereft of Vedic studies. He will serve a king, be
irritable but instantly pleased, will attain fame
through royal service, will tactfully achieve his
aims, be of royal disposition, valorous, passionate
and wicked. He will argue in his own favour, be
skilful in achieving for others and interested in
others’ jobs. He will have beautiful eyes and sharp
mind, and be famous. His body will have windy
and bilious imbalances.

2671 -2672. Birth on a Malefic’s Week Day:
Should birth be on a malefic’s week day, the said
native will be valorous, will perform adventurous
deeds, be irritable but instantly pleased and will
have fortunes made by dint of his efforts. He will
steal royal wealth, will have large eyes, be charm-
ing in appearance, will have thick (or heavy) hair
and will honour Gods and Brahmins. He will be
devoted Lords Vishnu and Siva. These effects for
2nd half of Champaka Nadiamsa will bear truth.

Notes: Malefic weekdaysare Sunday, Tuesday
and Saturday. Monday and Wednesday will also
fall in this category ifthe? \on and Mercury are in
order malefic planets as jer standard rules for a
given horoscope. The ap’ orisms discussed apply
to Sagittarius ascendant  . particular.

2673. Sagittarius Ascendant - 2nd Half of
Champaka Nadiamsa: According to another au-
thority, the native will be fond of being munificent,
towards deserving people. He will be skilful in
undertakings, will receive a lot of honour and be
eligible to be worshipped (or honoured). His deeds
will be exceedingly rejoiceable. He will havea soft
and strong physique. With the progress of time, his,
prosperity and merits will be on the increase.

2674-2676. Champaka Nadiamsa - Ist Half:
One of Sagittarius ascendant rising in this segment
will be a great sinner. He will honour gods and
Brahmins, be ever irascible, inebriated and will
spend his wealth on unworthy causes. He will be
hypocritic, lustful, addicted to salacious pleasures,
wicked, crafty, predominantly ignorant, will in-
dulge in mean deeds, will serve mean men and be
il-disposed.

2677 - 2678. Champaka Nadiamsa - 2nd
Half: This native will be devoted to Lord Vishnu
and Lord Siva. He will have two wives, be passion-
ate and will incur separation from his older brother.
He will have one brother and be wealthy. He will

prove to be a best father and will have very good
sons.

2679. Debilitated Sun: The lord of the 9th (the
Sun) in debility denotes that his father will lead a
difficult life. A paternal grand-uncle will have
some prosperity and happiness.

2680 - 2683. (a) Debilitated Venus: Venus,
ruling the house of paternal uncles (i.e. the 11th),
in debility (in the sign Virgo) and with exaltation
Navamsa (Pisces) will cause much happiness to
father. The native will have one paternal uncle and
two paternal aunts, Out of the latter two, one will
be endowed with progeny. Effects relating to pater-
nal uncle have been told in the concerned chapter
(in the work consulted by the author). Therein
explained are the ways and means to ascertain the
number (of paternal uncles).

(b) The Sun in an Immovable Navamsa: The
lord of the 9th, the Sun, in a benefic’s sign with an
immovable Navamsa while Saturn is in the 12th
from the ascendant will destroy the first issue (of
the paternal uncle). The son born later will be long-
lived. He will have limited number of issues.

Notes: (b) These instructions apparently relate
to the paternal uncle of the native.

2684 - 2692. (a) Champaka Nadiamsa - 2nd
Half: During his second Dasa, the person will join
a Soodra king. In his childhood, he will live in
another country ruled by a hunter. There will be
some fear of (epidemic) fever in the city (con-
cerned). Later on he will return to his native
country and will incur fever for a period of one
month. His health will recover later on. His
marriage will take place in the sub period of the lord
of the ascendant (Jupiter) during the 2nd Dasa
‘There are divergent views that the marriage will be
in the sub period of Rabu, or in the 16th year or in
the 14th year.

(b) For one born in the Ist half of the subject
Nadiamsa, marriage will be in the 12th year.

(©) Champaka Nadiamsa - 2nd Half: There
will be obstacles in the 20th year due to blemishes
caused by Rahu. The native’s prosperity will
follow his marriage and he will acquire good
fortunes due to royal service. Effects stated in
Deva Keralam for the 2nd half will bear truth.
During the native’s early days in the course of his
2nd dasa, the ruler patronising him will incur
destruction. ‘There will be a death in the
circle. Perhaps the subject will acquire happiness
through the princes. Possibly, in the Pinakini
region, there will be change of ruler (?) The middle
of 2nd dasa will cause grief to the king (?) The
native’s older brother will enjoy greater happiness
during this phase but due to misconduct, he will
occasionally be troubled by diseases.

2693 - 2694. (a) In his 12th year or 10th year,
Brahmins will be greatly endangered in the city of

Yavanas situated between the rivers Krishna and
Godavari

(b) In the Dasa of 11th lord (Venus) during the
20th year, auspicious effects should be declared.
After 22nd year, the native will be happy in respect
of his spouse.

Notes: (a) “Yavanas in the region of Krishna
and Godavari in the southe=n India” emerging from
the current verse should be carefully noted. This
clue from Deva Keralam explodes the baseless
myth that the Yavanas referred to in the context of
famous astrological works were Greeks or aliens.
This goes to prove that Yavanas lived in Indiaiitself
and were not the Greeks.

Yavanas also clearly means people of a certain
caste legitimately generated by a Kshatriya and a
Vaisya.

2696 - 2699. (a) Progeny of First Wife: Sons
obtained through his first wife will not survive.
(Only) one son and two daughters will be long-
lived.

(b) Progeny of Second Wife: He will have a
total of five sons and daughters through second
wife, out of whom two sons and one daughter will
be long-lived.

(c) For one born in the 2nd half of the subject
Nadiamsa, six sons and daughters in all will be
long-lived. There is another authority to state that

Sagittarius ascendant, Champaka Nadiamsa first
half, Venus is a malefic.... danger to the king of
Pandya ... When Saturn transits Pisces or its trine
or the 7th, evil to Chola kingdom will occur.
Perhaps its ruler will be endangered. Saturn mov-

Dasa, Pandya kingdom will have a female ruler
and more auspicious effects will follow. Brahmins

2711 - 2712. Third Dasa: The said native’s
elder brother will be destroyed in the middle of the
3rd dasa. The native will incur royal wrath and
enmity with Brahmins, His (professional) position
will be lost. He will be distressed and will set off
to the southern part of the country. He will move
hither and thither in this dasa.

2713. In the dasa of the 9th lord (in debility), he
will lose his position and be grieved while his
friends will turn into foes. He will earn the mercy
of the ruler of another country. The dasa in
question will (thus) give auspicious and inauspi-
cious effects and difficulties.

Notes: If the 9th lord is debilitated, the person
## Page 68

will initially suffer debacles in the dasa of 9th lord.
Changes will occur in his life at a later part for
better. This instruction can in general be applied in
respect of fortunes for all ascendants.

2714-2716. (a) During the dasaof a debilitated
planet, the person will serve amean king. He will
live in forests. His happiness will be curtailed.
Later on he will befriend a different king and will
live in a different village.

(b) Effects stated for 4th dasa will bear truth.
The 3rd dasa will give both auspicious and inaus-
picious effects. End of 3rd dasa will cause difficul-
ties in work and evils concerning progeny. Chil-
dren being then born will be destroyed while the
native’s son (depending on his age) will receive
some happiness.

Notes: (a) The dasa of a debilitated planet will
cause association withmean and questionablemen.
One will have change of place to his dislike and will
Sener much of his happiness.

2717 - 2718. Fourth Dasa: The dasa of which
is the 4th in sequence will cause association with a
new king and will beget honours and wealth. The
native will experience auspicious effects in an
abundant measure. He will earn a more prosperous
employmentand will befriend Brahmins (or learned
men). During this dasa, a ruler will die who would
be a childhood friend of the subject native. The
king served by him will be in grief and the king’s
son will be a source of trouble.

Notes: Some of the events relating to king
should be wisely replaced. The relevant portion

should be understood thus. The native in this dasa
will lose an important childhood friend following
which some relatives of that family will trouble
