3328 - 3329. Scorpio Ascendant - Durbhaga
Nadiamsa: Should Jupiter be in the 10th (Leo), the
person will have three brothers and be highly
worshipable. He will have an impressive face, be
libidinous and be liked by women for his appear-
ance. He will have a soft body, will speak within
a limit. His birth will be on a Monday.

Notes: The first line of sloka 3328 has some
deficiency. Its exact meaning is: “Birth in a good
time, on a Monday, in Scorpio ascendant”. The
concluding part of sloka 3329 reads in another text
as “Somavarasya Jatake”. All these refer to one
born in Scorpio ascendant, on a Monday, with the
subject Nadiamsa and having Jupiter in the 10th.

From sloka 3328 to sloka 3491, the Nadiamsa
mostly dealt with is Durbhaga in the ascendant
Scorpio with intervening slokas concerning Capri-
com ascendant.

Durbhaga Nadiamsa has two other names, viz.
Sura and Yamala. For Durbhaga Nadiamsa, please
see notes under sloka 8, page 2, Book 1.

3330 - 3331. (a) Saturn in the ascendant for a
person born on Monday will give an irascible
temper. He will be fond of others’ wives, be
ungrateful, cruel in speech, wealthy and lazy. He
will have a windy body and will perform valorous
and adventurous acts.

(b) If the lord of the week day, (i.e. the Moon)
occupies Libra, one will be addicted to women and
intoxicants.

3338 - 334, (a) The Moon, ruler of the 9th in
Aries with Libra Navamsa denotes that the native’s
father will be grieved on account of loss ofa brother
and thus will have no male co-born. He will have
asymbol of wheel on ole. He will follow the codes
prescribed for Vaishnavas and then will take to
Vedic path.

(b) Should the Moon join the 8th lord (Mer-
cury), the father will have an emaciated body and
be kind, truthful and proud. The subject will not,
enjoy happiness and fortunes through his mother.
Nor will he be happy on accountof mother. He will
be breast-fed by another woman. He will acquire
wealth through his own sweat and will become
famous through royal service. Due to the blemish
of the Moon’s conjunction, he will earn the wrath
of Vaishnavites and consequent infamy in royal
circles and be troubled very much.

friends and will acquire palanquins (conveyances).

3347 - 3350. (a) One’s 3rd year of age usher the
fortunes of his father, if the Sun in the Arudha Pada
of the ascendant and in Aries Navamsa apart from

a good deal of prosperity in the 2nd and Sth years.
Inbetween these years, he will acquire an excellent
conveyance. This applies to Scorpio ascendant.

(b) If the 4th lord is retrograde, the native's
father will be long-lived and will be quite happy
with his sons.

(©) Should the lord of the Ist dasa be in his
exaltation Navamsa, prosperity will occur (in the
first dasa). Particularly in the last one third part,
much fortunes will come to exist. (These should
apply to the native’s father and not to the native
himself.)

3351 - 3355. Capricorn Ascendant - Sura
Nadiamsa: One born in the Ist half of Sura Nadi-
amsa in Scorpio ascendant, will undergo difficu!-
ties due to the Navamsa of an exalted planet. His
father will be the husband of a barren woman. The
native will not have any full co-born, but half-
brothers and half-sisters. He will have expendi-
ture, be irascible, passionate and will due to the
curse of previous birth incur obstacles. He will
have limited fortunes, and will enjoy much happi-
ness due to his father. He will be wicked, short-
tempered, will worship Lord Siva, be happy through
his adopted son and will acquire wealth in his old
age.

Notes: Although “Keeta Lagna” or Scorpio
ascendant appears in the Ist line of sloka 3351, the
translation ismadeas Capricorn ascendant because
the entire first line is replaced by “Suramse
Purvabhage tu MakareLagna Jatake” clearly refer-
ring to Ist half of Sura Nadiamsa, Capricorn as-
cendant; Thus all of a sudden, here is a shift from
Scorpio to Capricorn ascendant.
## Page 119

104 Deva Keralam

The 2nd line of sloka 3351 is not also quite
clear. “Navamsa of exalted planet” does not con-
vey anything sensible.

Nor the reference to father being the husband
of a barren woman. Apparently, the text refers to
“Kaka Vandhya’, i.e. the mother becoming barren
following the birth of the native who will have no
full co-born, and then the father will remarry. Step-
brothers and step-sisters are referred to in the next
verse.

In verse 3353, “Ketu Prakritiman”
which means “disposition akin to Ketu’s”, It
should be “Kopa Prakritiman” meaning “irasci-

3356 - 33647. Capricorn Ascendant: (a)
Should the Sun be in the Sth from Capricom
ascendant, i.e. in Taurus, in aspect to Saturn, the
native will incur difficulties and evils to his father
for two or three years from the age of five. In the
end of the Moon’s dasa, paternal grand-father’s
brother will be inimical. On account of this, his
parents will be distressed.

(b) Taurus, the Sth house, containing the Sun
while the 8th (Leo) has Saturn denotes that the

father of the native will be foolish and will live a
trouble-ridden life.

(©) The Sun aspected by the Sth lord and
obtaining Aries Navamsa shows that his father will
be laden with troubles but will live fora long time.

(@) Paternal Grand-father: Venus and Ketu
joining in Capricorn (ascendant) denotes that his
paternal grand-father would be famous, fond of
joining widows, would face fear from weapons and
have numerous brothers and sisters.

(© Paternal Grand-father: Should Venus be
inexaltation Navamsa, paternal grand-father would
nurture his co-born. He would have only one son
(who obviously should be the native's father), be
wealthy and would have sons through second wife.
In his old age, he would undergo much difficulties
and be troubled by debts. During the native’s 5th
year, the said relative will pass away. Or else, he
may pass away in the very first year of the native
while in ascetic order. Due to the merits of his
previous birth, he would be disposed to take to
ascetic order following a family calamity. Or he
may die in the native’s 2nd dasa.

3365 - 3370. Scorpio nt - Durbhaga
Nadiamsa: When the birth is in the Ist half of the
Nadiamsa in question and the 5th lord (Jupiter) is
found with Saturn, declare long life to the native's
paternal grand-father. Further, the said relative
would be happy through his co-bomn, He would lose
his spouse in the middle of his life, be aappy
through his son and be without wife fora long spell.

He would incur damage to his eye sight in his old
‘age and would take to ascetic order following a

would incur evils and be fond of union with an ill-

native will have twin paternal grand-fathers. The
‘said grand-father would be interested inbad women
and be highly libidinous. The native’s father will
join widowed women and be inimical to the na-
tive’s mother. He will suffer from blemishes in
respect of the family’s growth.

3371 - 3374. Elder Brother: If the 11th lord
(Mercury) is with a benefic, his elder brother will
be famous and will have 3 conveyances, He will be
happy after the age of 20 and will enjoy abundant
paternal wealth. The native’ s 3rd dasa will produce
much wealth for the said brother. He will acquire
palanquins (conveyances) and landed properties.
Many of his children will be destroyed. He will
have two wives, be libidinous, disposed feed the
poor and attached to his parents. He will be long-
lived.

Notes: “Yamalamsa” found in the 2nd line of
sloka 3374 means “Durbhagamsa” as instructed in
slokas 3405-6 infra, Yamala Nadiamsa also referes
to Sura Nadiamsa.

3386 -3387. (a) Capricom Navamsa rising in
any of the four signs, viz. Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces
and Capricom will cause birth of twins provided a
malefic too ascends in the said sign.

(b) The concluding part of Sagittarius Nav-
amsa or the initial part of Capricorn Navamsa
rising along with a malefic will also produce the
same effect.

Notes: (a) “Tattrikonage” meaning “trines
thereof” in the Ist line of sloka 3386 is an intrusion
because 3 signs (Scorpio - Cancer - Pisces) are
particularly cited which are themselves mutual
trines.

(b) “Paapa Bhaage” in sloka 3387 meaning a
“malefic degree” should read as “Sa pape” - with
a malefic - in line with 2nd line of the previous
sloka.

3388 - 3395. Scorpio Ascendant: (a) The
Moon in Aries or its trines and begetting Libra
Navamsa containing Mercury will cause one to be
among twins. This applies to an immovable as-
cendant. The native will be fortunate and happy.
(b) Should the Moon, being in the same Nav-
amsa as Mercury is, be in Aquarius or its trine, one
will be among twins and be troubled. (Here again,
the ascendant should be a fixed sign as in “a”.)
(c) The Moon in Durbhaga Nadiamsa and in a
trine from the 2nd will usher pleasures, fortunes
and happiness in case of an immovable ascendant
in case of twin births. The native will be radiant,
intelligent, libidinous and be well-referred to by
mean of learning (i.e. he himself will be quite
learned). He will be liked by the army chief and
minister, will enjoy palanquins (i.e. conveyances)
and will make his fortunes, along with his father, in
adistantland. He will visitshrinesin his childhood.
He will also be famous and honoured by people at
large, will be versed in many languages, will
journey in the regions of Konkana and Kerala and
will gain wealth. He will befriend a king of a
different forest and will enjoy some happiness in
his childhood days. He will possess a lot of lands
and wealth. His prosperity will commence in the
middle of his life. He will befriend army chief,
ministers and Brahmins (or learned men). He will
make wealth through an emperor and will acquire
lordship over a city. He will earn the mercy of the
king and minister.

3396 - 340%, (a) The Moon in Aries or in a
dual sign (i.e. Gemini, Sagittarius or Pisces) with
Libra Navamsa will give the following results. The
native will be wealthy, very famous, and will enjoy
pleasures, gold, precious stones and honour. He
will be liked by women, be libidinous and will be
sinful by joining women of honourable families.

(b) Venus with a malefic in the sign Gemini
will create fondness for sexual union. His wife will
have some blemish. His elder brothers will also
beget these effects.

(©) Venus in the 6th or the 8th in relation to
Jupiter by aspect or association will lead to the
following effects (apparently to a sister, as femi-
nine gender occurs). Her son will be emaciated in
childhood and she will incur widowhood.

(@) The Moon in Aries or its trines with Libra
Navamisa will cause happiness to the native’s fa-
ther. He will follow the codes suggested by the
Vedas, be libidinous, will perform Yagas (sacrifical
rites) and will visit shrines. During the last one
third part of the first dasa of the native, father will
eam fortunes and be happy through his older
brother.

sign and placed in an angle or a trine from the

which is an angle from the lord of the ascendant

3405 - 3411. (a) Synonyms: Yamala Nadi-
‘amsa and Durbhaga Nadiamsa are synonyms. Sura
Nadiamsa’s Ist half is also known as Yamala
Nadiamsa.

(b) Scorpio Ascendant: For one belonging to
Durbhaga Nadiamsa, the Moon in Aries and join-
ing Mercury in Libra Navamsa will cause birth of
twin brothers. The native will enjoy pleasures. He
will make good prosperity through his brothers.

(c) Mars becoming the lord of Bhava Lagna
and associated with Saturn will cause small-pox. If
Jupiter aspects this yoga, he will recover his health;
the fear of ill-health will only be for two or three
weeks. (Without the aspect of Jupiter) the said
yoga will cause decaying (or disappearance) of the
native in childhood and he will somewhat have fear
of danger from quadrupeds and water. In the mid-
dle of his life, he will be imprisoned, will leave the
country and be grieved. He will undergo a lot of
difficulties in the region of Kerala and will be
displaced due to a revolution. He will wander in
## Page 123

108 Deva Keralam

many a country. After these, he will return to his
country and be happy. He will have expenditure,
beyond his income, in auspicious functions.

3412 - 3417¥4. (a) Irrelevant: Rahu in a trine
from the Bhava Lagna will cause distress to the
king(?) in the native’s first dasa.

(b) Rahu’s Transit: When Rahu arrives in
transit in the sign ascending (i.e. Scorpio), the
subject will be troubled till Rahu’ s transit in the end
of the ascendant. There will be a revolution in the
country. The army chief and minister of the country
will be changed. The native’s first dasa will
interrupt his progress.

(©) Inthe native’ s first dasa, his father’s friends
and relatives will prosper.

(@) Saturn’s Transit: Should the planet Saturn
bemoving in Virgo, the native’ s father will acquire
great fortunes, destruction of adversaries and ac-
quisition of happiness. Further, he will gain a
Prosperous employment and be happy.

(© Jupiter’s Transit: When Jupiter reaches
Cancer or its trine, his father will be happy. The
native’s education will commence and at the same
time his father’s fortunes will pick up. Jupiter
journeying in Virgo will cause Sacred Thread
Ceremony to the native followed by marriage.

3418 - 3419. (a) Jupiter reaching the end of
Cancer in transit will cause tonsure ceremony and
sacred thread ceremony. This applies to the native
of Sura Nadiamsa of Capricorn ascendant as well
as Durbhaga Nadiamsa in Scorpio ascendant.

(b) Should the Navamsa be Libra, Rahu’s sub-
sub period in the sub period of Jupiter in the dasa
of Venus, and Saturn’s sub period (in the dasa of
Venus) will cause auspicious effects.

Notes: (b) “Tulamsake Prajatascha” in sloka
3419 should, in my opinion, read as “Suramsake
Prajatascha” denoting the native of Sura Nadi-
amsa, in Capricorn ascendant, for whom both the
sub periods of Jupiter and Saturn in the dasa of
‘Venus will prove auspicious.

3420 - 3425. (a) One born in Scorpio ascendant
(Capricom is referred to in another version) will, in
the first dasa, with the Sun’s southerly course, go
in adoption into a family belonging to his own
Gotra. This will be in the sub period of Jupiter (in
the dasa of Venus).

() Jupiter joining or in aspect to Mars will
show some possibility of danger through ulcers. In
childhood, he will be thin-bodied. The 2nd year of
his age will cause troubles and there will be great
danger in the family of his maternal grand-mother.
The native’ s kinsfolk will incur evils, great danger
and mental distress.

(©) In the dasa of Venus and in the sub period
of Saturn, the maternal grand-father of Scorpio
native will enjoy happiness but will incur physical
illness. The person’s mother will be distressed in
## Page 124

Chandra Kala Nadi 109

the sub period of the 4th lord (Saturn). The second
half of Saturn’ s sub period in the dasa of Venus will
cause happiness to the native’s father followed by
a family marriage.

Notes: (b) “Vandhya” meaning “barren” is
found in the 2nd line of sloka 3422 dealing with
maternal grand-mother. This is a} truthless refer-
ence. This term also occurs in the next line.
Further, the word “Mata” (mother) in the first line
of sloka 3423 is replaced by “Mahadvipat” mean-
ing “great danger” in another text.

3427 - 3428. The sub period of Mercury in
Venus dasa will cause fear, with the Sun’s adverse
transit, of fever for a Scorpio native. The last one
third part of Saturn’ sub period will cause birth of
a younger brother, marriage to older brother and
happiness to father.

3429. This is not a logically constructed sloka.
Neither the planets involved are clear. “Tara” =
Nakshatra; “Arka” = Sun;

“Isa” = Lord; “Adhipa” again is lord. Thus the
term “Tara + Arka + Isa + Adhipa” yields nothing
sensible. These planets, if at all they mean some-
thing clear, should be in own sign or debilitation,
giving a Raja Yoga and great happiness in the 4th

dasa.

3430 - 3432. Scorpio Ascendant - Durbhaga
Nadiamsa: (a) In the sub period of the 11th lord
(Mercury) and in the dasa of the 7th lord (Venus),
one’s education will commence followed by sa-
cred thread ceremony.

(b) Capricorn Ascendant: For one born in
Capricorn ascendant’s Sura Nadiamsa, Ketu’s sub
period in the first dasa will cause sacred thread
ceremony.

(©) Forone born in Scorpio ascendant, vide “a”
above, the said ceremony in the 11th lord’s sub
period will occur in the region of a great river.

Notes: (b) “Vrschike Lagne” is replaced by
“Makare Lagne” in another version, which is only
correct.

3436 - 3441. Scorpio Ascendant: (a) The sub
periods of Mercury and Mars in all dasas will cause
physical illness due to troublesome undertakings.

() One bor in the first half of Durbhaga
Nadiamsa will be distressed in the sub period of
Mercury. So also to some extent in that of Jupiter.
(This applies to 2nd dasa.)

(©) The later half of the 3rd dasa will bring
wealth and acquisition of a son for the native’s
older brother. His father will also enjoy happiness.

(d) Saturn’s Transit: Saturn’s sojourn in the
end of Scorpio, or in Sagittarius will cause evils to
father. This may also be in a malefic’s sub period
in the 2nd dasa. During the natives 3rd dasa, with
saturn moving in Pisces, end of Aries or in Taurus
will endanger his father.

with Libra Navamsa will specially increase one’s

3456 - 3461. Saturn and Mars joining in the
10th (in Leo) with the aspect of Jupiter will cause
what is called “Buddhi Yoga” as a result of which,
the native will obtain great prosperity in the dasa of
the 9th lord through the hétp of his brother. The
dasa of-the 10th lord (Sun) will cause much
happiness to father. The last one third part of Ist
dasa will cause wealth to father. Throughout his

life, the native will enjoy fortunes. In the said
Buddhi Yoga, Jupiter being in the 4th, will cause
increase of quadrupeds and corns, and will grant
palanquins (conveyances) and omaments, apart
from fortunes through brother. The 5th dasa will
cause a family division and some enmity with
brother. In the native’s 32nd year, his wife will die
and he will incur plenty of grief, His 2nd marriage
will follow, following which he will see great
prosperity.

Notes: The yoga is referred to as “Buddhi
Yoga”. Buddhi meansintelligence and hasdirectly
nothing to do with Bhagya or prosperity. It should
read as Bhagya or Vriddhi Yoga in line with the
combination suggested although the title of the
yoga is not that important. Apart from giving
prosperity, the conjunction of Mars - Saturn in Leo
in aspect to Jupiter has other events as could be
seen.

3462 - 3464. One born in Scorpio ascendant,
Durbhaga Nadiamsa, will incur great danger to his
king if the 4th lord Saturn at birth is in Leo and
reaches in transit Pisces or its trine. Due to the said
troubles to the king, the native will himself be in
great troubles. The king will be destroyed when
Saturn reaches the initial part of Virgo, or a trine
thereof.

Notes: The important clue here is that one will
have great professional troubles with Saturn mov-
ing in the sign Pisces or a trine, if at birth he
‘occupies Leo, the 10th.

= 3473, (a) During the beginning and
concluding parts of the native’s 14th year, his elder
brother will gather wealth. In the dasa of the 9th
lord and in the sub period of the 8th lord, relatives
will be harmed and a son of his sister will die.

(b) Jupiter’s Transit: In the 2nd round of
transit, Jupiter arriving in Taurus or Aries will
cause much happiness. His father will be continu-
ously happy, will please the king and will experi-
ence auspicious effects. In the first round, Jupiter
in the initial part of Taurus will cause advent of
wealth. In second round, in the same space, Jupiter
will promote brother's growth.

(© Father: Jupiter at birth in Aries or as trine
thereof as the Sun begets his exaltation Navamsa
will cause illness to father, and great danger to
paternal kinsfolk. Relatives will be in the grip of
troubles. But happiness will prevail in friends’
circle. When Jupiter arrives in the beginning of
Taurus, his friends’ progeny will be fortunate,
famous and prosperous. When in 3rd round Jupiter
moves in the end of Gemini or Cancer, he will incur
enmity with the king, mental distress and loss of
position and wealth. In the end of Sagittarius or in
Capricom, Jupiter will cause obstacles and his son
will sills loss of spouse leading to much grief...

3483 - 3486. (a) Jupiter dasa, Saturn's sub
period will be the cause of death of one’s older
brother and the native of Scorpio ascendant will be
grieved much. His son will get great prosperity in
this dasa. End of the Sth dasa (different from 5th
lord's dasa) will cause distress and will destroy
step-brothers. In between the dasa fear of untimely
death (for step-brother) will prevail for which
remedies be done.

(b) The native’s brother will acquire wealth in
the beginning and end of the 9th lord’s dasa.
Prosperity and wealth of the family will increase.

constructed and is notamenable toa proper render-

3487 - 3491. (a) Death: The native of Scorpio
ascendant, Durbhaga Nadiamsa will die in the 2nd
lord’s dasa and in the beginning and ending of sub
period of Ketu or in the sub period of Venus. Death
occurring in Venus sub period in the dasaof Jupiter
will be in the 58th year, with the transit Sun in Libra
orits trine, in a dark fortnight, ona Friday morning.

(b) One born in the first half of Durbhaga
Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant, with Venus in the

8th along with a friendly planet, will die a bad
death. One will beget bad end if Ketu is in the 8th
or the 12th.

(c) Whatever has been stated in Deva Keralam
about Durbhaga Nadiamsa will be truthful.

3492-3494, Immovable Ascendant-Nisakara
Nadiamsa: With the Sun in an immovable sign,
one born with this Nadiamsa in any of the four
immovable signs will be a Soodra. While the
Soodra Kala of the subject Nadiamsa (i.e. its last
one fourth part) rises, if Venus becomes the lord of
the day time and gets the aspect of Saturn, one bom
will be a Soodra and will enjoy Raja yoga effects.
The Sun in aspect to Jupiter will cause birth of a
Brahmin in the Vipra Kala (Ist one fourth part) of
the said Nadiamsa. Kshatriyas and others will be
bom in the respective parts, ic. a Kshatriya in
Kshatriya Kala, a Vaisya in Vaisya Kala and a
Soodra in Soodra Kala, of the Nadiamsa.

Notes: Nisakra (known as Nisakari as well as
Nisachari in some versions) extends thus: from 19°
36'to 19° 48' of amovable sign, from 10° 12'to 10°
24' of an immovable sign and from 4° 36' to 4° 38°
of a dual sign.

bom if the Sun joins Mercury and Venus in aspect
to or in association with Satu. He will live by
trading activities and be emaciated in childhood
due to troubles. So also will his father be. The
native will make his fortunes in the middle of his

3498 - 3503, Immovable Ascendant: (a) Sat-
um becoming the lord of the Sun sign and associ-
ated with the Moon will destroy one’s brothers.
The native will have many sisters. According to
some, there will be no co-born. During his child-
hood, he will be dull-headed. He will be gentle,
libidinousand (later on will become) intelligent. In
his youth, his fortunes will be plenty. Old age will
bring forth troubles.

(b) The Sun in Nisakara Nadiamsa, while his
dispositor joins Saturn, denotes the following. The
person will have limited education. He will be
crafty, libidinous and be instantly irascible. In the
middle of his age, he will become smart. He will be
fond of bitter juices, wise, munificent and passion-
ate. In his childhood he will be indolent and in
youth gentle. He will be ever interested in taking
to the path of justice and will follow the Vedic
codes of conduct. He will have faith in gods and
Brahmins. His fortunes will register increase after
his 20th-year.

Notes: (a) “Kalagni Nadiamsa in Pisces” is an
interruption and a wrong copy for some other text
while the Shashtiamsa with this name appears
rightly in sloka 3504.

ascendant, with Saturn placed in Kalagni
Shashtiamsa, will indulge in dirty and cruel acts.
His 2nd dasa will cause much prosperity, but the
first dasa will usher grief, He will lose wealth in a
calamity orin a fire accident and his fortunes will
be on the decline upto the end of his 16th year.
Afterwards, he will enjoy limited (or some) happi-

3506 - 3510. (a) The lord of the ascendant,
which is an immovable sign, begetting the com-
pany of the Moon and occupying a Navamsa of
Venus, will cause losses in the 2nd dasa including
paternal inheritances.

(b) One born in the subject Nadiamsa having
two benefics in Taurus in Indu Shashtiamsa will
enjoy much fortunes in his 3rd dasa. He will enjoy
Prosperity and happiness in the 4th dasa. His Sth
dasa will cause mental distress and will physically
weaken him due to penury. In the 6th dasa, he will
make much prosperity and happiness. In the begin-
ning and ending parts of the 4th dasa, he will be in
grief for about 8 years. 3rd dasa will give wealth
and 4th dasa (?) much happiness.

3511 - 3512. An immovable ascendant having
Scorpio Navamsa, while the Moon is in Pisces ora
## Page 130

Chandra Kala Nadi us

trine thereof, will give the above effects, and
happiness in the 6th dasa. In the beginning or
ending portions of Mercury's dasa, or in the sub
period of a benefic planet in the 3rd dasa, there will
be fear of fever and the native will nearly die.

Notes: “Above effects” means the ones de-
noted at “b” of slokas 3506-3510. To wit, two of
the following combinations will give identical
results.

(1) The Moon in Taurus & C.

(2) An immovable ascendant which has Scor-
pio Navamsa & C.

The second yoga has an additional effect of
giving happiness in the 6th dasa.

3516 - 3520. Scorpio Ascendant - Marriage:
(a) One born in Nisakara Nadiamsa, Scorpio as-
cendant (one of the immovable signs) having the

7th lord Venus in the company of two planets will
have two wives. During his 14th or 16th year, his
sister’s marriage will occur. After this, in the last
one third part of his 2nd dasa, his first marriage will
occur which will coincide with Jupiter's transit in
the sign identical with the Navamsa occupied by
the 7th lord (Venus) at birth.

(b) Marriage will occur within two years after
the 16th year if the Moon is in a Navamsa of Satur
while the sign Pisces is related to Saturn by aspect
or position.

(©) The natal birth star of his bride will corre-
spond to the Navamsa occupied by he 7th lord
(Venus) or a trine thereof.

Notes: (a) “Sobhanam” means a propitious
function. Here it is to be interpreted as marriage.

3521 -3525. (a) For anative of Scorpio ascend-
ant, the Moon and the Sun in order occupying
Scorpio and Cancer Navamsas, marriage will oc-
curin his 2nd dasa. The possibility is, transit Jupiter
will then be in Taurus (the 7th). Or else, he could
bein Gemini (the 8th). The corresponding months
will see the Sun in transit in any of the 3 signs
commencing from Aries.

(b) Immovable Ascendant - Nisakara Nadi-
amsa: If Rahu is in the 7th from the ascendant,
one’s father will be grieved due to some calamity.
The grief, which will be abundant, will be due to
loss of the native’s paternal grand-mother. The
native will be in much grief due to loss of father, in
the 2nd dasa, in the end of Ketu’s sub period or in
Venus’ sub period. Following this, there will be
penury in the family, death of paternal uncle’s (i.e.
father’s brother’s) son and distress to the said
## Page 131

116 Deva Keralam

uncle.

Notes: (b) The name of the Nadiamsa in the
first line of sloka 3522 should read as Nisakara
Nadiamsa.

3526. Aquarius Ascendant: Mars being the
3rd lord and be in association with Rahu by com-
pany or aspect will destroy the native’s younger
brothers.

Notes: This applies to Aquarius, as Marsis the
ruler of 3rd for an immovable ascendant.

3527-3529, Immovable Ascendant- Nisakara
Nadiamsa: Should the 3rd house denoting the son
of father’s brother be aspected or occupied by
Rabu, danger to the said son through poison, fire or
water be foretold. Further due to enmity with the
said uncle, the native’s tongue will be cut off. His
elder brother would die due to haemorthoids (or
small-pox), prior to the native’s birth. He will have
many sisters but not a maternal uncle.

Notes: Nisakara Nadiamsa is at times referred
to as “Chandramsa”. The Moon's natal Navamsa
position as well as Cancer Navamsa are also de-
noted by the term “Chandramsa”, Suitable care is
taken in the rendering.

3530 - 3533. For one bom in the subject

‘Nadiamsa, the Sun in Cancer Navamsa would not,
cause a brother but a sister to the native’s paternal
grand-father. The said sister’s daughter will be
long-lived. The native’s paternal grand-mother
will incur eye troubles in her old age. She will be
long-lived but emaciated on account of (prema-
ture) loss of grand-sons. One paternal uncle will
have a wicked son, will be happy through his
prosperity and be enthusiastic of travels. Due tohis
sins he will incur ulcers in his old age and his
fortunes will decline. These effects will be evident,
when the native will be in his 2nd dasa.

3534 - 3538. (a) The sub period of Mercury in
his own dasa, which is the 2nd dasa from birth, will
commence one’s education. During this time,
much grief to paternal uncle will come to pass.

(b) Jupiter in the ascendantas Rahuis in the 7th
will make one dirty and sinful.

(c) The Moonaspected by Saturn, or being with
him, will cause poverty during childhood. He will
be happy after the age of 20. His fortunes will
increase in the 3rd dasa and he will be happy
through his son. ("Eka Yogo Na Vidyate” in sloka
3536 is a corrupt ccopy.)

of untimely death. Suitable remedies be adopted.

3545 - 3548. (a) One will doubtless have
progeny if the sign Aquarius is occupied by Jupiter
and Ketu, as the two planets Mercury and Venus are
together (elsewhere). Further, the native’s mother
will die following his father’ send. The subject will
lose his spouse in his 36th year due tohaemorrhoids
or windy diseases. After this phase, his happiness
will be great and he will soon remarry.

(b) There is another authority to state that one
born in the subject Nadiamsa with Jupiter's in-
volvement as at “a” above will have two long-
living children through his first wife. And the rest
(ofmale issues) will be destroyed. One long-living
daughter is also denoted.

lent sons and business progress.
(b) The lord of amovable sign in an immovable
sign will cause wealth exceeding 2,000.
(©) His two sons and daughters will be long-

3556 - 3560. Scorpio Ascendant - Visva Na-
diamsa: One born in this part of the zodiac will be
fair in complexion and will have charming eyes.
He willbe righteous, exceedingly intelligent, even-
bodied and proud. He will have predominantly
bilious and windy physique. He will incur diseases
of the head. His diseases will be limited, i.e. not
very serious. By birth a Brahmin, he will be
leamed, discerning, will spéak within limits and be
devoted to his mother. He will lose his father in
childhood, be happy through his older brother. He
will go to a distant land, maintain celibacy and beg
for alms in order to undertake study of the Vedas.

He will be rich in Vedic studies, skilful in many
branches of Sastras, wicked in boyhood and be-
come famous in his youth.

3561 - 3564¥2. (a) The native will be troubled
by a Soodra king in his childhood and will marry
early. He will be Due to the blemish of lord of the
Navamsa ascendant, he will incur fear of small-
pox. If he survives this danger, he will be later
endangered through water.

(b) Saturn, whois the lord of the 3rd joining the
Sun and occupying the sign of his debility (Aries),
will deprive one of younger brothers. However he
will have younger sisters. If Saturn is strong, 4, 5
or 7 brothers will be possible depending on his
strength.

(c) Two of his older brothers will have sons and
will have two wives. One sister will have sons and
will have her husband living for some time. Later
she will be widowed. She will be endowed with a
g004 history.

Notes: "Jeevana” in sloka 3561 should read as
"Yauvana" denoting youth, and hence early mar-
riage is indicated.

3565 - 3567. (a) For one born in the subject

‘Nadiamsa, the Moon in aspect to the Sun will cause

danger to brother through water. His family mem-
bers will be destroyed through wells and tanks (i.e.

(b) Note if Lagnadhi Yoga exists in the horo-
scope and if so the number of (Sarva) Ashtaka
Varga Bindus obtained by the'sign ascending. In
the year denoted by that number, the subject will
enjoy all kinds of fortunes, wealth and convey-
ances. His prosperity will last throughout his life.

3568 - 3569. The 2nd lord (Jupiter) should be
in strength in the 7th (Scorpio), as the Moon is in
the 2nd (Sagittarius), Mercury isin the company of
the Sun in a sign of Mars (Aries) and Venus is in
Pisces (exaltation). Asaresultof this entire set-up,
‘one born in Scorpio ascendant will be endowed
with longevity, wealth, two wives, lordship over a
village and scholarship. He will further be libidi-
nous, endowed with enjoyment of pleasures. He
will be educated, will attract women, will have the
radiance of the sun, and long-lived. In his (read
“from his”) 20th year, he will be skilful in offering
worship to the lotus feetof Lord Siva. He will have
acquaintance with various Mantras and Agama
Sastras.. He will beget sons (or progeny) in his old
age, be illustrious, will have an array of vehicles
and be revered by the ruler.

3570. Lords of Evil Bhavas: The lord of the
6th in the 8th while the 8th lord isin the 6th, or these
two planets in mutual aspects, will act as under.
‘The person will be aknower of the past, presentand
future (i.e. an astrologer). He will possess two

charming wives andbe happy. He will visit foreign
lands, be mad (oralternatively extravagant), learned
and will have self-made wealth. He will be liberal
and long-lived.

3571. One born in the subject Nadiamsa will be
highly skilful and will have a “second birth” (ob-
tainable by a Brahmin) due to knowledge of the
Vedas (i.e. versed in the Vedas). He will bea great
scholar, a renouncer, and be endowed with beauty
and wealth. After some time he will be in the grip
of poverty andbe ina lot of difficulties. Again later
on, he will be blessed with all kinds of prosperity
and enjoyments. He will have wide fame.
Notes: “Aparigraha” also means one destitute
of a wife, ie. unmarried; here it denotes a person
who renounces everything including the utensils
allowed for ascetics. Two marriages are indicated
for the person vide sloka 3576 infra.
‘As far as one’s wealth is concerned, it is
evident, that there will be great swings.

3572 - 3877. Further, he will be a devotee of
Lord Vishnu and will honour gods and Brahmins.
Inthe middle of his life he will receive the blessings
of a saint and (thereby) will have students and
students’ students (i.e. numerous students). He
will be well-versed in Meemamsa (examination of
## Page 135

120 Deva Keralam

the Vedic texts), justice, philosophy and various
kinds of Commandments. He will be an author, be
wealthy, illustrious and skilful in financial pur-
suits. He will be an expert in Vedanta (essence of
the Vedas) and be honored by royal circles, will
make sacrificial offerings (or worship) as pre-
scribed by the Vedas and will eke outhis livelihood
through his learning. He will undertake charitable
activities like construction of temple, tanks and
gardens. He will have two wives, be an eloquent
speaker and will have many sons (issues) in his old
age. His eldest son will be destroyed. The native’s
(first) spouse will possibly be a barren woman. He
will beget progeny through second wife. He will
hhave sons and daughters. His mother will be
widowed and be long-lived.

3578 -3584¥4, After the death ofhis mother, his
fortunes will pick up. He will incur grief in the
phase occurring after father’s death. He will
receive happiness following his marriage. In his
4th and Sth dasas, he will perform Fire Sacrifices
including the one called “Vajapeyi” and will make
his fortunes in a different country. He will have a
second marriage and be honoured by a Brahmin
ruler. He will firmly establish (the canons of) his
religion with the help of anew language. During his
childhood, he will have a profession related to the
Puranas and will live in a city ruled by an emperor.

He will beget success in learning and arguments.
He will be happy after his 20th year of age.
‘Through a Brahmin ruler, he will gain lands and
will ever attain Siddhi through Agnishtoma sacri-
fice and others. During his first dasa, famine will
strike his country(?) and his older brother will be
destroyed. His mother will incur grief. In the sub
period ruled by Mercury in the first dasa, his health
will have setbacks. Saturn’s movement in Taurus
will cause strength to Mlechcha kings and kings
will mutually fight(?)

Notes: “Vajapeya” is one of the seven Soma
Sacrifices offered by kings or Brahmans to attain
the highest position, preceding the “Rajasuya” and
“Brihaspati Sava”: One who has performed
“Vajapeya” yagna is called “Vajapeyi”. Kings
attain strength required for a battle while Brahmins
are blessed with Supreme Knowledge with this
Yagna, so say Sastras.

the 2nddasa and being with a debilitated planet will
make one’s life difficult. He will migrate to
another village. In Jupiter’s sub period he will
receive happiness. Later, his happiness will be less
and poverty will strike. (All these will occur in the
2nd dasa, of the Sun.)

(b) His 3rd dasa will cause endless difficulties
and abundant grief. His home and lands will be
destroyed.

(©) Inthe beginning and ending of 2nd dasa, and
in the 3rd dasa, during his youth, he will berich with
Vedic studies and be happy obtaining alms by
begging,

to evils including a national calamity (?). The
native will incur enmity with the Chola king during

due to national adversities and due to Pandya king.
Afterwards he will recover and will return to his
native land, The Chola king will gain ornaments
through the lost kingdom(?) In the 3rd dasa, the
native will have a change of country. He will learn
Sastras and enter into family life. During his stu-
dent days, he will eat in others’ homes by turn on
weekly basis (as was the practice in remote past in
case of indigent students desirous of studying
Sastras),

3610 - 3616%. (a) Mars’ Dasa: The native of
‘Scorpio ascendant, VisvaNadiamsa, willbe greatly
happy inthe Pandyakingdom, during his ascendant
lord’sdasa. He will befriend people in royal circles
and will acquire grains, clothes, wealth and the
like.
(b) Mercury’s Dasa: (During the dasa of
Mercury) because of his 8th lordship, the sub
period of a benefic will cause mental distress while
that of a malefic will bring forth much happiness,
royal honour and cause acquisition of quadrupeds
and wealth for the family, anyway followed by
adverse effects viz. evils to brothers.

(©) Mars’ Dasa: In the dasa of the lord of the
ascendant, during the Sth, 7th or 8th year of age, the
native will be fond of performing deeds related to
Yagna (Vedic sacrifices). He will have enmity
with his co-born in this dasa and evils to family will
occur. He will acquire wealth by resorting to
journeys to the western part of the country. He will
live in a different village, be fond of worshipping
Lord Siva, will enjoy happiness serving needy
people with sweet food and will meet aking. He
will obtain fruits of his labour effortlessly and be
happy with wife (i.e. will beget married). His.
prosperity will commence from the age of 22 and
may exist for 8 years. In between this time, he will
perform auspicious deeds and eam a lot of merits.

Notes: (a) This belongs tothe dasaof ascendant
lord Mars.

(b) Reference to Mercury dasa is an interrup-
tion, but is an independent and useful clue.

(©) In sloka 3612, reference to Jupiter occurs
without a proper connection. Whether it isin place
of some other useful word remains unknown.

war and his son and daughter-in-law will be hit to

3620 - 3621. During the native’s 28th year, the
junction of the dasa will give ordinary effects,
physical illness to self and he will experience grief
due tohismen being troubled by diseases. Later on,
health will be recovered and he will enjoy good
food and will gain lands.

3622 - 3623. The first sub period of the 4th dasa
will cause happiness. Its concluding sub period
will go without granting fortunes. In between (the
dasa), one will be in debts. During his 30th year,
he will fall in debts due to a friendly king due to ill-
placed Rahu. Inthe end, he will be grieved and feel
distressed. Rahu’s sub period will cause good and

bad effects.

3624 - 3629, Irrelevant: The army-chief will
be destroyed in the Pandya kingdom causing grief
to the king and the kingdom. The Chola kingdom
will be seized by other kings. During the transit of
Saturn, the people of Pandya will be troubled.
Relatives will disappear and the Pandya king will
be endangered which will be due to his own fault.
With Satu moving in Aries, chief men in royal
circles will be troubled and wealth gathered will be
destroyed. Due to activities that will displease the
king, the native will face obstacles. Huge army will
arrive in the northem direction. The southern part
will be seized by others. All ofa sudden, kings will
fight among themselves. During the march to-
wards western direction, humiliation will occur.
The king’s army will be destroyed.

3630 - 3637. (a) The native will enjoy much
happiness with Saturn's stay in Taurus in transit.
## Page 139

124 Deva Keralam

He will gain wealth and conveyances.

(b) 5th Dasa: Should Rahu bein the ascendant,
‘and his Dasa be the Sth in order, one will earn the
mercy of the ruler and will migrate to another
kingdom, Due to aking ruling a forest region, he
will make wealth in the sub period of Rahu and will
gain knowledge, entertainment, robes andthe com-
pany ofthe learned men. In the sub period of Saturn
under the said Sth dasa, his mother will be endan-
gered with Saturn moving in the 8th (Gemini).
After the age of 32, he will be surrounded by his
family members, kinsfolk and students, and will
beget royal mercy. He will ear plenty of money.
For ten years he will be endowed with prosperity
and all kinds of wealth. Rahu dasa occurring in the
middle of life will grant beds, gems and other
articles. He will gain wonderful robes, omaments,
garlands, silken clothes, golden and vessels, be
with his relatives and will enjoy pleasures day and
night.

"3638 - 3644. (a) If the Sth dasa lord is in an
angle or in his own Navamsa, he will prove auspi-
cious granting conveyances, plenty of money, and
the blessings of goddess Lakshmi. He will enjoy
happiness everyday and auspicious events.

(b) If the said planet is in Kala Nadiamsa, one
will be endowed with prosperity (in the Sth dasa),
happiness for co-born and exceedingly auspicious
effects.

(c) The Sth dasa will specially give auspicious
effects after the age of 30.

(@ The beginning and ending portions of the
4th dasa will give prosperity, perhaps, for 8 years.
His co-born will particularly obtain progeny.

(€) The native will face obstacles in begetting
progeny as aresult of the position mentioned at “b”
above. Because of that he will be distressed.

(O) Due to the blemish caused by Shashtiamsa
position for the Sth dasa lord, one will incurenmity
with men equal to him, in the sub period ruled by
abenefic. An evil planet's sub period will cause
mental distress and enmity with people in royal
circles and with the king and the minister. His
undertakings will face obstacles.

3645 - 3651. Scorpio Ascendant: (a) Venus
being the lord of the Shodasamsa ascendant and
being in an angle or trine from the ascendant will
give the said results(?) in the Sth dasa, perhaps after
the 2st year. Due to friendship of a minister in the
kingdom of the Yavana region situated between the
rivers Krishna and Godavari, the person will gain
villages, lands and wealth. He will be honoured by
the Yavana king. His undertakings will be fruitful.
He will gather wonderful gems and golden orna-
ments.

(b) The Sth dasa will specially reveal good
effects in its middle part. In the 32nd year, one will
make a lotof money and obtain more good effects
## Page 140

Chandra Kala Nadi 125

in the middle part of the country and will reside
there. He will gain much wealth, will live in a
foreign country happily for ten years, will travel in
that country for 3 or 4 weeks and beget auspicious
effects. He will undertake long distance trips to the
southern part of the country.

Notes: (a) “Kalaamsa” is another name for
Shodasamsa, according to sage Parasara. But the
mention “above results” isnot clear as the relevant
portion is not present in our version.

3652 - 36552, (a) When Saturn journeys in the
8th from the ascendant, one will be troubled by
(Serious) fever while his undertakings will bring
forth enmity with friends and he will be inwardly
hurt.
(b) In the last one 3rd part of the Sth dasa, he
will indulge in good deeds. He will be extremely
happy for three years in the beginning and ending
parts of the middle of the said dasa. He will have
another marriage and will obtain the mercy of a
great ruler. Much wealth will be possessed by him
due to the blessings of a goddess. In the 12th year,
in the 5th dasa, he will ever have wealth and
auspicious effects. The ruler will be kind to him.
He will be happy due to Adhi Yoga.

Notes: (b) “Maha Nrupati Satkriyaam” should
read as “Maha Nrupati Satkripaam”,

3656 - 3664¥. Irrelevant: In the Sth dasa’s
middle part, when Satur arrives in the 9th, evilsin
Pandya kingdom will occur due to aking of a town
situated in the west. When Saturn moves in the end
of Cancer or in the beginning, people will be in
trouble. Important people in royal circle will be
grieved, and will be distressed due to internal
strife. There will be quarrels in the northern part
andalsoon the banks of the river Kaveri. The kings
of these regions will be happy and will recover lost,
kingdoms. The happiness of the ruler of the
western part thereof will be destroyed. The pros-
perity of the progeny of the army chief will also be
destroyed. After hearing this, the king of Pandya
will collapse. The kings of Chola and Pandya
regions will become mutual friends. Both of them
will die in the course of time, When Saturn reaches
Leo, the rulers of Avanti and Yavana will enjoy
increased prosperity. In the 2nd half of the Sth dasa
Satum moving in the three signs from the end of
Leo will cause prosperity of Mlechchas. These are
the effects of Saturn;s transit. There will be famine
in the world. Due to strife caused to Brahmins,
people of many regions of the world will be in
trouble. Godsand Brahmins will be destroyed, will
leave thé country and lose happiness.

3665 -3672¥2, Venus’ Sub-period: In the said
Sth dasa, the native will have a change of place. He
will be distressed after his 41st year. In the last one
third part of the Sth dasa, in 41st or 42nd year, in
‘Venus’ sub period, he will be endangered. He will
be in grief due to a ruler, a close friend of his, and
be troubled through a Mlechcha king. Everything
of his will be lost and he will havea change of place.
He will be extinct in a quarrel and will come back
to his own place. Due to the Navamsa blemish of
‘Venus, he will incur variouskinds of distresses and
will wander. He will be grieved on his way and be
endangered through thieves and weapons. All his
ornaments will incur destruction and his mundane
journey will be doubtful. Following these, he will
‘come back to his home town and will somewhat
undertake royal assignment. He will obtain food
and shelter and will beget some happiness. He will
befriend the ruler of the region and will acquire
learning and wealth. He will help others for two

ears.

3673-3676. With the Dvadasamsa position of

Venus he will gain happiness, gold, robes and
conveyances. His desires will be fulfilled due to a

Brahmin king. He will again proceed to the north
and will incur obstacles in journey with Saturn
moving in the 3 signs commencing Virgo,
Mlechchas will prosper. In the Sth dasa, in a
benefic’s sub period, the native will incur fever.
His relatives will be troubled and be subjected to
evils.

3677 - 3682. In the last part of the 5th dasa, he
will be mentally disturbed and be extinct. He will
proceed towards the south. After the age of 42, he
will prosper. He will enjoy much happiness in his
country due to the mercy of the king and the
minister, will obtain wealth, grains and omaments.
These will be to some extent in the Pandya king-
dom. He will move towards the south on a pilgrim-
age. There, the native will be honoured by a king
and be endowed with new robes etc. in a benefic’s
‘sub period. He will enjoy happiness through his
wife and be happy in the month of Jyeshta and
others (i.e. onwards). Afterwards he will retum to
his country. Due to the blemish of Shodasamsa(?)
he will have quarrels in respect of education and
learning.

end of Virgo or in Libra, the subject will migrate to

a forest region and be happy, during the last part of

mercy (patronage) of a ruler. He will gain landed
properties and reside in a river region.

by the Sth lord will cause destruction of progeny.

3689 - 3692. (a) The junction period of Sth dasa
(with the 6th dasa), the native will have enmity with
aking.

(b) Irrelevant: The ruler of Pandya kingdom
will face destruction. For a period of 16 years,
anarchy will spread on account of a female. Brah-
mins will grow in strength, so also the princes in a
later period. The queen will be immoral. The

kingdom will be destroyed due to lust for pleasures.
Her grand-son will later recover the kingdom.
These are the effects for Visva Nadiamsa. Rest
should be guessed with the help of other books.

3693 - 3696. (a) In the dasa of the 6th lord
(Mars) and in the sub period of Mars, happiness will
prevail.

(b) The dasa of the (lords) of the 9th and 11th
from the Moon sign will produce prosperity. His
older sister-in-law will be physically ill. His
progeny will incur prosperity, wealth, corn and
clothes. The native will be exceedingly happy
through the mercy of the ruler and the minister.

(©) His brother's son will be happy on account
of auspicious functions in a malefic’s sub period
during the dasa of Jupiter.

(@) Saturn moving in an evil house will cause
destruction of elder brother and the native will be
grieved on accountof this. Earlierhe will gain birth
of a son.

3697-3700. (a) One born in Scorpio ascendant,
Capricom Navamsa will, in the 5th dasa or in
## Page 143

128 Deva Keralam

Jupiter’s dasa, gain wealth and lands in a river
region, during the operation of a malefic’s sub
period. He will be honoured by a great king and
will befriend a brabmin ruler.

(b) He will specially gain of villages in the
region of ariver. In the middle of Jupiter’ s dasa, he
will gain plenty of wealth and grains and celebrate
auspicious functions relating to his son.

(c) His daughter will cause grief due to (bad)
Navamsa position of Jupiter. He will obtain
progeny later and will enjoy prosperity for five

years.

Notes: (a) “Visvamsaka yogena” reads as
“Shashtadhi Yoge” in another version, obliquely
pointing to Adhi Yoga.

3701 - 3704. (a) During the operation of the
dasa of the 2nd lord (Jupiter), transit Saturn reach-
ing the 6th (Aries) will destroy a king with whom
the native is acquainted. His son will enjoy reduced
happiness. He will be endangered through his
relatives and be in much grief. He will be happy in
another country during this transit.

(b) In the dasa of Jupiter, the subject will gain
knowledge of law. In the first half of the dasa,
happiness will prevail. He will be troubled by
enemies and misunderstandings with co-born.
Attempts to family division will be made, His
undertakings will grow in the middle portion of the
first half of Jupiter's dasa.

3705 - 3714¥s. (a) During Saturn’s transit in
Aquarius, the native will lose his position in royal
circles, be distressed and will incur enmity of
friends and relatives. Offenses will occur in royal
family and the native will be distressed in the 2nd
half of Jupiter’s dasa. There will be birth of son,
and marriage in the family circles.

(b) Saturn journeying in Pisces will produce
distress. Evils in the kingdom of Pandya and
destruction of the army chief will also take place.
Especially enemies in the Chola kingdom will face
destruction.

(c) The transit of Jupiter(in favourable places)
will cause happiness (with the above adverse tran-
sitofSaturn). The subject will incur wealth and he
will perform auspicious and charitable deeds. He
will gain lands from the king patronising him
earlier.

(@ In the 2nd half of Jupiter’s dasa, the subject
will incur grief on account of brother.

(©) In the first half of the said dasa, the person
will have bodily diseases including those of hands
and legs.

(© The last part of Jupiter's dasa will give
auspicious effects due to his (good) position in
Navamsa. He will journey towards the west and
meet aking. He will gain much wealth, robes and
landed properties. He will be honoured by a new
## Page 144

Chandra Kala Nadi 129

king. The friendly king will be destroyed. He will
have gain of ason and auspicious family functions.
The middle portion of the last one 3rd part of the
dasa will give specially good effects in terms of
wealth, grains, clothes and knowledge of law.

3715 - 3723¥%. (a) The above will happen with
the transit of Saturn in Aries and Taurus. With
adverse solar movements during the said transits of
Saturn, the person will incur fever. His wife and
progeny will also be subjected to evils.

(b) In Rahu’s period of Jupiter’s dasa, mental
distress will come to pass. His older brother will
lose his son.

(©) Irrelevant: In the said Rahu’s sub period,
the king’s progeny will be exceedingly happy, will
gain lordship over villages and acquire wealth due
toa Brahmin-minister. The Yavana king will die
and his grand-sons will become strong. With the
Sun in his northerly course, and Jupiter in Leo,
kings of Pandya and Chola will mutually fight.

Huge ariny will be destroyed in the northern partof
Narmada. When Jupiter joins Ketu in a trine to
Saturn, the country will reel under famine condi-
tions; evils will occur in the Pandya region and the
king and minister will be destroyed. In the said
country, its towns and villages, there will be strife,
and fear from inimical kings, with Rabu joining
Saturn in 2nd, 8th, Ist, 4th or 10th, Army will be
destroyed and enmity with army chief will occur.

3724, - 3733. (a) One will be mentally dis-
tressed and will incur evils to progeny during the
Sun’s southerly course, in the sub period of Mars,
and the dasa of 6th lord (Mars). He will also fall
sick and be troubled by strife. His gains will be
reduced with adverse Sun's transits.

(b) Saturn occupying the sign causing Lagnadhi
Yoga, in the company of the 10th lord, will cause
a Raja Yoga and abundant prosperity.

(©) Saturn dasa will give good results if Satu
is aspected by the lord of the ascendant (Mars).
With this, one bom in royal family will acquire a
## Page 145

130 Deva Keralam

kingdom. Others will beget auspicious effects.

(@ With the Sun's northerly course in the dasa
and sub period of Mars, the 6th lord, one will have
plenty of wealth, and grains and will be busy with
auspicious functions. He will obtain excellent
moral wisdom, will perform Agnishtoma sacrifice
and experience good effects for his son. There is
another authority to stress specially auspicious
effects for the native’s son. The subject will obtain
abundant wealth, and also much auspicious effects
through his kinsfolk from the western direction.
His son will gain wealth, conveyances, robes and
omaments.

(©) Although Saturn is a malefic for Scorpio
ascendant, he will give happiness (in his dasa). In
his sub period, wealth and auspicious functions
will result. Due to the mercy of the king, he will
gain quality robes and his desires will be fulfilled.

3734 - 3735. When the 8th lord Mercury joins
Saturn in Aries (both in transit) one will fall sick.
His illness will increase with the adverse Sun’s
transits, His kinsfolk will be in grief and his work-
force be distressed. The sub periods of Mercury
and the Sun will cause grief to kinsfolk.

3736. The lords of 3rd and 6th are evil. Great
happiness will prevail because of the conjunction
with Saturn’s degree (or Navamsa). Golden oma-
ments and robes will be obtained apart from prog-
eny and abundant prosperity.

3737 - 3743. Irrelevant: In Saturn's sub pe-
riod, the army chief of the Pandya kingdom will be
destroyed. Soodras will grow in strength. The
kings of Chola and Pandya will be destroyed and
army will be destroyed in the war. When Saturn
moves in Taurus and Gemini, the king of Setu will
be destroyed. Quarrels will occur in the northern
direction. Soodras will grow in strength. Ministers
will fight in the Pandya region. From a Bhava,
Satumn’s aspectto or position in the 8th thereof will
destroy that bhava, The kings of Dravida and
Andhra will turn mutually inimical. The ministers
of Kamataka and Konkana will have enmity.

3744 - 3747. Saturn as the lord of the 4th will
in his sub period cause physical sickness. Health
and wealth will accrue when the Sun is not in
adverse transits. But with the Sun’s bad transits,
mental distress and loss of relatives, of quadrupeds
and of grains will come to pass. His spouse and
progeny willbe subjected to evils. The king and his
men will be distressed. The native will undertake
royal deeds (“Rajakarya prakurvanti” is another

reading, which is right). He will gain wealth and

3748 - 3750. Saturn, who is the 4th lord, will
cause death in his sub period (in his own dasa) ifhe
becomes the lord of the Moon sign. The relevant
sub period can also be of another malefic. Danger
to fatherand toa friendly king will also follow. The
native will incur cardiac diseases, distress and loss
of lands, quadrupeds and grains. These evil results
are due to Saturn’s acquisition of Moon sign lord-
ship.

3751 - 3755. (a) Father: In case of birth in
Scorpio ascendant, Capricom Navamsa, with the
Moon in Poorvashadha Nakshatra, the native’s
father will attain fortunes in Saturn’ s sub period (of
Saturn dasa). He will perform Agnishtoma sacri-
fice. There will be a family marriage. The native
will be very happy. His father’s elder brother’ s son
will obtain progeny and increased wealth and
grains. Mercy of goddess lucre will prevail in the
native’s family.

(b) During the middle part of Saturn's sub
period, Jupiter transiting in Libra will give wealth.
One’s son will obtain prosperity and his father will
be happy. Good effects will emanate for the native
from western direction. He will make much wealth

through royal favours,

3756. During the dasa of the Sth lord (ie. in
Jupiter’s dasa), note the sub period of Saturn in
currency. Asa coincidence, if Saturn moves in the
7th (Taurus), he will be capable of giving (good)
effects in the form of huge wealth.

Notes: Some may like to interpret the term
“Swabhukti” as the sub period of the dasa lord,
Jupiter. But in my opinion it refers to Saturn’s sub
period.

3757 - 3759. If at birth Saturn is in his debili-
tation Navamsa, while his (Navamsa) dispositor
(i.e. Mars) isin an angle from the Moon, itamounts
to neecha Bhanga. As a result, Saturn’s dasa will
grant prosperity. The 2nd half of Saturn’s sub
period will give huge wealth, superior clothes,
omaments and fortunes through the mercy of a
greatking. These apply to birth in Visva Nadiamsa
of an immovable ascendant.

Notes: Here the author resorts to identifying a
cancellation of debility occurring in Navamsa.
This is an important clue and worth putting to
practical use in a general context.

The angular position of Mars (or the planet
concemed) is better noted only in the Rasi chart.

3760 - 3764. (a) Jupiter’s Sub-period: When
Jupiter is in his seventh round of transit, the Suns’
movements in the three signs commencing from
Virgo will be a period of fortunes. The native will
attain excellent moral merits. Auspicious effects
conceming his son will take place. Much wealth
will prevail in his family. These are during the
operation of Jupiter's sub period (in the dasa of
Jupiter).

(b) Saturn’s Sub-period: When the planet
Jupiter attains combustion, or with adverse solar
transits, the sub period in question will cause’a
change of place and some physical illness. in the
64th year, one will gain the mercy of the king and
minister. His son will perform final rites. In the
spring season, the native will perform auspicious
deeds and will serve a Mlechcha king. With
Jupiter’ s transitin Libra, he will enjoy fortunes and
ommaments,

3765 - 3772. (a) Rahu’s Transit: Rahu jour-

neying in Aquarius while the Sun isin transit in the
3 signs from Pisces will bring forth royal honours,
happiness, learning and association with men of
letters. Good and bad effects will come to pass in
the 65th year. Good effects will be through the
earlier known ruler in the western direction. He
will ever gain ornaments etc. His son will obtain
conveyances and will enjoy auspicious effects.

(b) Venus in Pisces: With Venus in transit in
Pisces (as Rahu is in Aquarius), the native will
continue to gain golden ornaments. His son will
beget an excellent conveyance, happiness through
spouse and auspicious events.

(©) Irrelevant: Should transit Saturn be in a
trine to transit Rahu in Aquarius, aking friendly to
the native will be in trouble, Men in royal circles
will be in grief. Ministers will be in grief. Soodras
will prosper.

(d) Saturn in Gemini: With Saturn’s transit in
Gemini (while Rabu’s transit is in Aquarius), the
following effects will occur for one born in Scorpio
ascendant. He will suffer from physical diseases
caused by imbalances of wind andbile. He will hate
food and incur family strife. His expenditure will
cross his income limits, He will be bodily emaci-
ated. His spouse and progeny will be subjected to
evils.

(©) Irrelevant: Grains in the country will per-
ish; people will be in grief. Good and bad effects
will come to pass.

3773 - 3774. Death: The native of former half
of Visva Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant, will face
fear of untimely death in the dasa and sub period of
Saturn. In his 25th year, he will be greatly an-
guished and will incur diseases of bilious excesses.
Due to the kindness of Lord Siva, he will recover
his health.

in Visva Nadiamsa, the conjunction of Saturn and
Mercury at birth will cause ascetic order following
a family calamity in the dasa of the 6th lord (Mars).
This will be in Mercury's sub period in the dasa of

3779 - 3780. There is another authority to state
that one born in the 2nd half of Visva Nadiamsa,
‘Scorpio ascendant, will live in a cave in the region
of Sambhu Hills. He will quit the world in his 65th
year with the Sun’s northerly sojourn, in the sign
Capricorn, in dark lunar half.

3781. The death of the native of 2nd half of
Visva Nadiamsa in an immovable ascendant will
occur on a Bhadra Thithi.

Notes: “Nidhanadhipa Samyukte” means “join-
ing the 8th lord”. It is not clear who should join
Mercury. Perhaps, the Sun in transit in Capricom
will be with Mercury. From this sloka, we have to
take only the Thithi and Mercury joining the Sun,
and then read with dark lunar half & C, vide the
above two slokas.

3782 - 3783. One bom in the Ist half of the
subject Nadiamsa will not take to ascetic order. In
the sub period of the 8th lord (Mercury) and in the
dasa of the 6th lord (Mars), his death will come to
pass, in the $2nd year or in the 55th year, or in the
57th year, in a bright lunar half.

3784. Should the lord of the 12th (Venus) be in
his exaltation sign, the native of Visva Nadiamsa
will attain Brahma Loka and freedom from trans-
migration for his soul. But this will not happen in
case of the 1st half of the said Nadiamsa.

3785- ee eones (a) Foranative
of Indava Nadiamsa, Libra ascendant, the last one
third part of Jupiter's dasa, which is the 3rd from
birth (ie. for one born in Rahu dasa), mental
anguish and loss of accumulated wealth will come
to pass. 5
(b) In the sub period of the Moon (in Jupiter's
dasa), he will incur illness, troubles of indigestion
and loss of appetite. He will be ever in distress in
respect of his undertakings and will incur the wrath
of the ruler. The king and chief men in his circles
## Page 149

134 Deva Keralam

will be harmed(?)

(©) The sub period of Mars will cause anguish
apart from destruction of wealth and friendship in
ministerial circles.

(@ The beginning and end parts of the Moon’s
sub period, and the sub period of Mars, in the 3rd
dasa (of Jupiter), one will have enmity with kins-
folk. His ruler will receive happiness(?) and the
native will face impediments in the undertakings
related to his village. He will spend his money on
account ofa Yavanaking, and will gain villages but
less happiness.

Notes: Slokas from 3785 to 3789 occur under
“Scorpio ascendant - Visva Nadiamsa” in the origi-
nal. But these belong to Libra ascendant, Indava
Nadiamsa, as the following ones.

Allofasudden discussion on Jupiter’s dasa, the
3rd from birth, suggests missing of a number of
introductory slokas, which introduction is other-
wise characteristic of Nadi literature.

3790 -3803. Libra Ascendant - Indava Nadi-
amsa: (a) His wealth will decline in the sub period
of Mars during the 3rd dasa (i.e. Jupiter's dasa) if
Mars is in debilitation.

(b) Irrelevant: The king’ s spouse will lose her
children in the process of their births.

(c) Loss of wealth for the native will occur in
the beginning and concluding parts of the sub
period of Mars, and also in the sub period of Rahu.
(That is Mars and Rahu sub periods will cause
financial loss in 3rd dasa.) In the months denoted
‘by adverse solar transits during the sub period of
Mats, one’s expenditure will exceed his income.
He Will be continually distressed due to the routines
of the village (i.e. professional tension).

(@ Irrelevant: Lack of rains will usher a
famine throughout the country. The sub periods of
the Moon and Mars are being described.

(e) In the sub period of Mars, the native will
incur enmity with the people in royal circles. His
quadrupeds will perish. He will gain villages ina
different kingdom and will incur benefits through
purchasing activities. His undertakings will be
destroyed due to Brahmins in royal circles. He will
visit temples, will goon pilgrimages along with his
family members and will attain Wisdom. His
money will be spent on charities and activities of
Vedic Sacrifices. The sub period of Mars will
(thus) prove auspicious. It will give wealth when
the Sun has favourable transits.

(f) In the sub period of the Moon, in Jupiter's
dasa, the villages headed by him earlier will be
destroyed (i.e. loss of authority). In the sub period
of Mars, he will gain altemative villages. The
subject will experience sadness as well as happi-
ness. Auspiciousness will come to pass except
## Page 150

Chandra Kala Nadi 135

when the Sun moves in adverse signs. He will see
his friends and relatives in anguish while his ex-
penditure will be beyond his resources of income.
Family strife and living in a different place will
take place.

(g) The sub period of Rabu will involve him in
a great danger and he will face much anguish in his
undertakings. His king will be grieved on account
of loss of ason(?) The native’s quadrupeds will be
destroyed and he will incur troubles through
ministers in his 33rd year. He will be honoured by
a Yavana king and will gain horses, elephants,
clothes and ornaments in the sub period of Mars.

Notes: (f) The term “Santikamsa” is wrong and
is in place of “Indava Nadiamsa”.

3804. In the dasa of the 6th lord (Jupiter) and in
the sub period of the 2nd lord (Mars), one will lose
his wealth in relation to village’ s undertakings. His
expenditure will exceed his income due to his
efforts to fulfil royal transactions.

Notes: In a nutshell, this verse suggests loss of
money and facing obstacles in work in the sub
period of Mars, in the dasa of Jupiter by a Libra
native.

3805 - 3806. Saturn coming in transit on the
Moon sign will cause various kinds of anguish in
the sub periods of Mars and Rahu. In the sub period
of the Moon in Jupiter dasa, commerce will de-
cline. Mars sub period will promote famine to the
grief of all people.

Notes: “Janma Sani” generally means transit
Saturn on the natal Moon sign. Transit of Satur
can also be applied to the ascendant and related
houses but with a different yardstick.

The present instruction can be applied in gen-
eral and not to Libra ascendant only. When Sat-
um’s evil transit occurs, bad sub periods in cur-
Fency will only promote the evils, and good sub

periods will fail to deliver good results in expected
measure.

3807 - 3810. When Saturn moves in the end of
Gemini, prices of commodities will go up. Healthy
conditions will prevail in the north. People of the
Chola region will be tormented. Loss of grains will
occur. Saturn's movements in the beginning and
ending of Gemini, and in Cancer, will promote
evils to Chola region’s populace and endanger its
king who will be destroyed due to quarrels with his
kinsfolk. InRahu’ssub period, the northern part of
the country will be involved in quarrels.

3813 - 3816. (a) In the 3rd dasa (of Jupiter) and
in Rahu’s sub period, one will be anguished for a
period of twenty months.

(b) For a native of Indava Nadiamsa, Rahu's
transit in the 9th will be auspicious as well as
## Page 151

136 Deva Keralam

inauspicious. Evilsinroyal circles, and destruction
of one’s own kinsfolk will surface during the 33rd
and 34th years.

(c) Rabu and Saturn joining in the 8th in transit
will produce distress in one’s mind. He will have
a change of place due to conditions of strife. He
will live in another village and will not enjoy
happiness from his spouse. These will be applica-
ble to the sub periods of the Moon as welll as Rahu
(in the dasa of Jupiter). Further, he will lose his
position.

Notes: (c) “Gramantara Vinasanam” should
read as “Gramantara Nivasam” meaning that one
will live in another village or place.

3817 - 3830. (a) Note if the Moon is in the sign
Gemini, with Scorpio Navamsa. In that case,
Rahu’s sub period in Jupiter's dasa, will cause
much anguish, particularly through the king.

(b) The sub period of Mars will lead to mental
sickness if Saturn moving on the Moon sign begets
8th Navamsa. The. subject will live in another
village. In Rahu's sub period, one’s kinsfolk will
confront obstacles.

(©) Jupiter Dasa - Rahu Bhukti: According to
another version, the sub period of Rahu will cause
physical diseases to the subject particularly after 20
months. Great anguish and destruction of wealth
will also occur. People will be subjected toevilson
account of heavy rains as well as drought(?) For
‘one year, the native will be mentally anguished and
will face much difficulties in his undertakings in
the sub period of Rabu.

(@) Should Mars be in debility at birth, his sub
period will lead to evils and anguish.

(©) Rabu’s sub period will cause anguish for
‘one year. Toremove this blemish, prayers to Rahu
are prescribed. By offering prayers to Rahu, Mars,
Jupiter and Saturn, happiness will be available.
Mrityunjaya Japa should be recited as ordered in
the Puranas (ancient literature). Remedies by way
of recitation of 1000 (rather 1008) names should be
resorted.to in a temple of Lord Siva for a period of
one year using sesamum seeds, Sami firewood and
Bilva leaves. Lord Siva should be worshipped with
the Mula Mantra. By doing so, all obstacles will be
removed and physical felicity will reappear.

(b) One born in the 2nd half of the subject
‘Nadiamsa will be endowed with auspicious effects
in the sub period of Rahu during the 3rd dasa with
the help of prayers. On a daily basis, three earthen
lamps using gingili oil should be lit in a Siva
temple. Mrityunjaya Japa should be recited and
offers with “Deeparadhana” (worshipping with
earthen lamps) should be made. These will elimi-
## Page 152

Chandra Kala Nadi 137

nate fear of untimely death, and destroy the entire

3831-3832. (a) Should Rabu be in Vargottama
Navamsa, his sub period in the 3rd dasa is said to
be auspicious. One’s desires will be fulfilled due to
royal and ministerial favours. (These apply to 2nd
half of the subject Nadiamsa.)

(b) However, in case of birth in the 1st half of
Indava Nadiamsa, Libra ascendant, (even with the
Vargottama position of Rahu), one will incur fear
from the king and loss of quadrupeds, grains and
money.

The effects stated for Rahu's sub period will be
a truth. These should be applied for Ist and 2nd
halves differently.

3833 - 3835, Irrelevant: Anarchy will spread
in the Pandya region during Rahu’s sub period.
Strife will prevail in the north. The head of the
region will be anguished. A lotof horses and army-
men will be destroyed. Jupiter and Rahu joining in
the 3rd or the 11th at birth will cause obstacles to
prosperity, difficulties to people and an invoive-
ment in a war for the king. In Rahu’s sub period,
the Pandya kingdom will be in the grip of anarchy.

Notes: From the above factors, one particular
result applies to natal horoscopy. And that isin the

form of obstacles to prosperity of a Libra native
having Rahu and Jupiter in the 3rd (Sagittarius) or
{lth (Leo). The following effects are in continuity
hereof.

also Brahmins(?) ‘The native will receive royal
honours and (other) auspicious effects in Rahu's

cles in respect of auspicious functions.

king of the Setu region will be endangered.

3840 - 3841. (a) When the dasa of Jupiter is
about to end, if he attains his debilitation Navamsa
or gets combust in transit, one will be enthusiastic
of involvements in quarrels. If Jupiter joins Rabu,
one will have fear from the king and thieves.

(b) Irrelevant: The king will be grieved. He
or the principal members in his circles will be
subjected to great diseases. For these evils, rem-
edies be done.

3842 - 385544, (a) Rahu placed in the 2nd will
cause expenditure of money in his sub period.

(b) If in the process his dispositor (Mars) is in
debility (in Cancer) one will be involved in debts
and disputes. He will live ina different village (i.e.
have a change of place). Mental distress will be
produced by adverse solar transits.

(c)Rahu in the 2nd in a Navamsa of Saturn will
cause fickle-mindedness. Due to his own inwardly
cunning disposition, the native will incur expendi-

help acquire elephants, horses, clothes, omaments
and grains. He will gain prosperity in undertakings
related to war in the western direction.

(® For a native of Indava Nadiamsa, the ad-
verse months represented by the Sun’s transits are
in Scorpio, Taurus, Aquarius and Cancer. There is
another view to specify Aries, Virgo, Libra and
Scorpio. These'are for the Ist half of subject’
Nadiamsa.

‘Notes: (c) “Chapalottama” is not a proper
conjunction of meaningful words. “Chapala” is
fickle-minded while “Uttama” is excellent. The
later expression is excluded in the English version.

(bh) The good effects mentioned hereof applies
to the native of 2nd half of Indava Nadiamsa.

3856 - 3864. (a) The concerned month (de-
noted by Sun’s transit) and the year (Jupiter’s
transit) should be avoided where there are no
Bindus in the Sun’s Ashtaka Varga. Evil months
will cause illness, loss of money, misunderstand-
ings in royal circles, debts and litigations. In-
creased auspiciousness will resultauspicious months
(ie. Sun’s transits) apart from financial gains and
much happiness. Further, one will beget the mercy
of the king and the minister.

(b) The sub period of Mercury in the 4th dasa
will cause continuing wealth and happiness. If the
Moon joins Mercury in Gemini, one will gain
wealth (in Mercury's sub period).

(©) Satum’s dasa, Mercury's sub period will
cause wealth through learning and assemblies. The
native will have increase of quadrupeds and grains.
He will beget children.

(@ Should the Moon be with Mercury in the 9th
(Gemini), the 4th dasa will cause increase of pros-
perity. Particularly the sub periods of Saturn and
Mercury will give wealth. Since Mercury is the
lord of the 12th, this blemish will cause evils to
father. Except in evil months (i.e. with adverse
solar movements), the native will get wealth. His
prosperity will be on the increase throughout his
life (because of the Moon - Mercury conjunction in
the 9th in Gemini).

3865 - 3870. Libra Ascendant - The Moon in
Durbhaga Nadiamsa: (a) One belonging to Libra
ascendant, while the Moon is in Durbhaga Nadi-
amsa as Jupiter is in a trine or an angle is to be
declared as having been born out of other’ loins.
‘The 4th lord should also be with a malefic to
complete the Yoga.

(b) The Moon in the Ist half of Durbhaga
Nadiamsa indicates that the native is an illegal off-
spring. He will enjoy pleasures, be devoid of co-
born, only child of the family and will indulge in
wicked deeds.

(c) The Moon in the 2nd half of the said
Nadiamsa causes increase of prosperity. The na-
tive’s parents will be long-lived, dear to the king,
be happy, eloquent and passionate.

(@ For Vichitra Nadiamsa and Durbhaga Na-
diamsa, identical effects are declared. Parasara’s
statements will bear truth as reflected in Deva
Keralam.

(@ Jupiter in Scorpio and Mercury in Virgo
with Virgo and Pisces Navamsas in order will make
one bom in the Ist quarter of the said Nadiamsa
wealthy right from birth. A professional trader,
lord of the earth and one of mixed caste are
respectively born in the 3rd, 2nd and 4th quarters
hereof.

3871 -3879. (a) Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadi-
amsa in 8th, 11th or a trine from the ascendant will
deprive one of after-born. He may perhaps have
step-coborn.

(b) Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadiamsa will make
one fair-complexioned and handsome in appear-
ance. He will be black-bodied(?) and will face
obstacles in education.

(©) Jupiter in Scorpio with Virgo Navamsa will
give an even body and pride in speech. He will be
discerning in respect of virtues and fame, be bold
and truthful. He will be a writer, be dreadful in
appearance, garrulous, intelligent and firm. He
will be interested in serving the king. His father
will be an intellectual.

(@ Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadiamsa, and in a
friend’s sign will give the following effects. The
person will be ever libidinous, will serve the king,
be wealthy, and famous. He will have a step-
mother and will be fond of his children.

(©) Rahu in the ascendant with the Sun and
Mars will cause a windy and bilious body. Atone
time, the native will incur enmity with his kinsfolk.

Deva Keralam

He will incura lotof grief on account of his mother.
During his childhood, he will be in distress, will
lose his father and will be happy through his step-
brother. Lateron, he will have enmity with his half-
brother and will destroy his wealth through Soodras.

(© Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadiamsa while
Rahu is in the ascendant denotes that the native’s
mother will indulge in sinful deeds and will have
such a conduct characteristic of people born in a
mixed caste. The native’s first wife will hate her
progeny. His father will befriend Soodras. The
family will earn a lot of money on account of
business activities.

Notes: No caption in the original is given as to
which ascendant the present nine verses relate.
These are for Libra ascendant. The ones from 3880
are for Aquarius ascendant.
