3880 - 38842. Aquarius Ascendant - Angada
Nadiamsa: (a) The native of former half of the
subject Nadiamsa will have a step-mother and an
elder brother.

(b) The 11th lord in Visvambhara Nadiamsa
denotes that the native will have a half-brother.
There will also be two long-living sisters.

(©) Rahu in the ascendant makes him a pleased
person.

(@) Elder Brother: The 11th lord in the 10th
(Scorpio) will give “Rabu’s nature” to his elder
brother. He will be sinful, libidinous, attached to
## Page 156

Chandra Kala Nadi 141

material pleasures, be fond of a conduct character-
istic of Soodras, devoid of truthfulness and will
spend money on undeserving counts. He will
neglect his father’s words and will value others’
words (i.e. heed to others’ advice). He will ever
join harlots and will hate his own spouse.

Notes: “Angada Nadiamsa” in this part of the
text should actually read as “Agada Nadiamsa”
comments on which could be found in the notes
under sloka 98, page 11, Book 1.

(@ The term “Rahu Prakritiman” meaning the
nature of Rahu is a very strange term. Ihave never
seen any text explaining one’s nature in terms of a
planet. This is apparently a misnomer.

3885 - 3889¥4. (a) The lord of Sth counted from
the 11th - indicating progeny of half-brother - being
in the Sth from the ascendant with Taurus Navamsa
denotes that the step-brother will obtain dead prog-
eny and will incur impediments in having children.

(b) The step-brother of one born in the Ist half
of the subject Nadiamsa will be childless. Even if
they be born, they will only die (soon) because of
the last birth’s sins of the said relative. To over-
come this sin, dips in the Setu and worship of Lord
Siva are to be done. Maintaining religious vows
and fasting on the Thithi Shashti (6th day of a lunar
fortnight) will cause birth of children. This particu-
lar Nadiamsa has been dealt with in another text
based on the school of sage Vyasa. By these
remedies, the step-brother will obtain happiness.
Heshould worship the idol of Gopala (Lord Krishna)
and give charities to the astrologer. Asa result he
will beget healthy and long-living children.

Notes: (a) The text considers the 11th house as,

the house of half-brother (or sister) as will be
evident from sloka 3890 infra. The 5th from the
11th, i.e, the 3rd from the ascendant deals with the
said person’s children. In this case the planet
concerned is Mars who should be in Gemini’s 8th
Navamsa, i.e. 23° 20' to 26° 40' of Gemini.
While on the subject, I would like to rectify a
mistake of mine. Ihave earlier wrongly understood
that the 5th is the house of step-brother’s children
instead of the 3rd house. Hence “Sth lord” stands
corrected as “3rd lord” in sloka 114, page 13, Book
1. Similar errors if noticed in this context may
kindly be corrected on these lines. I offer my
apology to my esteemed reader.

3890-38974. (a) The step-brother’s ascendant
will be the sign denoted by the Navamsa occupied
by the 11th lord, a trine thereof, or the one that is
aspected by the 11th lord, Similarly the 9th and its
lord denote the ascendant of the father. So also the
‘Navamsa occupied by the Sun ora trine therefrom.
(b) The native’s father will die in childhood if
the Sun is in Leo in Manjusvana Nadiamsa, in the
company of Ketu. He will be devoid of a co-bom
and will suffer from bodily ulcers and injuries. His
death will occur when the native will be in the end
of the ascendant lord’s dasa or in the 2nd dasa.
## Page 157

142 Deva Keralam

(©) The father will be subjected to evils if the
Sun and Jupiter are in order in Manjusvana.and
Visvambhara Nadiamsas. He will incur ulcers and
injuries and will have enmity with the progeny of
his first wife the consequence of which will be
family division. He will be greatly anguished due
to loss of the wealth gathered by him.

(@) Marsin the Sth from the ascendant while the
9th lord (Venas) is with a malefic denotes that the
native’s father will die in a distant land.

(©) The Sun in Manjusvana Nadiamsa will
cause father’s death in a remote village due to
indigestion and vomiting.

Notes: (a) Father’s natal ascendant could be a
trine from the 9th, or the one originating through
the 9th lord, as done above with the help of 11th
lord concerning step-brother. The instructions to
be used with the help of the Sun are clear.

(b)“Janmadhipa dasa antyaadau” actually reads
“Janma dasa antya adi”. This expression is only
correct. Thus it indicates that one will lose his
father in the junctions of Ist and 2nd dasa periods.

(©) For Manjusvana Nadiamsa, see notes under
slokas 121 - 122. page 14, Book 1.

3899 - 3901. (a) The father of the subject
Nadiamsa will die en route if the 9th lord (Venus)
and Mars are not close to the solar ascendant.

() A malefic Navamsa in the above context
will cause a bad end to father while a benefic’s

longed illness). At the time of father’s death, the
native’s mother will be in a distant land.

3905-3908!2. Maternal Relatives: The Moon
as the significator of maternal uncles (being the 6th
lord) placed in Kutila Nadiamsa with the aspect of
Mercury will give twomaternal uncles who will be
endowed with longevity. According to some, there
will be none. Further, the native’s mother will be of,
aquestionable history and will be emaciated due to
loss of a brother. She will live as a widow fora long
time and be endowed with a long span of life. She
will be happy with her middle son. These effects
are from Chandra Nadi. Sukra Nadi also describes
effects in the same way. In the chapter on effects,
Sukra Nadi states good and bad things extensively
about maternal relatives etc.

3909-3911. Half-sister: (a) Should the 11th
lord (Jupiter) be in Aquarius Navamsa along with
Saturn, one’s step-sister will have a long-living
husband.

(b) The Moon and the Sun in orderin Kutila and
Manjusvana Nadiamsas will cause widowhood to
one’s half-sister in her very girlhood (or early
days).

(©)Incase of one of AngadaNadiamsa, Aquarius
ascendant, Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadiamsa will
cause a limited number of issues to his half-sister.
She will be an auspicious (or fortunate) person.

3912 - 391414. Sister: The Moon in Kutila
Nadiamsa will cause somewhata sickly sister. She
will be abundantly grieved due to loss of two of her
sons (or due to twin sons as “Yamala Putrah” is
another reading). Her husband will suffer from
diseases and be devoid of co-born. He will lose his
fortunes in the middle of his life and incur utter
penury and be emaciated. The native’s step-
brother-in-law will be humiliated in royal circles.
He will enjoy conveyances, omaments, prosperity
and fame.

brother will emerge pure-hearted (after a sinful

3924 - 3930. (a) The Moon in Manjusvana
Nadiamsa will grant relief from obstacles. The
brother of the native will obtain happiness on
account of spouse, etc. Religious vows for the Sun
God and dips in the Setu will cause purity of heart
to half-brother (leading toa united family) and will
destroy all evils. Increase of progeny and happy
domestic life will also follow. In the absence of
these, there will prevail penury.

(b) The Sun and Ketu in the 3rd from the
ascendant, without a benefic’s aspect will cause
obstacles in obtaining progeny by the half-brother.
His prosperity will be destroyed due to a “Soodra
author”. After the destruction of the said author,
step-brother will receive auspicious effects. From
the age of 8 to 10, father’s Soodra friend will die.
Following this, step-brother will be greatly happy
and wealthy. Prior to this, the native’s step-sister’s
husband will obtain prosperity.

3931 - 3934, Venus & Saturn in Cancer:
Venus ruling the 9th and placed in the 6th along
with Satur will grant much learning and lordship
over many villages to the native. He will be skilful
if writing and learning, well-read in three lan-
guages, will enjoy effects of Raja Yoga and be
devoted to Lords Vishnu and Siva. He will be
somewhat happy upto the 7th year of age. His
Sacred Thread ceremony will take place through
his tep-brother. His marriage will be celebrated in
his 3rd dasa, perhaps in the very first sub period

which will coincide with the 12th or 14th year of his

-age.

3935 - 3936. (a) The Moon in Pisces: For a

native of Aquarius, the Moon in the 2nd in Kutila

~ Nadiamisa will give the following effects. He will

be crooked in thoughts, garrulous, will serve the
king and earn fame in royal service.

(b) Should Jupiter be in Visvambhara Nadi-
amsa, one will have some fear of infantile mortality
and be troubled by small pox (or haemorrhoids). If
he survives, fortunes will be availed of by him.

3937-3939, Sagittarius Navamsa: The Moon
inthe 2ndin Pisces with Sagittarius Navamsa while
Jupiter is posited in an angle or trine from the
ascendant will lead to possession of vehicles and
fortunes between the 20th and 25th years of age. He
will attain predominance to some extent after 16.
From the age of 19, he will beget intellectual
superiority. So also from 22nd and 25th years, and
in the junction of dasas. He will be endangered by
fear of untimely death at 32 for which remedies
should be sought:

3940 - 3942. (a) Should Jupiter be in
Visvambhara Nadiamsa, one’s mother will die
during the junction of (4thand) Sth dasa, in his 48th
year. This will be her timely death. She will have,
## Page 160

Chandra Kala Nadi 145

however, fear of untimely death during the native’s
24th year of age.

(b) Note the Navamsa position of natal Jupiter.
Transit Saturn in the sign represented by the said
Navamsa, or a trine thereof, will cause distress to
mother and anguish to kinsfolk. Even if Saturn
transits the sign that is in aspect to Jupiter at birth,
kinsfolk will be mentally distressed.

3943 - 3946, Irrelevant: The king will be
destroyed when Saturn transitsany of the following
four places - the sign represented by the Navamsa
occupied by the 4th lord, a trine thereof, the sign
occupied by the 4th lord and the sign aspected by
the4th lord. Inthe firstdasa, the king of the country
will be destroyed. His brother will come to promi-
nence for a period of ten or twelve years. Later on
happiness will prevail due to change of ruler. Lack
of rains and partial famine will come to pass in the
Ist dasa. In the 2nd dasa, famine and grief will
exist. In the Ist dasa, when Saturn comes to the
Moon sign, the kingdom will be under siege.

Notes: This kind of verses are obviously a
result of unintelligible copying of the manuscript.
These should not lend credence to the use of dasa
periods in mundane astrology.

3947 - 3950%, (a) When Saturn transits a trine
of the 8th lordat birth, wealth will be tosorhe extent
destroyed. Saturn's journey on Moon sign in the
end of 22nd year, (or) in the sub period of Mercury
or Ketu in the 3rd dasa, one’s spouse will be
subjected to evils.

(b) Jupiter in Visvambhara Nadiamsa will give
three wives. The first child will be destroyed and
the following ones will survive. 23rd year will give
a son. Prior to this, one will obtain only dead
children. Through subsequent wives fie. except-
ing the first wife), one will have increase of prog-
eny. Two or three of the sons will be long-lived.
The number of surviving children will correspond
to the number of Bindus in Jupiter's Ashtaka
Varga. Two daughters and two sons will be en-
dowed with longevity.

3951 - 3955. (a) Mars in the 5th while his
dispositor is in the 8th from the ascendant will
delay birth of children and will cause many a dead
issue. To overcome this blemish, vows to Lord
Kartikeya and dips in the Setu be undertaken.
Consequently, long-living sons (or children) will
be obtainable.

(b) During the 24th year of age in Venus dasa,
one’s step-brother will be destroyed andmuch grief
will occur.

(©) Relatives equal to the native will be trou-
bled by fear of untimely death when he will be in
the sub period of the Moon or Jupiter, in the dasa
of the planet ruling the 8th from the natal Moon.
Suitable remedy should be sought.

sub period in the dasa of the 8th lord, one will be
reduced to fear of fever. Adverse year (transit of
Jupiter) and adverse month (transit of the Sun)
should also be noted in this context. Necessary
remedial measures should be performed to pacify
the evil.

3956 - 3958. The lord of the 5th (Mercury)
moving in his sign of exaltation at the time of
commencement of the dasa of the 9th lord (Venus)
will produce unending fortunes. His acquisition
will exceed ten thousand (gold coins).

(b) The 4th dasa (independent of “a”) will give
fame (because of birth in Angada Nadiamsa,
Aquarius ascendant).

(©) The 3rd dasa will give a superior convey-
ance in the 25th year. After this age, his prosperity
will be great. He will obtain a superior employ-
ment. His fortunes will last his whole life.

Notes: (a) “Janmaath aayush paryantam” means
from birth to end of life. This term is conveyed as
“unending” in the translation as the dasa of the 9th
lord may not always be the birth dasa.

3959, The planets Jupiter and the Sun in order
positedin Visvambharaand Manjusvana Nadiamsas
will cause one’s death at the age of 56. The transit
Sun will then be in Virgo or else in Pisces. The day
of death will be Tuesday.

3960 - 3963. Sagittarius Ascendant - Varuna
Nadiamsa: (a) In case of birth in Varuna Nadi-
amsa, Sagittarius ascendant, Jupiter joining the
Moon in aspect to Mars will destroy after-born.

(b) There will be rains for three nights at the
time of birth of the subject with Jupiter in Varuna
Nadiamsa, in conjunction with the Moon.

(©) Jupiter begetting Pisces Navamsa while
Mars is in the last quarter of the subject Nadiamsa
will cause birth of a Soodra. The native concerned
will have a dark and bilious body. He will be fond
of sweetand sourjuices, be indolent and gentle. His
voice will be akin to that of an ass. He will be very
intelligent.

Notes: For Varuna Nadiamsa, please consult
notes given for sloka 188 - 189, page 20, Book 1.

3964 - 3966. (a) The Native: If the lord of the
ascendant, i.e. Jupiter, is in the subject Nadiamsa
in own sign and with an aspect of Mars, it will cause
fear of small-pox during one’s childhood. He will
nearly die. He will also incur disorders of digestion
and ulcer (or injury) and be extremely saddened
(ie. pained). His first dasa will endanger him with
fear of untimely death. After the 3rd year of age, he
will feel happy.

(b) Father: Further, as a result of the said
combination, his father’ sprosperity will register an
increase. Probably kinsfolk will contribute to the
family’s wealth. The native’s father will obtain
wide fame and honour in royal circle and also be
humiliated in the said circle.

3968 - 3969. (a) Should Jupiter be in the said
Nadiamsa with the 8th lord and in aspect to Mars,
‘one will be in great danger in the Sth year. How-
ever, note 5 Bindus in Jupiter's Ashtaka Varga.
Later on, his co-born will be subjected to evils due
to small-pox.

(b) Jupiter in the 9th will cause after-born but
with infantile death.

Notes: (b) Only Jupiter in the 9th is referred to.
His connection with the Moon and Mars is implied
as at “a” above. Ashtaka Varga Bindus are not a
part of this yoga.

3970 - 3971. (a) Jupiter in Varuna Nadiamsa
will cause birth of brothers endowed with longev-
ity, in the end of the first dasa. The last one third
part of 2nd dasa will also cause long-living co-
born.

(b) The native of Varuna Nadiamsa, Sagittarius
ascendant, will have only one brother.

Notes: Combinations at “a” and “b” are differ-
ent from each other. One emanates from the
position of Jupiter while the other does not.

the subject Nadiamsa with Satur in the 3rd

3973 - 3975. (a) The trio - Mars, the Sun and
Mercury -inan evil house, Saturn in the 3rd, Jupiter
and the Moon in the ascendant, Ketu and Venus in
the 4th, and Rabu in the 10th - one born with this
horoscope will be greatly happy, bea great man and
be troubled in childhood. He will bea poetand will,
have a limited number of issues.

(b) Brother: As a result of the said yoga, the
following results will occur concerning his brother.
He will be a virtuous person, will enjoy kingly
fortunes, be wealthy and be an illustrious person
after the age of 20 or 32. He will be endowed with
learning, discourses, entertainment, spouse, prog-
eny and fame, and will delight in association with
others. He will be versed in a particular school of
the Veda (“Sakha Vichakshana”). His fame will
spread “in the heavens and on the earth”, (i.e. he
will be exceedingly famous). After the 10th or the
16th year, his father will be happy. From the 5th or
8th year, father will be mentally distressed for a
period of 8 years.

Notes: (a) The position of Mars, Mercury and
the Sun in an evil house should be understood as to
be in the 6th only (not 8th or 12th) as Venus is
supposed to be in the 4th.

There is no mention of the ascendant in the
verse concerned. It is to be deemed as Sagittarius
for the current dealing is with this ascendant and
reference to Jupiter and the Moon (8th lord) occurs
in the nearest verses. This link can be established
from sloka 3982 infra.

Individual combinations of the present horo-
scope are also discussed by the original, in the
ensuing slokas.

(b) “Sakha” denotes school of the Veda, éach
school adhering to its own traditional text and
## Page 163

148 Deva Keralam

interpretation and the scholar recites that particular
version of the Veda. According to sage Saunaka’s
“Charana Vyuha”, 5 Sakhas exist in the Rig Veda;
42, 44 or 86 in the Yajur Veda; 12 (out of original
1000) in the Sama Veda, and 9 in the Atharva Veda.

“Sakha” isrecognised as anastrological Samhita
by Varaha Mihira. Thus “Sakha Vichakshana” can
also denote one versed in a certain branch of
astrology.

amsa will bring forth prosperity for the father from
the native’s 18th year of age probably through.a
replacedrruler. Hishappiness will bereduced when
Vaishnavites become powerful. After the 8th year
of the native, father will be anguished and will
recover some happiness 18 months later.

3978 - 3980. (a) Due to the blemish of 8th lord
(ie. Moon is assumed to be in the ascendant,
Sagittarius), the dasa of 6th lord (Venus) will check
father’s prosperity during the sub period of the 10th
lord (Mercury). Father will incur enmity with
Vaishnavites, particularly from his kinsfolk. He
will face a great deal of humiliation, anguish etc. in
royal circles.

(b) In the said sub period of Mercury, the
subject will lose his brothers and sisters being born
at that time. He will be mentally distressed in his
9th year and will incur difficulties for his co-born.

3982-3983. Education: (a) The 9th lord (Sun)
joining the Sth lord (Mars) in the 6th (Taurus) will
in the Sun’s dasa commence one’s education.

(b) Education can also commence in the dasa of
the 11th lord (Venus) in the sub period of the 2nd
lord (Saturn).

3984 - 3985, Venus in Aquarius Navamsa:
Jupiter in the ascendant and Venus with Ketu in the
4th (Pisces) will make one skilful in three lan-
guages apart from giving him expertise in author-
ship and learning. However, Venus should obtain
Aquarius Navamsa. Further, one will be fond of
drama, literature and music. He will transact im-
pressively in the Sanskrit language and be devoted
to gods and Brahmins.

planets in Pisces and begetting (for himself) a

one highly skilful in authorship and learning. But
he will be somewhat a dunce.

3987 - 3989. There is another authority to
instruct that Jupiter in Varuna Nadiamsa in aspect
to Mars will obstruct the process of education:
However, the person concerned will be quite fa-

mous in royal circles. In his 3rd dasa, he will be
## Page 164

worshipped by the ruler and will exceed his father
in terms of prosperity, perhaps in royal circles. The
last one third part of the 2nd dasa will cause great
eminence. There will be a family marriage as well.

3990 - 3991. The 7th lord (Mercury) in Leo
Navamsa while Venus isin Aquarius Navamsa will
bring forth marriage in one’s own country or region
(ie. bride will be from his own region), orelse from
the northern direction. The bride’s birth sign will
correspond to the one occupied by the 7th lord
(Mercury), a trine thereof, or the one that is as-
pected by him.

Notes: “Daridrajaam” in sloka 3991 is not a
proper term. It should read as “Darese” denoting
the 7th lord. “Daresa drishti rasau” meaning “the:
sign aspected by the 7th lord” confirms this conclu-
sion without any doubt.

3992 - 3996, (a) Saturn in Capricorn Navamsa
while Venus in the sign Pisces in Aquarius Nav-
amsa will cause obstacles to the marriage of the
native of the subject Nadiamsa.

(b) For the subject native, Saturn in the 3rd
(Aquarius) will cause obstacles the moment mar-
riage is Sought. This will be because of his sins (of
the past birth). After a trial of 2 or3 weeks, he will
succeed. Due to the sins of his brother-in-law, evils
will exist in the in-laws’ family.

(c) The 3rd house is the house of father-in-law.
When the said house is in possession of a malefic

by occupation, the native’s father-in-law will be
endangered once he gives his daughter to the native,
in marriage.

(@ Note if the said occupant is in a benefic’s
Navamisa, or isin aspect to a benefic, as the 3rdlord
is strong. Then in spite of the blemish of giving
away his daughter in marriage to the native in
question, the father-in-law will receive auspicious
and inauspicious effects. (That is inauspiciousness
will come down.)

3997 - 3998. Mars in the 7th (Gemini) in
Mangala Nadiamsa will cause a spouse with blood-
red complexion. The person will be happy follow-
ing his marriage and will endlessly enjoy wealth
and auspicious effects. His family will acquire
quadrupeds and wealth.

3999 - 4001. Maternal Uncle: (a) One will
have (only) one maternal uncle while his father-in-
law will have two wives if Mars is in the 6th
(Taurus) with Aries Navamsa and Mangala Nadi-
amsa.

(b) The said maternal uncle will incur evils
during the 6th lord's dasa if the ascendant lord
(arrives in transit) in the sign represented by the
Navamsa occupied by the 6th lord (i.e. Aries
‘Navamsa as at “a” above), or a trine thereof. The
same way Navamsa occupied by Jupiter (at birth
will also have to be considered).

(©) Jupiter in Varuna Nadiamsa will give only
one maternal uncle. Further one will be much
grieved on account of loss of brother in childhood.

Notes: (a) For Mangala Nadiamsa, see notes
under sloka 226, page 24, Book 1.

(b) There is no clear mention of transit in this
## Page 165

150 Deva Keralam

verse. The dasa of the 6th lord (Venus) is referred
to which leads to such a possible interpretation.

4002 - 4006. (a) Maternal Grand-father:
‘Mars ruling maternal grand-father (the 12th from
the ascendant which is the 9th from the 4th) and
posited in Mangala Nadiamsa will ensure longev-
ity to the said relative who would lose many a
brother. He would incur much grief on account of
loss of progeny in his old age. He would somewhat
be in the order of a house-holder.

(b) Grand-parents: Jupiter in Varuna Nadi-
amsa will give longevity to mother. His maternal
grand-parents would also be long-lived.

(© Paternal Grand-father: Mars, the planet
denoting paternal grand-father, (as the 5th lord)
should be in Mangala Nadiamsa and Aries Nav-
amsa. Asaresult, the saidrelative would be famous
and would have co-born. He would acquire pros-
perity following his marriage and be happy. He
would live in a town surrounded by forests and be
happy throughout his life.

(@) The lord of the Sth (Mars) in the company
of Mercury, begetting Aries Navamsa, denotes that
one’s paternal grand-father would have large testi-
cles. This applies to Prabha Nadiamsa.

Notes: (d) Since Prabha Nadiamsa and Varuna
Nadiamsa for Sagittarius ascendant appear in the
same breath, identical effects for both the di \:
are to be understood for this sign ascending. Such
a mention for two different Nadiamsas has been
made earlier.

4007 - 40012. (a) Birth in Sagittarius ascendant
with Taurus Navamsa while Jupiter is in Varuna
Nadiamsa in the company of the 8th lord Moon,
will grant longevity to parents. The native’s pros-
perity will occur after the age of 20. At 25 he will
beget a superior conveyance. Through his leam-
ing, he will make wealth at 28 (read from 28) along
with his father. ;

(b) One born in the 2nd half of Prabha Nadi-
amsa, Sagittarius ascendant while Jupiter is in
‘Varuna Nadiamsa will undergo mental distress for
a period of 8 years from his 9th year.

(c) The native of Ist half of the said Nadiamsa
will enjoy happiness after the 8th year. His father
will have increased prosperity and will be in em-
ployment for 10 years. After this, he will incur
poverty and be endangered 8 years later.

(@) One of 2nd half will be happy after 18
months and his father will earn much prosperity in
the end of the native’s Sun dasa.

No. 4013 stands incomplete.) ‘The ascendant is

4016 - 40217, (a) Limited Fortunes: Birth in
Sagittarius should be during day time while the Sun
sign is occupied by a malefic. Fortunes will then
be meagre provided the Sun sign is bereft of a
benefic’s aspect.

(b) One will be fortunate if the ascendant
Sagittarius begets the Moon's occupation while
Jupiter and the 9th lord (Sun) are in order in Varuna
and Kumara Nadiamsas. The person will obtain
fortunes, subsequent to marriage, which will ex-
ceed that of his father. He will be happy in his early
days while in his pre-youth days he will not be so
happy. Fortunes will rise along with his adoles-
cence. Wealth will come to him in his 3rd dasa.

(c) Father: The yoga mentioned at “b” above
will cause some troubles to father because of the
Moon's 8th lordship. Following paternal grand-
mother’s death, his father will be grieved. (“Sukhi”
denoting happiness to father in a contradictory
term in the present context.)

(@) The Sun in Kumara Nadiamsa and joining
Satur in the 10th... in that dasa, one’s prosperity

will be checked. In the end of the first dasa, in the
dasa of .... due to blemish of 8th lordship, the 2nd
dasa will not grant prosperity.

Notes: (4) “Khanda” means deficient, crippled
or a chapter. None of the meanings suits our
context in the expression “Khanda Dvaya”. The
word “dvaya” means two.

Similarly, “Khandadipati dayake” meaning “the
dasa of Khanda” leads us nowhere. No house ina
horoscope is called “Khanda” although this term
occurs in Ashtaka Varga for a group of four houses.
Perhaps, the reference is to the 2nd dasa which is of
the Moon. So naturally the first dasa checking
prosperity should be of the Sun who is in the 10th
with Satur. This assumption of dasa periods is
corroborated by the ensuing sloka.

4022 - 4024. Raja Yoga Effects: The 8th lord
‘Moon in conjunction with the ascendant lord (Ju-
piter), while the latter is in Varuna Nadiamsa, will
cause wealth in the 3rd dasa. The native will get
Raja Yoga effect (leading to kingly prosperity) or
something equal to that. Conveyances, omaments,
powerful employment, happiness and birth of chil-
dren will all result. His sister will also become
wealthy.

Notes: The condition of Chakra Yoga seems to
be an interruption because Chakra Yoga by itself is
a far superior one. Also please see notes under
sloka 244, page 27, Book 1 hereof for further
details.

4025 - 4029. Pilgrimage: (a) The native of
Sagittarius ascendant having Jupiter in Varuna
Nadiamsa will undertake a pilgrimage along with
his father in the dasa of the 11th lord (Venus).

(b) When Saturn transits the sign denoted by
the Navamsa of the lord of the ascendant (i.e.
Jupiter), or a trine thereof, one will go to Setu (on
pilgrimage) in the dasa of the 11th lord (Venus).

(©) The 4th dasa and Sth dasa will also give
pilgrimage to Setu etc. for a period of three weeks.
Dips in sacred Ganges will also be the result
concerned.

(@ Rabu in the 10th will enable one to under-
take pilgrimage to the Ganges or else to the Setu,
for a period of three weeks.

(e) The subject will get the fruits of a great
donation if the 9th and 10th lords are conjunct at
birth. Further he will construct a colony of Brah-
mins and acquire fame.

Notes: (e) “Maha Dana” means a certain valu-
able or great gift. Sastras like “Pancharatra” have
enlisted sixteen such Gifts. Some recognize seven
Gifts.

Some of the Gifts are - offering libations to
ancestors (Pinda Dana), giving away a girl in
marriage (Kanya Dana), donating a cow (Pasu
Dana), and poor-feeding (Anna Dana).

4030 - 4031. Dasa of Jupiter: (a) In the dasa of
Jupiter (placed in Varuna Nadiamsa), the native of
Sagittarius ascendant, will experience good and
bad effects in an equal measure.

(b) Mercury's sub period thereof will produce
evils which are probably remediable.

(©) He will have to die in the sub period of the
8th lord (Moon) in the dasa of the said dasa of
Jupiter.

4034 - 4039. (a) Jupiter in Sagittarius ascend-
ant with the aspect of Mars will cause passionate
and somewhat indolent dispositions.

(b) A Superior Raja Yoga: Jupiter in the
ascendant, Venus in the 4th (in Pisces), and a
malefic in the 3rd (Aquarius) will give a royal
assignment with authority. One will rule over
many countries, be versed in the just duties that are
due to a king, will attain fame and be an expert in
ancient scriptures. A devotee of Lord Siva, he will
perform service expected of a devotee of Siva.

(c) The Sun in Scorpio in Kumara Nadiamsa
will make a Sagittarius native 4 devotee of Lords
Vishnu and Siva. He will ever take to a good path
of conduct and be interested in the Vedic conduct.
He will be specially devoted to Devi (female deity)
and be versed in justice and meritorious deeds. Sot
also will his father be. He will be quite famous in

4040 - 4043. (a) Father: One of the signs of
Saturn should become the Arudha for the 9th.
Saturn should be in the said Pada and beget Capri-
corn Navamsa. As aresult, the native’s father will
beget the following effects. He will be inwardly
irascible, but be pleased and be a miser but be ever
ready to help others. He will be interested in sacred
texts and ancient laws.

(b) Paternal Relatives: Further, as a result of
the said Yoga, the native will have two paternal
uncles one of whom will be a great sinner. Another
will be meritorious. Father’ s sister will have along-
living husband, will have many sons and her prog-
eny will acquire period after period. Fame and
conveyances will be acquired by her sons through
the kinsfolk.

Nadiamsa will be truthful. Two of the native’s
paternal uncles (father’s brothers) will be childless.
After a long time one of them will have progeny.
According to sage Garga, one particular paternal
uncle will have a good son.

4046 - 4052. The following are according to
sage Garga.

(a) Paternal Uncles: Jupiter in Varuna Nadi-
amsa while the Sun is Kumara Nadiamsa will give
sons to a brother of the native’s father. The other
of the two uncles will have a barren wife or will
beget dead issues. After some time, he will obtain
long-living progeny. This view has been held by
senior Garga.

(b) The Native: The 5th lord Mars in Mangala
Nadiamsa in the company of the Sun and Mercury
will give a limited number of issues to one born in
Varuna Nadiamsa, Sagittarius ascendant.

(c) Saturn in the 3rd (Aquarius) will give many
dead children. Perhaps one son will be endowed
with longevity.

(@ Jupiter in Varuna Nadiamsa will give two
wives and also progeny. Two sons and two daugh-
ters will be long-lived,

(e) The Sth lord (Mars) in Mangala Nadiamsa
will also cause two wives and progeny to the native
of Prabha Nadiamsa. His children will be many in
number. In the 19th year, during the 3rd dasa, he
will obtain a son. Due to Saturn’s placement in the
3rd (Aquarius) his first issue will be destroyed.

Not (e) Prabha Nadiamsa and Varuna Nadi-
ama in Sagittarius are capable of giving identical
effects, as mentioned earlier.

() Add the longitudes of the lords of the 9th,

4058 - 40641. (a) The birth sign of one’s son

will correspond to the Navamsa occupied by the
Sth lord.

(b) Mercury’s Dasa: The lord of the 10th
(Mercury) joining the 9th lord in Mangala Nadi-
amsa will in his dasa bring forth much fortunes.
Obstacles will crop up in the sub period of Mercury.

(©) Moon’s Dasa: The Dasa of the 8th lord
(Moon) will cause differences with co-born and in
royal service, during the sub period of the 10th lord
(Mercury). The native’s spouse will incur physical
illness and he will have a change of place with an
adverse transit of the Sun, Distress to the king and
his men will also result(?)

(d) Sun’s Dasa: The following are the effects
of the dasa of the 9th lord. His father will obtain
predominance and will be abundantly anguished
due to Vaishnavites and friends’ circles in the sub
period of Mercury. Later in the sub period of
‘Venus, father will become predominant and be
happy. During the dasa of the Sun, with the
movementof Saturn in Leo, marriage of the daugh-
ter of one’s paternal uncle will take place. But the
said uncle will be subjected to evils.

Notes: (b) “Karmesa” is a wrong addition as
already the 10th lord is denoted by the term
“Rajyadhipa”.

4065 - 4073. Evils: (a) Saturn moving in the

:

Chandra Kala Nadi 155

end of Sagittarius or in Capricorn, when the dasa of
the 8th lord (Moon) is in operation will promote
evils to one’s spouse. There will also be a great
danger in the in-laws’ family.

(b) One’s mother will be subjected to evils as.
Saturn passes through the following places - the
sign equal to the Navamsa occupied by the 11th
lord, a trine thereof and the sign aspected by the
11th lord.

(©) Father's death will occur in the 4th dasa
with Saturn moving in the end of Aries orin Taurus.
The corresponding month will be shown by the Sun
transiting in the sign equal to his natal Navamsa
position or a trine thereof.

(@ Evil to father will persist during Saturn’s

with Saturn's transit in the sign caused by the
‘Navamsa position of the 4th lord, or a trine thereof.

(©) One's father will mentally be distressed
with Saturn's transit in Scorpio. The relevant dasa
will be that of the 9th lord (Sun) and the sub period
will be of the 8th lord (Moon). Further, there will
exist fear of father’s untimely death for which

(e) At the time of Saturn’s transit in the sign
‘equal to the Navamsa position of the 12th lord or a

will be destroyed (?) Evils to the members of the

way, effects for one’s king are foretold(?)

andat that ime... the minister ... Apparently, there

4074 - 4078'2, Irrelevant: In the end of the
ascendant lord’s dasa, with Saturn’s journey in
Gemini’s end, or in the 8th (Cancer), the country
will reel under partial famine due to absence of
rains. In the beginning and ending of 2nd dasa, or
in the 3rd dasa, Saturn’s transit in Libra, intense
shortage of rains and fear of famine will come to
pass. Lack of rains will coincide with Jupiter's
transit in Sagittarius ... Saturn in end of Gemini ..
Acalamity will occur to one’s distress, particularly
to one’s king. Saturn’s stay in Virgo, for a native
of Chandra Yoga(?) will cause loss of army and
horses in the region placed between Krishna and
Godavari. The same will occur in Pandyakingdom
when Pisces is transited by Saturn. In Taurus, he
will destroy Mlechchas. Chola kingdom will per-
ish with Saturn coming to Cancer. In the trines of
Cancer, Saturn’s movements will kill the king of
Pandya.

4079 - 40842. The Sun in Kumara Nadiamsa
and joining the 10th lord (Mercury) will give the
following effects for a Sagittarius native. He will
have a soft and huge body, be greatly intelligent,
will eam a lot of trust and affection of the ruler and
will become predominant through the king in the
## Page 171

156 Deva Keralam

10th lord’s dasa. For about three weeks, he will be
subjectedto royal wrath. Soalso for one weekin the
3rd dasa as well as the 4th dasa, Royal wrath will
be specially evident in the Sth dasa, during the sub-
rulership of a planet of obstacles (i.e. a planet ill-
disposed to Sagittarius). When the transit Sun
comes into the signs denoted by the Rasi and
Navamsa positions of the lords of the 6th, 8th and
12th, misfortune from royal circles will be present.
The subject will be imprisoned, will lose his wealth
and be anguished. These should be estimated with
the help of sub periods.

Notes: “Rajyapa” and “Karmadhipa” both in-
dicate the 10th lord only.

The present slokas continue to deal with Varuna
Nadiamsa of Sagittarius ascendant, and not with
Kumara Nadiamsa. The Sun’s position in Kumara
Nadiamsa is now dealt with, as done on earlier
occasion. This position is finally mentioned in
slokas 4100 and 4101 also. The current text thus
deals with 3 different Nadiamsa positions for 3
different planets as mentioned in sloka 4101.

Kumara (or Kumari) Nadiamsa has been dis-

cussed in the notes under sloka 244, p 26, Book 1. -

4085 = 4092. Jupiter in Sagittarius with Virgo
Navamsa While the Moon is in the sign Aquarius,

(or Jupiter andthe Moon being in order in Virgoand
Aquarius) will in their dasa periods trouble the
native by diseases. In the first dasa, the first sub
period (whichever sub period rules the native as the
opening one) will nearly kill the subject. The 8th
lord’s sub period will cause fear of untimely death.
When Saturn comesto the Jeeva Rasi at the time of
junctions of two dasas (KCD), the person’s kins-
folk will confront evils. In the beginning and
ending of Libra dasa, or in Scorpio dasa, intense
difficulties will exist. Saturn moving in the end of
Virgo or in Libra will cause fear of untimely death.
To overcome this fear, donation of an image of the
zodiac be done. In the 19h, 20th or 2st year, when
the junction of Kala Chakra dasas of two Rasis
occurs, misfortune will strike the native of Varuna
Nadiamsa. To surmount this phase, remedies have
to be sought. By donating the image of a zodiac to
the astrologer, fear of untimely death will vanish.
‘The adversities of the dasa junction include evils to
people equal to the native, loss of a child in the
relatives’ circle and anguish to maternal uncles
because of death of maternal grand-father.

Notes: In sloka 4086, “Kanya Jive Ghate
Chandre” occurs which may otherwise look to be
in excess of what has been stated as in the 2nd line
of sloka 4085 - Jupiter in Sagittarius & C. One of
the two conditions can only apply but both cannot
at one and the same time exist. It however occurs
to me that the condition of the two planets, Jupiter
and the Moon, in mutual 6 and 8 positions in Virgo
and Aquarius is logical. As reference to Kala
Chakra dasa occurs simultaneously, Virgo and
Aquarius could be Jeeva and Deha Rasis, or vice
versa. In that case, the dasa references are to that of
the signs concerned.

4102 - 4107%2, (a) These are the effects for
transits and for the Nadiamsa positions of the 3
planets (Jupiter in Varuna Nadiamsa, Mars in
ae Nadiamsa and the Sun in Kumara Nadi-
amsa).

(b) When the dasa of Taurus (KCD) com-
mences, its owner Venus transiting his exaltation
sign will produce effects of Raja Yoga. The 3rd
dasa (Vimsottari) will give wealth. Because of
Ketu’s association (with Venus at birth, vide sloka
3973 - 3975 supra), the dasa of Taurus will cause
anguish in the last 1/6th part. Further his father will
incur obstacles to his prosperity.

(c) The dasa of Gemini, as the 2nd one (follow-
ing Taurus dasa as the first one), will not give
predominance to the native of Prabha Nadiamsa
because of the aspect of the 8th lord Moon on it
(from Sagittarius, vide slokas 3973 - 3975).

(@) The dasa of the Sun placed in the 7th
(Gemini) will in the middle of Sun’s dasa cause
royal honour to father anda family marriage. Atthe
time of the commencement of the Sun’s dasa, if the
transit Sun is conjunct Jupiter in a sign that is trinal
to the natal Sun’s Navamsa position, the dasa will
give auspicious effects. Further, one will beget an
eminent position and conveyances.

Notes: (c) Normally, terms like Janma, Sampat,
Vipat etc are used only to denote the order position
in Vimsottari. Here in respect of KCD also, such
terms are used, Perhaps it is because we have nine
## Page 173

158 Deva Keralam

sequences in KCD as in Vimsottari.
(@) This is independent of slokas 3973 - 3975.

4108 - 4110, (a) Making a dasa into twelve
parts according to the sign being odd or even, the
results for sub periods are declared by astrologers.

(b) The sign aspected by the ascendant lord will
give auspicious effects.

(©) So also the signs aspected or occupied by
the 9th and 10th lord.

(@ The 2nd dasa and the dasas of the four
angular signs will yield auspicious effects.

Notes: (a) This is an incomplete clue and
belongs to Jaimini school of astrology.

(d) Same as “a” above.

4111 - 4113. (a) Transit of Jupiter: Jupiter’s
transit in the end of Gemini will give wealth. With
his movement in Cancer, much prosperity
come to pass apart from marriage to sister. He will
give Raja Yoga effects and auspiciousness through
father while moving in the end of Leo or in Virgo.

(b) In the sub periods of malefics and in earlier
mentioned adverse months, one will undergo a lot
of difficulties in undertakings and incur obstacles
to prosperity.

Notes: These apply to Jupiter’s transits while
the 2nd dasa operates for a Sagittarius native.

Certain transits.of the Sun are noted as adverse,
vide sloka 3842 - 55% for Libra ascendant. It is not
known whether the same apply here.

4115 - 4116. In the last one third part of the 3rd
dasa, or in the 4th dasa, when Saturn transits the
end of Capricom, or Aquarius, the native of Sagit-
tarius ascendant will-have his 2nd marriage. This,
should be declared after analysing the dasa periods.

Notes: Results in continuation of this yoga are
mentioned in two more verses, i.e. 4119 and 4120
infra. Thus slokas 4116 to 4118 are independent of
the present yoga featuring Mars.

4117. (a) Looking at transits with reference to
“added longitudes” (read sensitive degrees), re-
sults should be declared.

(b) In the dasa of the lord of the Sth, great
danger to paternal uncle will occur.

(c) In the dasa of the lord of the 8th, father’s
sister will be distressed.

Notes: (a) “Added longitudes” refers to the
sum total of the degree positions of lords of speci-
fied houses and or specified planets, as done, for
example, in slokas 4053 - 4057%.

‘We can understand these in a better way, thus.
We add the longitudes of the lord of the 5th and
Jupiter and scrutinise the same with future transits
to know about progeny. The present hint is a
general given by the text stressing the importance
of transits on sensitive degrees.

4118, Note a house ruling a specific relative
and also the planet ruling the 8th from that house.
Locate the Navamsa position of the said 8th lord.
When Saturn transits the sign represented by the
said Navamsa, or a trine thereof, death of the said
## Page 174

Chandra Kala Nadi 159

relative will occur according to the blemish of
killer-planets (Marakas).

Notes: In this verse, Saturn’s transit is used to
know about the time of death of one’s relatives.

For example, take a Pisces ascendant horo-
scope. Leo, the 6th therefrom denotes maternal
uncle or aunt. The 8th from Leo is Pisces whose
lord is Jupiter. Say Jupiter is in Gemini Navamsa.
Hence when Saturn transits the sign Gemini (not
the Navamsa Gemini) or Libra, or Aquarius, the
person's maternal uncle or aunt will die.

However another condition is added, because
Saturn’s transit in one sign or the other, out of the
said three, is not rare. Simultaneous Maraka

respect of the said uncle, along with Saturn's
specified transit, are a part of the event. In the
absence of such Maraka influences, death of the
relative should not be foreseen. But some kind of
illness, misfortune etc. to a maternal relative can
occur.

This way, the various family members should
be connected with Saturn’s transit.

4119 - 4120. The native’s prosperity will rise
after 35. He will acquire wealth, importance and
honours from the ruler of the kingdom and will
conquer his foes in a battle.

Notes: The present two verses are in continu-
ation of sloka 4115'supra where Vargottama Mars
in Leo is required to be under Jupiter's aspects.

4121 - 4123. Navamsa Positions of Sun &
Mercury: Should the 7th lord Mercury be in Leo
Navamsa while the Sun is in Aquarius or Leo in
Navamsa, the native will have illicitrelations with

women. He will have somewhat windy aphysique.
After some interval, he will emerge pure-hearted
and will give up illicit ties. He will adopt a just
path. He will suffer from diseases to some extent.
His spouse will ever be troubled by diseases. Butin
between she will recover. With the help of the
Ashtaka Varga of Venus, happiness of spouse
should be elaborately understood.

4124 - 4128. Mars & the Sun: (a) The native
of Varuna Nadiamsa having the Sun and Mars in
order in Kumara and Mangala Nadiamsas will live

"upto the ‘age of 56. He will incur physical illness at
28. A great danger will strike him during his 36th
year. Trouble from fever and death of children
being born will come to pass at the age of 22.

(b) The death of the above native will occur in
the dasa of Aries (KCD) or in the dasa of ascendant
lord (Vimsottari). Saturn in transit will be in Aries
(the KCD sign), while the Sun will be in Sagitta-
rius, Gemini or Virgo. Death will be on a Monday
in bright lunar half.

(©) There is another authority to state that the
day of death of the said native will be a Thursday
in bright lunar half.

Notes: In sloka 4128, the term “Kumaramse
Mangalamsa Samudbhavah” means the Sun and
Mars'respectively placed in these Nadiamsas as
stated earlier, and not birth in these Nadiamsas.

4129 - 4131. After Death: (a) With the Sun in
Karala Shashtiamsa while Jupiter is in Varuna
Nadiamsa, one’s sins and meritorious deeds will be
equal in measure. He will attain the Heavens after
death. According to some, he will attain none, (i.e.
neither the Heavens nor the Hell - will have re-
birth).

(b) Ifthe 12thlord Marsisin Deva Shashtiamsa,
he will attain such world according to his race of
birth and be a wise person.

(©) Thus have been brought to light the effects
of the Nadiamsa positions of the 3 planets.

Notes: (a) Reference to Kumara Nadiamsa is
not essential with reference to the Sun.

(b) Similarly Mangala Nadiamsa for Mars.

“Attaining a world according to race of birth”
is not a clear expression.

(©) These refer to Jupiter, Sun and Marsin order
in Varuna, Kumara and Mangala Nadiamsas, as
have so far been dealt with.

With this, the discussions on Varuna Nadi-
amsa, Sagittarius ascendant ends.

4132- 4134.50 Sagittarius Ascendant: (a) With
the Moon in the Vipra Kala of Uraga Nadiamsa
while Mercury is conjunct Jupiter, a Brahmin will
be born.

(b) The Moon in the Soodra Kala of the said
Nadiamsa and with aspect or conjunction of Mars
will cause Balarishta or infantile mortality. The
native isa Soodra by birth and will be the 3rd or the
4th issue of the family. He will have no after-bomn,

Notes: (a) From here upto sloka 4188, the
ascendant dealt with is Sagittarius without any
Nadiamsaspecifiedas rising. However, the Moon's
Position in Uraga Nadiamsa is a simultaneous
requirement.

(b) “Paschat bhagya vinasavan” should read as
“Paschat bhratru vinasavan”. Since the number of
issues is being discussed it should relate to co-born
(“Bhratru”) and not to fortunes (“Bhagya”).

4135 - 4136. The Moon in the 10th in the first
half of the said Nadiamsa in the company of Rahu
and with Jupiter’s aspect would cause birth of the
child entwined in umbilical cord. The Moon in the
2nd half of this Nadiamsa will not cause this
blemish: Due to aspect of Jupiter in the first case,
the child will be happy, i.e. free from troubles at the
time of birth.

4137 - 4142. (a) The Moon in the 2nd half,
Uraga Nadiamsa will cause fear of infantile mortal-
ity to some extent. The birth of the child will be in
a town under the rulership of Mlechchas, near a
forest region.

(b) The Moon in the Isthalf of Uraga Nadiamsa
will cause birth in a country of meritorious deeds,
in acolony of Brahmins. Another authority men-
tionsbirth ina temple of Lord Siva. The subject will
probably be the only issue of the family while his
co-born will be destroyed.

(©) One having the Moon in the 2nd half of
Uraga Nadiamsa will have sisters and brothers. He
will lose his father in his childhood. Probably he
## Page 176

Chandra Kala Nadi 161

will have three older brothers. He will be endowed
with wife and sons. He will be honourable and will
have much fortunes through business activities.
During his 8th year, his father will be endangered.
If he survives this period, there will be surely death
of father in the 10th year. His 16th year will prove
auspicious.

Notes: (c) The 2nd line of sloka 4140 denotes
one elder brother to the person with the Moon in the
Ast half of Uraga Nadiamsa, which is not included
in the translation to avoid conflict with the result
mentioned at “b” above. This line seems to be an
interruption to the effects for 2nd half.

4144-4145, The Moon & Rahu: The Moon in

the 10th (Virgo) in the firsthalf of Uraga Nadiamsa

in the company of Rahu will cause a bluish (i.e.

dark) but sound physique. He will be quite intelli-

gent, will have broad eyes, be eloquent in speech

and will acquire a dark-bodied spouse. He will

have limited. wealth and limited progeny and be

famous.

4148 - 4150. (a) The Moon in Uraga Nadiamsa
in the company of a malefic but receiving the
aspect of a benefic will give the following effects.
The person will be given to anger inwardly, be a
pleased soul, will be favourable even to enemies
and will have two undertakings,

(b) The Moon in the 1st of the said Nadiamsa
with Jupiter's conjunction or aspect will give a
peaceful disposition, learning and wealth.

(©) In case the Moon is in the other half of the
subject Nadiamsa, one will be a thief. He will be
fond of illicit pleasures with women in the absence
of which he will have quarrels because of his
wicked wife.

(@) With the Moon in the Ist half one will
belong to a good family. He will be devoid of
paternal fortunes, will have self-earned wealth and
be dear to his parents.

Notes: (a) “Dvi-karyavan” should reads “dvi-
bharyavan” indicating two wives. “Karya” means
undertaking while “Bharya” means wife.

will give the following effects. The native will be
wealthy, valorous and grateful. He will be dis-
posed to commit sins, be irreligious, infatuated by
his friends and will eat anything.

ered by fair means, will serve Mlechchas and will

4154 - 4156. (a) The Moon in the 2nd half of
Uraga Nadiamsa will cause 3 elder brothers and
two wives to the native.

(b) In case of Ist half, there will be one sister
who will have a long-living husband due to Jupi-
ter’s aspect (on the Moon).

(©) Mother as well as paternal grand-parents
will be long-lived with the Moon in 2nd half of
Uraga Nadiamsa. After the 8th year of the native,
paternal-grand-father will die.

4157-4162. (a) For one having the Moonin the
2nd half, the blemish of Rabu will be in abundance.
The native’s father will be long-lived. Paternal
grand-mother will die in the native’s 16th year.

(b) Evils to paternal grand-father will exist
with the Moon in the Ist half Uraga Nadiamsa.

(©) One’s maternal family will be destroyed if
the Moon as at “b” is in aspect to Mars and is ina
friend’s (or Leo) Navamsa.

(@) The Moon in the 2nd half of the subject
Nadiamsa will cause expansion of maternal family
and great happiness. He will have three maternal
uncles one of whom will have two wives.

(©) Evils tomaternal family willexist in the 2nd
dasa of the native who has the Moon in the former
half of Uraga Nadiamsa. Losing relatives and
wealth, his father will be saddened with debts. The
native’s brothers will gradually prosper from time
to time and as a result he will acquire fulfilment of
desires, marriage and wealth.

Notes: (4) Expansion of maternal family indi-
cates acquisition of sons and their sons by one’s
maternal grand-father.

4163 - 4165%2. Wife: (a) The Moon in the Ist
half of Uraga Nadiamsa will cause a wife who will
be ever in the grip of diseases.

(b) Ketu in the company of the 7th lord (Mer-
cury) will cause eye diseases to one’s wife. Due to
sins of his previous birth, he will beget a spouse
who will suffer from health problems.

(©) The Moon in the 2nd half of Uraga Nadi-
amsa indicates that one’s spouse will be wicked but
will have a sound body free from diseases. She will
be aquestionable woman during her life-time. The
native will have two wives.

4166 - 4174¥2. (a) The Moon in the Ist half of
Uraga Nadiamsa in aspect to Jupiter will cause
longevity to two sons and two daughters. At the
time of the native’s marriage, there will be heavy
rains. His kinsfolk will incur troubles from royal
circles.

(b) The Moon in the 10th while Jupiter is in the
6th (Taurus) in Sura Nadiamsa, there will be no
Gandanta blemish. (That is the ascendant is as-
sumed to be in the junction of Scorpio and Sagitta-
rius.)

(©) The Moon in Uraga Nadiamsa will cause
long life to father and father-in-law.

(@) Should the Moon be in the Ist half of the
subject Nadiamsa, mother will die in the native’s
3rd dasa. Danger to father (i.e. death) will occur in
the 2st year or 22nd year. Otherwise, 36th year
will doubtlessly cause death of father.

(e) Saturn transiting the sign identical with the
‘Navamsa occupied by the Sun or the 9th lord, or a
trine thereof, will promote father’s death.

(® In the 20th year, mother and paternal uncle
will be endangered. After his father’s death, his
father-in-law will quit the world,

(g) During the operation of the dasa of Mer-
cury, the transit of Saturn occurring in the sign
identical with the Navamsa position of Mercury, or
a trine thereof, father’s death can take place.

(h) Father-in-law’s death will occur when Sat-
um influences the Navamsa of the 3rd lords at “g”
above.

(@ Saturn moving in Aries in the dasa of 8th
lord and sub period of 1 1th lord, will cause death of
paternal grand-father in case the Moon is in the Ist
half of the subject Nadiamsa.

Uraga Nadiamsa will cause auspicious effects dur-
ing the later half of 3rd dasa. He will obtain
prominence on the strength of hisown intelligence.
Oldage will be asource of troubles forhim, He will

4178 - 4182¥2, (a) Lagnadhipati Nadi: Now
stated are the effects collected from the chapter on
## Page 179

164 Deva Keralam

“Effects” from Lagnadhipati Nadi narrating in
detail about the first half of Uraga Nadiamsa.

(b) Jupiter’s Dasa: In the 2nd half of the dasa
of Jupiter, the ascendant lord, the native’s brother-
in-law will become predominant.

(©) 1th Lord’s Dasa: Venus dasa, Saturn's
sub period, will endanger the native to a great
extent. Jupiter's sub period will produce mental
distress but an equal measure of good and bad
effects.

(@) 10th Lord’s Dasa: Mercury’s dasa as the
Sth one will cause fear of windy diseases to some
extent, Increase of wealth and grains, and prosper-
ity toa brother-in-law will come to pass. The sub
period of Venus intense difficulties will occur.

Notes: “Koorma” means tortoise. “Koorma
Phala” is apparently conveys no sense. Itshould be
“Karma Phala” dealing with livelihood.

4185 - 4188. (a) The Moon in the Ist half of
Uraga Nadiamsa, while the Sun occupies his de-
bilitation sign and Navamsa will bless one with
longevity. 71 years willaccrue tohim by Yoga Ayu
method. By Amsayu method, it will only be 61.
Death will be during the Sun’s southerly course.
The Sun will be in Cancer, Virgo or Scorpio. His
sins and meritorious deeds will be in an equal
measure. Laterhe will be bom as valorous Soodra

in the Nakshatra of Indra (Phalguni).

(b) With the Moon in the 2nd half of Uraga
Nadiamsa, one will be a great sinner and will not
attain good world after death.

(c) One having the Moon in the Ist half of the
said Nadiamsa will beget ... effects, and happiness
and unhappiness.

Notes: (a) “Indresa Nakshatra” reads as
“Indrasya Nakshatra”, which indicates the
Nakshatra of Indra, i.e. Phalguni.

According to Parasara, Indra is the presiding
deity for Svathi and Anuradha.

‘Yoga Ayu indicates life-span arrived at by
certain planetary dispositions. Amsayu is one of
the systems of calculating life span, like Pindayu
and Naisargika Ayu as discussed in Brihat Parasara
Hora.

(c) The last line of sloka 4188 contains “Mitra
Phalam”. Mitra means friends, “Phala” effects. It
should read as “Idam Phalam” meaning “thus, the
effects”. However this is not important.

4189 - 4195, Aquarius Ascendant - Durdara
Nadiamsa: One born with this Nadiamsa rising in
a fixed sign, as Jupiter joins or receives the aspect
of Saturn, will beget the following effects. His
elder will be destroyed (will die early). He will
have younger brothers, be wealthy and emaciated
in childhood. He will have two wives, be passion-
## Page 180

Chandra Kala Nadi 165

ate and given to anger but immediately pacified.
He will be fond of learning, of consuming sweet
and sour juices, be libidinous, dear to his mother,
sacrificing in disposition and attached to young
women. He will have paternal wealth, fond of his
younger co-born, will lose his paternal uncleand be
troubled by small-pox in childhood. He will have
injuries on his body and be troubled windy disor-
ders. He will be passionate, somewhat indolent and
will eatuneatable things. He will also be somewhat
gentle, be skilful in earning wealth and will lose his
first son. He will have increase of sons through his
second wife. Inall, he will have two surviving sons
and two daughters. His brother will have similar
number of children,

Notes: Though reference to fixed sign occurs,
most of the current slokas - upto sloka 4308 -
specially apply to Aquarius ascendant. The other
three fixed signs should also be suitably incorpo-
rated.
Notes: For details of the subject Nadiamsa,
pleas consult notes under sloka 438, p. 44, Book 1.

4196 - 4198. (a) The ascendant lord in the 8th
(particulary Saturn) in Mohana Nadiamsa in as-
pect to Jupiter will cause fear of infantile mortality
to some extent. This blemish can be removed by
palliative measures.

(b) Malika Yoga commencing from the 6th
from the ascendant will cause urinary disorders.

(©) One with the ascendant lord in Mohana
Nadiamsa will cheat others of their undertakings
and wealth. He will obey his father’s orders, will
incur disorders of digestion, will help others, be
passionate and somewhat justly disposed.

4198 - 4202. (a) Progeny: Saturn in Virgo (8th)
and Jupiter in Pisces (2nd) will cause a limited
number of children. The native will obtain many
dead children. One son and one daughter will be
endowed with longevity.

(b) Sisters: The lord of the 3rd, Mars, being in
an angle from the ascendant with Libra Navamsa
will cause twolong-living sisters. One of them will
be ever anguished.

(©) Sister & Brother: Jupiter in the 10th will
cause a long-living husband to asister. One brother
will marry a wicked woman, will have obstacles in
begetting progeny and will not be well-disposed to
his coborn.

4203 - 4204. One born in Durdara Nadiamsa
with Jupiter in Pisces will be highly fortunate,
interested in righteous deeds, ever in the grip of
diseases, will lose his coborn to some extent and
willhave two wives. He will be endowed with sons,
intelligence and skills to earn wealth.

4205. Mother’s Life-span: The Moon being in
Sudha Nadiamsa in Cancer with Jupiter’s aspect,
will increase the life-span of the mother of an
Aquarius native.

under sloka 454, page 45, Book 1.

4206 - 4208. Father: The number of father’s
co-born will correspond to the number of (recti-
fied) Ashtaka Varga Bindus obtained by the Moon
and the Sun. There is another authority to state that
the father of the native of Aquarius ascendant,
Durdara Nadiamsa, will lose all his co-born. He
will obtain fortunes after some time (with initial
obstacles). During childhood, he willbe emaciated
and will live well in his maternal uncle’s home.

4209 - 4213¥3. Maternal Family: (a) The 6th
lord Moon in aspect to Jupiter and obtaining Can-
cer Navamsa will cause expansion of the maternal
family of Aquarius native. Perhaps, he will have
two maternal uncles.

(b) Some authorities including the work
Naradeeyam state that in case of birth in Durdara
Nadiamsa of Aquarius ascendant, Jupiter in aspect
to Saturn will give only one maternal uncle, but two
‘maternal aunts in another context (or occasion) i.e.
when Jupiter is not aspeted by Saturn.

(©) Thus has been said about the house of
maternal uncle (the 6th).

(4) The maternal grand-mother of the native of
subject Nadiamsa will be long-lived.

(e) In a work of Sukracharya (the legendary
preceptor of Demons), long lives to maternal un-
cles have been indicated. Mother will have two
sisters one of whom will be endowed with sons and
longevity. She will be somewhat sickly, be ema-
ciated due to loss of brother and will obtain much
fortunes due to her sons’ rise.

Notes: (b) "Adhikacchati" vide sloka 4211
should read as "Adhigacchati"

4214 - 4219. (a) Jupiter’s Dasa: The subject
will be highly fortunate if Jupiter in the sign Pisces
acquires Aquarius Navamsa, while Venus is in the
9th from the ascendant (i.e. in the sign Libra). {n
the dasa of Jupiter, abundant fortunes will come to

(b) One belonging to the 2nd half of the subject
Nadiamsa will obtain vehicles. He will beget wide
fame at the time of the prosperity of his paternal
uncles and aunts. He will become predominant as
well.

(©) First Dasa: Saturn, the ascendant lord, in
Mohana Nadiamsa in Aries and receiving the as-
pectofabenefic will produce muck fortunes during
the first dasa. Third year of birth will be more
auspicious (for the family).

(d) Dasa Effects: If the 11th lord (Jupiter),
denoting father’ s co-born, is in the 9th (Libra), the
dasa of Jupiter will usher much fortunes. The 2nd
year will give wealth (to the family).

(©) Ascendant lord (Saturn) in Mohana Nadi-
amsa (in Aries as stated earlier) with Jupiter's
aspect will give 3 vehicles to.ason of father’ sister.
The 3rd dasa will give beneficial results and one
will obtain predominance and conveyances.

4222 - 4229. (a) Evils: Saturn transiting the
sign identical with the Navamsa occupied by the
12th lord, or a trine thereof, will cause loss of a
child and enmity with the king (ie. professional
enmity). The minister will lose his life?) The
native’s paternal uncle will incur evils.

(b) Dasa periods: Saturn in Mohana Nadiamsa
in Viruddha Avastha(?) (apparently in an enemy's
sign) will cause grief to the native due to enmity
with royal sources in the 3rd dasa. Loss of position
and mental anguish will prevail fora periodof three
weeks in the 4th dasa. Afterwards he will return to
his place and will earn favours from the king and
minister.

(©) Maternal Relatives: Satum placed in
Durdara Nadiamsa as the Moon occupies a Nav-
amsa of Jupiter will cause two brothers and two
sisters, all long-lived, to the mother of the native.
There may be oné maternal uncle instead of two
(according to some). After some time, maternal
uncle will be in grief due to loss of brother. Two
of the maternal aunts will be blessed with sons and
daughters. One will be anguished and be emaciated

due to loss of brother.

(@) His mother’s sister will be emaciated due to
loss of brother and son in his (i.e. the native’s) 2nd
dasa. One of the maternal aunts will have a long-
living husband and many sons.

4230 - 4239¥%, (a) Venus in the 11th with Aries
Navamsa in “Mrityu Cheshta” will cause loss of
brother in case of the native’s father, prior to the
native’s brother. Father's maternal uncle will be
childless. Father would be in grief due to loss of an
elder brother. When the native will be in his 3rd
dasa, father will be grieved due to loss of younger
brother.

<(b) In case of birth in Aquarius ascendant,
Durdara Nadiamsa, Jupiter in aspect to Saturn will
cause childlessness to father’ smatemal uncle. Due
to grief thereof, (i.e. childlessness), the said rela-
tive’s spouse will somewhat become impure. This
Yoga will bring a blemish for the maternal family
of the father. Or else (i.e. in the event of the said
## Page 183

168 Deva Keralam

aunt of the father being pure in character), father’s
maternal uncle’s daughter, or alternatively the
native’s sister, will be corrupt. (Thatis, the present
yoga will lead to existence of a questionable woman
in father’s maternal family. Or'else, the native’s
sister will be immoral.) The native’s mother will
be a chaste woman.

(c) Saturn journeying in the sign identical with
the Navamsa position of the 9th lord, or a trine
thereof, will destroy the progeny of mother’s sister.

@) Similarly Satum’s journey with reference
to the 11th lord will produce much grief in the
mother’s family.

(©) Cousin: The Navamsa dispositor of the 3rd.
lord Mars occupying an angle or a trine counted
from the ascendant (Aquarius) will cause follow-
ing effects toa son of the sister of the native’s father
(ie. toa cousin of the subject). He will be wealthy,
endowed with full and half co-born and will ac-
quire paternal inheritance. His father will have two
wives, He (i.e. the said cousin) will marry atthe age
of 12. Afterhis 8th year, many efforts will be made
for his marriage.

Notes: (a) The condition laid down is faulty.
“Meshamse” reads as “Meshantye” meaning “end
of Aries”. Further there is no standard term as
“Mrityu Cheshta” which is obviously indicative of
“Mritavastha” or a planet being in the last six
degrees of an odd sign. If Venus is required to be
in the end of Aries, reference to his position in the
11th (Sagittarius) is inadmissible. Hence ignoring
the condition of “end of Aries” and naturally
“being in extremis”, the implication is: Venus
should be in the 11th in Sagittarius (within first 3°
20’), thus begetting Aries Navamsa.

(e) “Pitrushvasu Sutamsese” should read as
“Pitrushvasu Sutesa Amsese”. Thus we have to
locate the Navamsa dispositor of Mars, the 3rd
lord, who indicates the progeny of the paternal aunt
in case of an Aquarius subject.

4240 - 4244, Paternal Grand-father: Mer-
cury (the Sth lord) ruling the native’s paternal
grand-father should be placed in the 9th (Libra)
along with the Sun, as Jupiter is in the 2nd, one
would enjoy happiness through the said relative.
Paternal grand-father would lead a difficult life and
be endowed with elder brother and long-living
sister. He would also be long-lived and would lose
his co-bomn. After some time, in his old age, he
would be grieved due to loss of progeny, spouse and
co-bom. Prior to the native’s birth, grand-father
would have lost his spouse.

Notes: “Bhagye Jive” in sloka 4241 (contain-
ing one line only) reads as “Vite Jive” in another
version requiring Jupiter to be in the 2nd.

4245 - 4247/4, Maternal Family: With Venus
in the 11th (in the sign Sagittarius) in Gada
‘Nadiamsa while the 12th lord (Saturn) isin Mohana
Nadiamsa, one’s maternal grand-parents will be
long-lived. A younger brother of maternal grand-
father would possibly adopt a son. One of the
native’s maternal uncles will be endowed with
longevity and will have 3 sisters. The 2nd dasa will
cause distress in maternal uncle’s home. Maternal
grand-father will be grieved losing his son-in-law.
The native’s mother’s sister will die an untimely
death during the course of pregnancy.

4248 - 4257. Maternal Family: (a) In case of
birth in Durdara Nadiamsa, Jupiter posited in Pis-
ces in aspect to Saturn, will cause death of two
‘maternal aunts (mother’s sisters) during pregnancy.
Or the spouse of maternal uncle will have such
death. On account of such grief, maternal grand-
mother will be mentally distressed. During the
native’s 3rd dasa, his paternal grand-father will be
grieved on account of loss of a son.

() The 4th lord in the 4th (Taurus) in Gada
Nadiamsa in the company of Ketu will bring forth
evils in maternal family in the dasa of Venus and in
a malefic’s sub period. 3rd dasa will endanger
maternal grand-father.

(©) Satumn’s sojourn in the sign identical with
the Navamsa of the 7th lord (Mercury) or a trine
thereof will endanger one’s maternal grand-father.

(d) Saturn’s movement with reference to as-
cendant lord’s Navamsa on the same lines will
cause mental distress to maternal uncle due to loss
of maternal grand-mother.

(©) A malefic in the house concerning the
husband of mother’ sister (i.e. in the 12th from the
ascendant) will cause dead childrenand widowhood
to mother’s sister. As a consequence, she will be
quite saddened. The 2nd dasa’s concluding part,
or the 3rd dasa has been described thus by the
astrologers.

(© Paternal Uncle: Father's sister’s husband

will be long-lived, rich and happy. The end of Ist
dasa, or 2nd dasa will cause wealth. The 2nd year
will give full effects, and prosperity (to the said
relative) will commence. :

4258 - 4260. Evils in 2nd Dasa: The Moon and
Saturn in order in the 6th and 8th will greatly
endanger one in his 2nd dasa, fora period of 2 years
from the age of 5. This blemish, of infantile
mortality, should be suitably remedied. Father’s
family will also incur adversities in the form of
quarrels with kinsfolk. Father's friends and rela-
tives will pass through an evil phase. 2nd dasa will
thus produce a series of upsets.

4261 - 4264, Malika Yoga Turning Evil: (a)
IfMalika Yoga commences with the sign occupied
by the lord of the 6th (i.e. the Moon), Daridra Yoga
(yoga for utter poverty) forms. The native’s child-
hood will (also) be marked by difficulties.

(b) When Daridra Malika Yoga is formed (asat
“a” above), the 8th from the ascendant having a
malefic, without beneficial aspect, will unfailingly
promote penury.

(©) If Daridra Malika Yoga forms while Saturn
isin the 8th and the 2ndis occupied by Jupiter, one
will become prosperous (instead of incurring pov-
erty). His old age will be significant for penury.
Intermittently he will enjoy auspicious effects and
great fortunes, including conveyances.

‘Notes: (a) Malika Yoga, a favourable one, for
## Page 185

170 Deva Keralam

prosperity can also turn adverse and cause poverty
= not fortunes - if the 6th through the 12th are
occupied by 7 individual planets. The 6th house
should necessarily have the Moon.

(©) In this part, a proper understanding is
necessary. Only 7 planets (to the exclusion of the
nodes) are authorized to cause Malika Yoga. Natu-
rally, by implication, Daridra Malika Yoga should
as well be restricted to 7 planets only. Hence the
right interpretation is that Saturn in the 8th should
begin the yoga to be concluded by Jupiter in the
2nd. Thus, the present yoga is bound to be inde-
pendent of the one cited at “b” above.

4265 - 4271. Venus in 2nd & Jupiter in 11th:
Let there be a hundred evil combinations at birth.
If the 11th and 2ndare in occupation by Jupiter and
Venus in order, one will be wealthy, famous and
will beget name in royal circles. His 3rd dasa will
greatly accelerate his fortunes. Particularly its
later half. He will enjoy continual wealth, high
esteem and prosperous employment. Beneficial
effects will occur during the time of prosperity of
one’s paternal aur!’s son, particularly during the
4th dasa. In the St, dasa, half of the period will
cause distress. Good and bad will come to pass;
prosperity and diseases will be included. Troubles
will show up after tne age ot 52. The dasa of the 8th
lord (Mercury) will be distressful. 2nd half of
Mercury's dasa will usher poverty and grief on

account of loss of a son. In the end of Mercury's
dasa, death of the native will result. Fear of
untimely death will exist in the middle of the said
dasa. In the 2nd dasa, father will proceed on a
pilgrimage to Setu. On the way, he will be in grief.
In Setu (i.e. in the destination) he will be troubled
by fever. To overcome this blemish, a milk-pot
should be gifted away. Health will prevail later on
and he will return to his native place.

4272. Rabu in 10th: The native with Rabu in
the 10th will undertake pilgrimages in the 3rd dasa.
He will visit shrines and earn the merits of having
had dips in the Ganges.

4273. Pilgrimage: Jupiter in Durdara Nadi-
amsa will be an obstacle for dips in the Ganges. But
the native wili visit the Setu and will earn the merits
of having had dips in the Ganges.

1th (Sagittarius) in Gada Nadiamsa will cause
troubles through diseases just at the time of one’s
prosperity. Indigestion and futile journey, the latter
for a period of 3 years, will come to pass.

4275 - 4283.’ Diseases: (a) Saturn in the 8th
(Virgo) will cause fear of small-pox and “Sannipata”
(acomplicated derangement of the three humours,
causing utter collapse, sometimes literally death).
To some extent, one will suffer from dysentery as
well. The wise should guess these effects in the 4th
dasa.
(b) The sub periods of Jupiter and Mars, in all
the dasas, will cause physical ailments (for one
born in Durdara Nadiamsa, Aquarius ascendant).
‘The corresponding months should beknown through
the Sun’s evil transits. Mercury's sub period will
also to some extent be capable of causing illness.
During Satum’s sub period, in an evil month, when
Saturn gets combust, one will fall sick, ona Wednes-
day, Tuesday or Thursday.

(©) Fortunes: The 3rd dasa will be a source of
great fortunes with Jupiter transiting in Sagittarius,
Aries or Gemini. Father etc. will also obtain
wealth.

(@ Enmity: Jupiter’ s 3rdround of transitin the
end of Libra or in Scorpio, in the 4th dasa, produce
calamitous enmity with the king.

(© Parents: The 4th lord (Venus) in Gada
‘Nadiamsa will destroy father in the native’ s 30th or
35th year of age. 24th year will greatly endanger
‘one’s father. In the 5th dasa, with Saturn moving in
Gemini, one will lose his mother in the 24th year.
Or Saturn moving in the end of Sagittarius or in
Capricorn, as Jupiter transits Gemini or Cancer,
will kill the mother. She will beextremely troubled
when these transits are in trine thereof.

(© Progeny: At the time of Saturn’s sojourn in
the sign caused by the Navamsa position of the 12th
lord, or a trine thereof, or the sign that is aspected
by the 12th lord at birth, evils to one’s own children
will be experienced.

4284-4288. (a) The respective relative will die
when Satum transits the sign represented by the
‘Navamsa occupied by the lord of the 8th from the
relevant Bhava. The sign aspected by the 8th lord
from the said Bhava will also be applicable for
‘Saturn’s transit in the same manner. This should be
guessed from the ascendant through the 12th house
(for the various relatives).

(b) Deha Rasi containing a malefic will cause
physical ailments. A malefic in Jeeva Rasi will
produce danger to life. In the junctions of (Kala
Chakra) dasas, great danger and mental distress
will come to pass.

(©) The Sth is called Rajya Sthana. The 9th is
called Prabhu Sthana. The 10th is called the house
of friends. Relativesare indicated by the 4th house.
(Danger to) relatives, friends and enemies of the
king should be understood from the Navamsa
occupied by the 8th lord from the Bhava con-
cemed.

Notes: The instructions given at “a” and “b”
have appeared more than once in the whole text.

However, the one at “c” should look to anyone
to be somewhat strange. Except kinsfolk denoted
by the 4th house, the other three houses have been
designated in rather a peculiar way. Rajya Sthana
and Prabhu Sthana both are denoted by the 10th
house. Friends are denoted by the 11th house.
Further dealing with the king’s life from another
native’s horoscope sounds quite illogical. After
all, each citizen of a kingdom (or a country) is
bound to have conflicting influences of planets at
one and the same time. What then will indeed
transpire in the king's life is beyond imagination.

4289. According to Manu: Note the positions
of the lords of the 8th of the varioushouses from the
ascendant and others (i.e. upto the 12th), and the
signs aspected by each of these 8th lords. Saturn’s
transit (in the relevant place) will cause death (of
the relevant relative). Good and bad effects (con-
cerning the relevant relative) should accordingly
be estimated.

Notes: In sloka 354, Book 1, the texthas given
the use of the Navamsa dispositors with Saturn's
transits in regard to danger to specific relatives.

In the present case, (a) the sign position of the
concemed 8th lord, and (b) the sign aspected by the
said 8th lord, from a given Bhava, is brought into
play in the context of impending danger by Sat-
um’s transit. In my notes on p.37, Book 1, the
reader could find description of relatives by each
house and the 8th from each of such Bhava.

‘Saturn transiting (a) or (b) as above will endan-
ger the particular kinsfolk by way of illness, death,
calamity, accident etc.

We will have an example thus. The 12th from
the ascendantis the 8th for the 5th. To wit, the 12th
from the ascendant is the spot for danger to one’s
progeny. So also the sign or signs aspected by the
lord of the 12th. When Saturn transits the 42th
house, or the sign/s aspected by the 12th lord, or the
sign denoted by the Navamsa occupied by the 12th
lord, one’s children will become a matter of con-
cem - by danger, illness, running away from home,
death etc.

Also see slokas 4284 - 88 supra in this context.

4290 - 4299. Jupiter & Venus: For the native
of Aquarius ascendant, Durdara Nadiamsa, Venus
in the L1thas Jupiteris in the 2nd or 10th, will cause
great fortunes through Mala Yoga. (That is, the
present Yoga is described as Mala Yoga giving
great fortunes.) His fortunes will be in a town near
the region of a great river (i.e. watery zone). He
will prosper in a foreign land. He will enjoy
fortunes and will serve a mean king, be deeply
attached to trading activities, passionate, sacrific-
ing in nature and eloquent in speech. His 3rd dasa
will give him conveyances following birth of chil-
dren. Abundant prosperity and wide fame will
follow the 4th dasa. He will leave his birth country
and will enjoy uninterrupted wealth and happiness
(in a foreign land). Between 20 and 25, his
prominence will occur, and he will enjoy some
happiness. He will gather a lot of wealth following
his 26th year of age. Enormous grief on account of
Joss of friends and employer will have to be experi-
enced by him. The 4th dasa will cause him wealth
through a change of ruler (or employer). In this
dasa, his daughter’ smarriage will take place, which
will be followed by son’s marriage. The Sth dasa
will cause mental agony due to enmity with friends.
When Saturn moves in Libra, during 2nd dasa, he
will obtain co-born, Saturn’s journey in Virgo will
increase his fortunes; more so of his kinsfolk. His
father’s happiness will coincide with the time
prosperity of a son of his (ie. father’s) sister.
## Page 188

“Chandra Kala Nadi 173

Saturn transiting in Libra will give wealth (to
father) while in the middle of Virgo, some auspi-
cious results will come to pass. Transit of Saturn
in the end of Scorpio will destroy the native’s
paternal grand-mother apart from producing trou-
bles to maternal relatives and unpleasant changes
for relatives in general.

corn) of the 10th (Virgo). The translation is

4303 - 4304¥4, The 9th lord (Venus) in the 11th
(Sagittarius) in Gada Nadiamsa indicates that the
native was a Brahmin in his previousbirth. Hisnext
birth (following the current one) will be one with
material outlook (i.e. in the present birth, he has no
yoga for Emancipation). In the ending phase of
Brahmin birth, taking to ascetic order following a
family calamity is indicated. (That is in the previ-
‘us birth he took to such order). Thereby he would
eam merits of a good deed. Or else, he would be
next born as a person following the Vedic path.
After completing the next birth, he will attain
Emancipation, if the 12th lord (Saturn) is in aspect
to Jupiter.

Notes: “Vipra Kala” is not included in the
translation to avoid confusion. “Vipra Kala” is the
first quarter of a Nadiamsa.
