5244 - 5246. (a) Note the planet ruling the dasa
that will be the 8th from the commencing one. In
the sub period equal to that, during the dasa that is
the 6th from birth, Saturn moving in the end of
Pisces will produce fear of untimely death of the
person. Further, one may be separated from (or
lose) his older brother and see his men in the grip
of evil effects. But this phase is not stated to be a
death-giver for the native.

(b) The end of the said sub period (vide “a”
above) in the 6th dasa will usher a danger if at birth
the Moon in Pisces begets Scorpio Navamsa while
Mercury is exalted with Somalata Nadiamsa posi-
tion.

Notes: (b) “Naidhanesa dasa” in the 2nd line of
sloka 5246 is a faulty text. It only denotes the sub
period as above.

5247. Note the sub period as stated in “a”
above. This phase within the dasa that will be
eighth from birth (some may interpret it as 8th
lord’s dasa), death will occur with Saturn in Can-
cer. The transit Sun will then be in the Moon sign
or a trine thereof.

Notes: “Kulira Chakra” posesa riddle to detect,
the exact implication. Whether it refers to Cancer
- Moon dasa (as per KCD) or to Saturn’s journey in
Cancer. Such difficulty occurs in the later pages
also. Earlier part of the text has been so pliable that
it can be interpreted either way. In some places it
offers no acceptable meaning.

5248 - 5250. The Sun and Venus in Sudha
Nadiamsa as Mercury is Somalata Nadiamsa will
cause loss of fortunes in the 8th lord’s dasa. Its last
one third part will give some happiness and a
marriage function in the family circles. The na-
tive’s son will be married with Jupiter in transit in
Taurus with Aries Navamsa. Another authority
states that during the native’s 35th year, his son’s
marriage will take place.

Notes: The term “Sukerasarkshe” isa hurdle to
translate the 2nd line of sloka 5250.

12th having a Kartari yoga (i.e. malefics on either
side of the 12th) will only cause a medium span of
life. Further, one will not have progeny (or male

5262 - 5270. (a) The lord of the Sth in his sign
of debility, as the 12th is occupied by twomalefics,
will destroy twins born to the native. After this, his
fortunes will also vanish. Prior to these, he will lose
his brother and see his employer in a great danger.
‘Due toadverse transits of Saturn, the king’s brother
will die (?) Anguish in abundance will occur due
to loss of wives (to the native). The end of the 8th
lord’s dasa and the sub period of the 10th lord will
give these effects. But the native will enjoy
prosperity till the end of his life (?)

(©) In the dasa of the lord of the ascendant, he
will undertake a pilgrimage and beget auspicious
effects.

(©) With the Moon in decline in aspectto Satum
while the planet Jupiter occupies the 7th from the
ascendant, one will incur piles, intestinal disorders
and lung tuberculosis. Further, there will be a
danger of untimely death at 32. Suitable remedies
will have to be done to enjoy protection. At 40,
mental anguish will exist while health will have ups
and downs. The 41st year coinciding with the 1st
sub period of the 4th dasa will undoubtedly kill him
due to blemish of “Kalachakra etc. (7) Results
should be understood with the help of sub periods.

(a) Two malefics in the 12th will be a source of
pains. These planets will consign him to the hell
following death. In the chapter entitled “Cheshta
Kendra’s Effects (in the work consulted by the
author), decline of one’s dynasty is mentioned due
to causing distress to friends by one having
Chandradhi Yoga.

5271 - 5276. Scorpio Ascendant - Lunar
‘Trines: (a) The Moon and Jupiter respectively
placed in Snigdha and Seetala Nadiamsas will
cause ablack thin physique toone bornin “Sumada”
Nadiamsa, in Scorpioascendant, He will be the 3rd.
or the 4th issue of the family.

(b) Jupiter in the 11th (Virgo) will make one
wealthy and will cause older brothers.

(c) Jupiter in Seetala Nadiamsa, in an angle or
trine from the ascendant, will contribute to long-
living parents. The native will have brothers and
sisters.

_ (@) Indra Yoga: Jupiter in the Sth in his own
sign and be in aspect to or in conjunction with four
heavenly bodies will cause what iscalled “Chandra
Yoga” (read Indra Yoga) applicable to a fixed sign
ascending (i.e. toLeo and Scorpio only). One born
in Indra Yoga with Scorpio ascendant will have an
older brother who will be wealthy right from his
birth, be a great man, will enjoy pleasures and be
widely famous. The native’s father would be
indigent, would lose his father in childhood, be-
come thin-bodied, would lose his brother in the
middle of bislife tohis anguish and would have two
wives.

Notes: From sloka 5271 through 5294, the
ascendant dealt with is Scorpio.

(@) Chandra yoga in the text should read as
Indra Yoga. “Seetala Nadiamsa” in sloka 5275 is
redundant as it describes effects of Indra Yoga. In
the following slokas, in some places, the term
“Indra Yoga” redundantly appears which is ex-
cluded from the rendering.

‘Cause two sisters to one’s father. One of them will

5279 - 5288. (a) The Moon in Snigdha Nadi-
amsa will produce the following results. The
native will be a pleased person, will have a limited
number of friends, an emaciated and tall physique,
be pure (or honest), highly intelligent, timid, en-
dowed with elder descendants and be charitable.

(b) The said Moon joining Rahu and begetting
the aspect of Saturn will cause birth with umbilical
cord and to some extent fear of childhood mortal-
ity. He will live through royal patronage, be heroic,
be highly intelligent, subdued, pure, crafty, elo-
quent, will enjoy pleasures and will be pious.

(c) Jupiter in Seetala Nadiamsa and being in
aspect to Saturn and Mars will trouble one of
Sumada Nadiamsa, Scorpio ascendant, with indi-
gesiion and small-pox during childhood. The Ist
## Page 258

Chandra Kala Nadi 243

dasa will somewhat cause a calamity. Jupiter,
being the lord of the 8th from the Moon sign (i.e.
the Moon should be in Taurus or Leo), will nearly
kill the person. To ward off his lunar blemish, a
silver image of the Moon god should be wor-
shipped and donations given, For two or three
weeks (i.e. fora brief duration), in the Ist dasa, the
subject will incur physical ailments which can be
surmounted by charities. The image of the Moon-
g0d duly robed should be placed on a copper vessel
accompanied by white rice grains, vegetables etc.
along with butter. According to Manu, one should
with his own hands worship the Moon-god with
white flowers reciting the “Amritaya” Mantra and
should donate it (the image) to the astrologer.
Tuberculosis or other great diseases will vanish and
the blemish of infantile mortality will be elimi-
nated. The child will instantly recover its health,
and auspicious effects in an increased measure will
‘occur to parents.

while Satum is in he ascendant in aspect to a
benefic will cause longevity with the donation ofa
“strong” (healthy) cow; the parents and the child
will be relieved of a thousand blemishes.

living older brother.

(©) The lord of the ascendant (Mars) in the 10th
(in Leo) will produce wide-spread fame particu-
larly in the 3rd dasa. His childhood will restrict his
happiness.

Nadiamsa: One belonging to the Ist half of this,
Nadiamsa will be the Ist or 2nd issue of the family.
His birth will be in a street spread from the east to
west, near a temple located in a place surrounded
by water and many a tree. The one born in the 2nd
half of the subject Nadiamsa will be the 5th issue.

5298 - 5308. (a) The native of Cancer ascend-
ant will havea symmetric physique if the lord of the
ascendant (the Moon) is in exaltation, or if the
ascendant is occupied by Jupiter. So also when the
ascendant or its lord begets Jupiter's aspect.

(b) Navamsa of Venus: Birth in Dhanada
Nadiamsa of Cancer as the Moon in a Navamsa of
‘Venus will make one talk in a limited manner. He
will have limited sleep and be highly wakeful. He
will ever be in royal service, will have a number of,
brothers, be garrulous, truthful, and will have a
beautiful name and appearance. He will be an
expertin various branches of learning, arts, singing
and musical instruments, and be a scholar of Phi-
losophy and Advaita (Non-duality, the school that
identifies supreme soul with human soul; Cf Dvaita
or duality preaching that the said two souls are
separate from each other). He will be addicted to
illicit union with women. He will organize meet-
ings of learned men and be fond of literature and
drama, will daily listen to mythological stories and
will honour Brahmins and Gods. He will take the
side of truth and be a connoisseur of arts and
Sastras. He will be intentupon coition with outside
women on prohibited days, be lucky, virtuous and
will experience Raja Yoga effects. He will be
blood-red in complexion, of medium build and
short-statured. He will be wicked 1o wicked people
and will repay cruel men in the same coin. He will
not enjoy prosperity through father. His wealth
will aczvuc by his own efforts.

(c) Saturn's Navamsa: Venus, the 4th lord,
being in a Navamsa of Saturn will give the follow-
ing effects. The subject will be an expert in the
‘meanings of various Sastras. He will help one and
alland be endowed with valour, jocular disposition
and wide fame. He will ever listen to mythological
accounts, be wealthy and well-disposed to the

destitute, He will hold vows and fastto please Lord
Vishnu and be fond of hearing songs in praise of
Vishnu. He will earn religious merits by construct-
ing temples, tanks and the like, be attached to
taking virtuous path and enjoy fortunes of horses
and palanquins (i.e. conveyances).

(@ If Venus joins Gemini Navamsa, he will
live by serving the king.

5314 - 5315. (a) The lord of the 4th (Venus) in
the 4th (Libra) with many planets will cause
widowhood to mother. He will lose his mother in
his youth, and father in his childhood.

(b) Jupiter in exaltation in the ascendant along
with the Moon, Venus in exaltation, Mars in Aries
and the Sun in the 11th (Taurus) will lead to the
following. The subject will be born with fine hair
on his head. He will be intelligent, be charitable,
an ocean of virtues, wealthy, famous and a great
personage. His abode will be a synonym of ever-
lasting wealth including horses, palanquins etc.

5318 - 5325%. Diseases & Remedies: The
native will be bereft of any torment (or trouble)
from his kinsfolk but will suffer from abdominal
and urinary disorders if the 6th lord (Jupiter) is in
debility (in the 7th) in relation to Mars by aspect or
union. He will become thin with endless diseases.
To overcome these blemishes, palliative measures
be adopted. Religious vows reciting the names of
the 12 Adityas (Mitra, Dakshaetc. representing the
Sun in the 12 months of the year) repeatedly on
Sundays. No food shall be consumed on Sundays
(during the currency of Vow). Sins of the past birth
and incurable diseases of the urine and glandular
(or splenary) enlargement will, as a result, be
destroyed. The astrologer should be honoured with
gifts before the commencement of vow for the Sun
god. By worshipping a Linga (of Lord Siva) of 4
‘Angulas (an ancient measurement) length covered
by “Rukma” leaves (scientific name of Rukma is
MessuaRoxburghii), one will obtain physical well-
being and auspicious effects. According to one’s
resources, as prescribed in the Vedangas (Cf
Mundakopanishadetc.), gold should be given away
incharity, preceded by Dhyana (Prayer), Avaahana
(Invocation) etc. with the prayer to the Sun God
with the following recitation:

“Divakara Namstubhyam Praanyagnaana
Pranasavaan /

Trayimayarkam Visvaatman Grahanaatha
Namostuthe //”

After the end of the Vow for 12 or 48 Sundays,
physical felicity will exist due to the mercy of the
Sun-God.

house in aspect to a malefic while the signifcator

only one wife. In“Randhra Nadi” effects of spouse
have been detailed in the “Bhava chapter”.

‘5329 - 5331. One belonging to Dhanada Nadi-
amsa in Cancer will enjoy a life-span of 72. In the
Ist dasa, he, will possess less wealth and less
fortunes. In the sub period of an adverse planet
during the 2nd dasa, he will be troubled by ill-
health and mental distress, and will earn less hap-
piness. In the end of the 2nd dasa, during his
Spiritual Initiation, he will face royal wrath, hap-
piness through co-born, ill-health and expenditure
‘on account of a royal decree. His 3rd dasa will
cause fear of untimely death.

5332 - $333. Libra Ascendant - Manohara
Nadiamsa: (a) One born in this space of the zodiac
will be rich from the time of his birth and be greatly
wealthy. He will be lofty-minded, wide-eyed,
wealthy, famous and happy.

(b) The lord of the ascendant (Venus) in the
Lith (Leo) with own Navamsa and endowed with
the aspect of Jupiter will cause a royal birth.

redundant and interrupt the continuity of the text.

5334 - 5338%4. (a) Conceptional Phenomena:
The child in question enters bis mother’s womb in
the month of Asvina, on Krishna Paksha Chaturthi
(4th day of dark lunarhalf) coinciding with Wednes-
day and Rohini Nakshatra with Capricom rising.
His birth ascendant will be the 10th from Adhana
Lagna (Capricom), that is he will be a Libra native
(with Manobara Nadiamsa).
() Previous Birth: His previous birth was of
a Brahmin. Then he performed penance and wor-
shipped Lord Vishnu. He was firmly devotedto the
Almighty, was a pleased soul and was learned in
moral law and ancient stories. He would donate
food to others, was a house-holder and patronised
many. He had much wealth and many children and
lived well upto 61 and died painlessly.
(©) Current Birth: His present birth will be in
the Ist half of Manohara Nadiamsa, Libra, in a
royal family. He will be the first issue and be ever
happy.

5343 - 5352. (a) One bom in the subject Nadi-
amsa will be charming in appearance, intelligent,
eloquent in speech and be endowed with attractive
face and eyes. He will be fortunate, virtuous,
black-bodied, handsome, and of medium build.
His eyes will resemble a lotus petal. He will
consume limited food, will speak in a limited
manner, willhave limited sleep and be exceedingly
intelligent. He will have symbols of wheel on his
palm ahd sole. A king and ruler of the earth, he will
have many servants. He will be garrulous in
speech, be truthful, skilful in many branches of

learning. He will have an attractive name and will
patronize gods and Brahmins. He will speak truth,
be knowledgeable in Philosophy and illicitly ad-
dicted to females. He will gather wealth by dint of
his own efforts and will achieve his aims by suit-
able expedients. He will take the side of good
people, will shun the wicked, be honoured by his
kinsfolk, be highly intelligent, be an expert writer,
be skilful in four branches of leaning and be
discerning in his undertakings. He will be ever
intent upon gathering wealth and will construct
temples, tanks and Brahmin’ s colonies for charity
purposes. He will be devoted to Lords Vishnu and
Siva, particularly to Devi (female deity), fond of
following the Vedic path, be passionate, will think
of others (i.e. be a well-wisher of others) and be
dear (to others). He will receive honours in assem-
blies, will appreciate others’ merits and achieve his
aims by suitable expedients. He will be troubled in
childhood and be happy after the 5th year.

(b) Birth in the subject Nadiamsa will be in a
prosperous family if the lord of the ascendant is in
the 11th (Leo) as the 9th lord (Mercury) is in the
10th (Cancer).

5353 - 5356. (a) Indra Yoga - Libra Ascend-
ant: The ascendant lord in “Yogamsa” in the “11th
& 9th” (read 11th or 9th) in aspect to the 10th lord
causes Indra Yoga (for Libra ascendant). One born
in this Yoga in Libra ascendant will enjoy pleas-
ures, be dear to the king, will take to the path of
virtues and will ever be equal to Kubera (in riches).
He will earn religious merits by constructing tem-
ple compounds, market-places (or watch-towers)
etc. and be happy.

(b) During the 6th year of age, when the Sun
transits the sign Cancer, the person will enjoy great
## Page 263

248 Deva Keralam

happiness and acquire a power of authority under
the king. The said Indra Yoga will bestow headship
of a country (or a region) and spotless fame.

Notes: (a) This sloka needs some rectification.
“Yogamsa” may be a misprint for “Yugmamsa”
meaning Gemini Navamsa (which has been dis-
cussed earlier also and appears in ensuing verses).

Thus, Venus should be in the 11th (Leo) or the
9th (Gemini) with an aspect of the 10th lord Moon.
While being so, Venus should be in Gemini in
Navamsa. As a result, one particular kind of Indra
‘Yogais formed for Libra ascendant. The native in
question will enjoy material pleasures, will per-
form charitable activities, be close to men in power
and be opulent.

(b) The age mentioned poses a riddle. Further
“Shashtage” means only the 6th house. The word
“varshe” adds to the confusion. Apparently we
should understand the relevant Dasa from a given
horoscope and find out which transit of the Sun in
the sign Cancer will denote the month to upgrade
the subject to royal power.

in the former half of Manohara Nadiamsa.

placed respectively in the ascendant, 8th and 5th
will produce fear of a disease in the very first year
of birth, ‘The 2nd year will be a source of great
danger.

Saturn be in the 8th (in Taurus), the person will be
troubled by small-pox, dysentery and danger of

- vitiation of the three humours. —

(b) General: Longevity will cometo passif the
8th is occupied by the significator (i.e. Saturn).

5361. Fear from Dog: The lord of the 4th
(Saturn) in the 8th while Mars is in the ascendant
will cause fear from dogs in childhood. The 8th
year will (also) be a cause of such fear to some
extent.

5362 - 5364. (a) Early Loss of Mother: Saturn
indicating mother should be in the Sth counted
from the Moon sign while the signifcator of mother
should be in the 4th from the ascendant. Asaresult,
the child will lose its mother in childhood.

(b) The Moon in the 4th and Saturn in the 8th
will cause early death of mother and being brought
up bya foster-mother (not necessarily step-mother).
The native’s mother will be virtuous, pure and be
devoted to her spouse. She will be gentle, religious
and be endowed with all kinds of prosperity.

Notes: The Moon should be in the 4th from
Libra (i.e. in Capricorn) while Saturn is in Taurus
(ie. Sth from the Moon). This Yoga is directly
repeated in sloka 5363. Then danger to mother
during the early years will have to be feared, apart
from other results mentioned above at “b”,

5365 - 536712. Effects for Father: The lord of
## Page 264

Chandra Kala Nadi 249

the house of father, Mercury, joining the significator
concerned, i.e. the Sun and begetting the aspect of
the 10th lord (Moon) will cause Raja Yoga to
father. He will be equal to aking, with this Yoga,
if the native belongs to Ist half of Manohara
Nadiamsa, Libra ascendant. Further, he would be
lonely (issue of his family), be libidinous, happy
with royal honours, famous in royal service, be the
head of royal stable and be independent. He will be
wealthy, bighly intelligent, will honour gods and
Brahmins, be modest, soft in speech, valorous,
passionately attached to young women and be
intelligent.

5368 - 5369. Mercury related to Satum by
aspect or conjunction and be in the 9th in Navamsa
(not necessarily Gemini Navamsa), will produce
the undermentioned effects concerning father. He
will have plenty of everything, will have foes, be
learned in music, literature and arts and will enjoy
pleasures with royal women. His acts will be
majestic and disposition kind.

5370 - 5373. (a) Danger to Father: The Moon
placed in the 8th from the 9th (i.e. in Capricorn
which is the 4th from the ascendant Libra) while
her dispositor Saturn is in the 12th (Virgo) will
cause danger to father. The impending danger will
be reflected when Saturn transits the sign Gemini
(the 9th) during the currency of the very first dasa
of the native. After this, the native will enjoy
abundant prosperity, will earn position with au-

thority under the king, be endowed with learning
and intelligence and be happy.

(b) Step-mother: The Moon in the 4th in
aspect to the Sun and occupying Gemini in Nav-
amsa, as the planet Mars is in the 10th from the
Moon (i.e. in Libra ascendant) will cause a step-
mother following the death of mother. His father
will (thus) have two wives. The subject will be
happy through his step-mother.

5374 - $376. Avatara Yoga & Effects: With
Mars in an angle from the Sun as well as from the
Moon while Mercury is angular to the ascendant,
Amsavatara Yogais formed. Its effects are: On the
native’s radiant palm, a symbol of white lotus
(“Pundarika”) will be found. Hispalm and sole will
have the marks of Sri Chakra and “Danda” (i.e.
club). On his chest, “Sri Vatsa” mark will exist.
His body will be erect like a staff. He will be an
illustrious person with a great deal of fame, will
please others with his virtues, be intent upon giving
away charities, kind to all living beings, will follow
religious vows, be eloquent and be an astrologer.
Notes: From sloka 5374 to 5599, the ascendant
generally discussed is Libra. In some cases, mov-
able ascendant also features, widening the scope of
applicability to all the four movable signs ascend-
ing. A judicious use of certain Yogas - toa limited
extent - can however be made to other ascendants.
‘Coming to the present Yoga, vide first line of
sloka 5374, reference to Mars appears from the Sun
as well as from the Moon. Apparently, the Sun and
the Moon should be in mutual angles (or in harm-
less conjunction) so that Mars is angular to both of
them.
## Page 265

250 Deva Keralam

“Sri Vatsa” is one of the three rarest symbols
explained in ancient physiognomy. The other two
are “Nandy-Avarta” and “Mauktika”. One having
such a symbol will be an illustrious person blessed
with incomparable prosperity and be historical.

Lord Vishnu is known as “Sri Vatsa” - favour-
ite of “Sri” -thatis Lakshmi. His chestis Lakshmi’s
abode.

Sri Vatsa also denotes a curl of hair on the
breast of Vishnu or Sri Krishna.

The essence of Amsavatara Yoga is that the
subject will have some of the most specially auspi-
cious symbols - though not exactly the same as
described in the original - on his palms and soles
which would be rare. He will have a straight body
and will be an illustrious man whom the world will
praise. Other effects enlisted are easy enough to
follow.

5377-5379. Hari Hara Brahma Yoga: Venus
in Gopuramsa, Jupiter in Paravatamsa and the
Moon againin Paravatamsa cause Hari Hara Brahma
Yoga. The native with this Yoga will be endowed
with health, prosperity, wealth and grains aplenty.
He will be the chief of men in the world and a
successful ruler. His speech will be auspicious and
eyes will resemble a lotus petal. He will possess
pleasing virtues, will enjoy pleasures and will be
surrounded by all his kinsfolk. He will have a
beautiful physique, be tom by lust, will enjoy
pleasures, be famous, honoured by all kings of the
world, highly charitable and be an astrologer. His
eye will be beautiful like a lotus. He will be

devoted to the Almighty. The wealth of his pleas-
ing virtues will last as long as the moon and sun
exist. He will enjoy wide fame on the earth.

Notes: “Hariharaadija” is the term used in
another version. “Aadija” denotes Brahma as he
was the’ “chief-of-the-Beings”. Brahma is also
known as “Aja” - unborn,

Characteristic of ancient literature, effects of
various rare combinations are quite exaggerated.
We should take quitessential clues behind them
and apply with wisdom, and not liberally.

5380-5383. Lakshmi Yoga: (a) Jupiter as well
as the Moon in Paravatamsa while Venus is in
Gopuramsa will produce Lakshmi Yoga.

(b) Alternatively, Lakshmi Yoga is formed by
the Sun in Paravatamsa in conjunction or aspect to
the 10th lord (Moon) while the 4th (Capricorn) is,
‘occupied by a benefic.

(©) Effects: This Yoga- either one of the above
two - will bless one with pleasing virtues, learning,
wide fame and charming looks. He will be the lord
of many countries. He will be honoured by the
kings extending to the ends of the various direc-
tions. He will be the leader of the king of kings and
will have many wives and sons. His fame will
extend to the three worlds (viz. earth, heaven and
nadir). He will be engaged in worshipping gods,
Brahmins and saintsand will construct temples. He
will be attached to the wives of other men, will help
## Page 266

Chandra Kala Nadi 251

one and all, be a great man and be an ocean of
virtues and charitability.

Notes: The firstkind of Lakshmi Yogais called
Hari Hara Brahma Yoga, vide sloka 5377 supra.

5384 - 5386¥. Kubera Yoga: One will be
equal to Kubera (god of wealth) or an emperor the
10th lord (the Moon) is in the 4th, or a trine or an
angle. He will be the lord of elephants, horses and
servants, will enjoy music and dance and will
obtain a good spouse after the age of sixteen. Due
to this yoga, he will beget happiness after the Sth
year. He will obtain a position of authority with the
king. In the 16th year, he will gather alotof wealth.
Should the four Bhavas, viz. the 2nd, 9th, 11th and
10th be strong, the native will be equal to Kubera
and will possess full-fledged wealth.

"5387 - 5391. Libra Ascendant: (a) Mars,
ruling the 7th, and placed in an angle or a trine
counted from the 7th (i.e. in the Ist, 4th, 10th, 11th
or3rd house) while Jupiterisin the 9th from the 7th
(ie. 3rd from ascendant) will give a virtuous wife.

- 9th from the 7th house (i.

(b) The 7th lord (Mars) in the ascendant, the
significator Venus in the 11th, and the planet
Jupiter related to 7th by aspect or placement will
cause many wives.

(©) The lord of the 7th involved in Indra Yoga
while the significator (Venus) is inGopuramsa also
will cause many wives. Further, the person will be
born with a Raja Yoga (combination for royal
status).

(d) One born in the Ist half of Manohara
Nadiamsa with Libra ascendant will have five or
seven wives.

(©) The conjunction of Venus with Rabu will
give ten wives.

Notes: (c) The 7th lord Mars being in Indra
Yoga is not understandable, Another version adds
to further confusion; it states Mars should be in
“Indra Bhava”. There is no such house known to
us.

(d) & (e): Combinations at “d” and “e” are quite
typical and.can be appliedin the contextof multiple
marriages provided the exact ascendant degree is
available.

5392. Brides from Various Castes: The lord
of the 7th, Mars, should be in a malefic’s Navamsa
and occupy a sign which is equal to an angle from
the Moon and also the 11th from the ascendant.
(That is Mars being in amalefic’s Navamsa should
be in the sign Leo while the Moon is in an immov-
able sign.) Such a native will beget brides from
various castes.

will be a source of evil to one’s spouse.

5395 - 5396¥4. For anative of Manohara Nadi-
amsa, Libra ascendant, the end of the 2nd dasa or
the first syb period of the 3rd dasa will cause
marriage apart from Raja Yoga effects. His pros-

perity and attainment of royal status will com-

success and royal kind of wealth will occur. He will
gain kingdom (or status) according to his capacity,
apart from much fame and valour.

5397. Kahala Yoga: Let “the sign dispositor
of the Nadiamsa of the ascendant be called “a”.
Call the sign dispositor of “a” as “b”. The planet
denoted by “b” should bein an angle from the rising
sign or the Moon sign. In effect, Kahala Yoga is
formed.

Notes: “Vilagna Nadi” denotes the Nadiamsa
rising in the ascendant, The Nadiamsa does not
however fall in ariother sign. It can only be in the
sign - any sign - rising. Obviously it denotes the
Navamsa of the ascendant and the correct text
should then be: “Vilagnaamsa sthita rasi natha”
etc. In that case, the planet denoted by “a” should
be knowa thus - the owner of the rising Navamsa.
Only then the Yoga can be properly produced.
Otherwise not.

“5398 - 5399. Effects of Kahala Yoga: The
subject will be wealthy, endowed with success,
will have wet (or perspiring) palms because of
giving (endlesss) donations, be ever libidinous, and
endowed with pleasures. He will earn abundant
fame after the age of 18. He will enjoy learning, the
play of musical instruments, entertainment and
assemblies, be a leader (or head) of women, be
endowed with prosperity, progeny, friends, kins-
folk, elephants, horses and servants and be a chari-
tableking. He will perform “Purusha Dana” and be
the head of a region.

Notes: In the 2nd line of sloka 5399, the term
“Tula Dana” means “Purusha Dana” which seems
to be one of the gifts or charities given at the end of
specified religious ritual. I could gather no mean-
ing more than this. For practical utility, it is to be
understood that the subject will be a donor of
religious note. Alternatively, Tula Dana could bea
misprint for Tula Bhara which means weighing a
person with gold, silver etc.

5400 - 5403. Sankha Yoga: Birth should bein
day time during a bright lunar half. The ascendant
should be in an angle to the Moon while the
ascendant lord (Venus) is with Rahu. Whatis then
formed is Sankha Yoga. Another kind of Sankha
‘Yoga will existif the 3rd, 4th and Sth signs from the
natal ascendant are occupied by benefic planets, as
a malefic is in the 5th. (That is the Sth should
## Page 268

Chandra Kala Nadi 253

contain both a benefic and a malefic.) The native
of Sankha Yoga will be wealthy, discerning in
disposition, learned in the branch of Vedanta or
Philosophy, charitable, leader of many people, be
aking, and endowed with the splendour of the sun.
Other kings from the various directions will ever
sing his praises. He will be a leader of women, be
prosperous, will perform meritorious acts and will
have a slender and charming body. He will speak
agreeably, will possess great mental velocity and
be highly valorous.

5404 - 5405. Bheri Yoga: Mars in the 8th/6th
from Saturn, Saturn in the 6th/8th from Mars, and
the Sun in the 6th/8th from Jupiter will produce
Bheri Yoga. The native concerned will be wel-
comed to the tune of musical instruments by sub-
ordinate kings in the company of elephants and
horses. He will be a king praised by virtuous men,
and be long-lived.

Notes: This yoga requires 6/8 positions be-
tween two pairs of planets, viz. Saturn-Mars and
Jupiter-Sun. Rich status, wide honours and long
life will be the results.

5406 - 54114. Libra Ascendant- Manohara
Nadiamsa: (a) The Sth lord, Saturn, placed in a
benefic’s sign with a benefic Navamsa, as Jupiter,
the significator, is strong, will doubtlessly enable
acquisition of a son (or progeny).

(b) Ketu placed in the 5th (Aquarius) as Saturn
is in the 8th (Taurus) with the aspect of Mars will
cause obstacles in begetting progeny. If, however,
the significator (Jupiter) is with strength, children
will be bom after delay. Alternatively, the 9th lord
(Mercury) should be in the 10th (Cancer) while
Jupiter is in the 9th (Gemini). However, Jupiter
should bein the 9th place in Navamsa diagram with
the company of another benefic.

(©) Jupiter with a malefic will promote acqui-
sition of children through re-marriage. The native
will have two sons and a daughter who all will be
long-lived.

(@) The lord of the Sth (Saturn) and the
significator (Jupiter) in “Yauvana amsa"(?)
through strength and weakness should be under-
stood the number of children. (This part of the text
is apparently incoherent.)

(€) One will obtain progeny through his wife if
Jupiter in strength is in the 9th from the 7th (i.e. 3rd
from the ascendant), at the same time being in the
9th place in Navamsa in the company of a benefic.

5412 - $414, (a) Jupiter in Gemini Navamsa
will give two or three children. Initially one will
beget female issues, and later on male issues.
Jupiter in the comipany of Ketu (or the Sth house
being occupied by Ketu as noted earlier) will be a

(b) The native will beget such a son in the first
sub period of the 4th dasa, with transit Jupiterin the

5415 - 5416. Sri Matsya Yoga: Jupiter or
Venus should be in the Navamsa occupied by the
9th lord (Mercury) to cause Matsya Yoga, the
effects of which are as under. The subject will be
an astrologer, be merciful, an ocean of virtues, will
attract females, be a king, blessed with a beautiful
physique, firm in disposition, fond of learning and
entertainment, be ever endowed with sons, spouse
and abundant wealth, and will enjoy prosperity
akin to the head of a country.

5417 - 5418. Movable Ascendant - Kala
Nidhi Yoga: The ruler of a movable ascendant
being in an angle in aspect to or combination with
Jupiter or Venus will give birth to one with Kala
Nidhi Yoga during day time. One with this Yoga
will be munificent, will donate lands, will have wet
palms (perspiring because of giving incessant do-
nations), be famous in the world, be endowed with
pleasing virtues, will patronize his kinsfolk, be
wide-eyed and will have symbols of fish on his
palms and soles.

5424, Rudrasana Yoga: If the 6th, 8th and
12th contain planets in (simultaneous) exaltation,
Rudrasana Yoga (or Rudrasanapada Yoga) is
formed.

expressed whereas it occurs again in right form, as

5425- 5431. (a) Effects of Rudrasana Yoga:
One bom in Rudrasana Pada (or Rudrasana) yoga,
according to Yavanas, will be the ruler of the entire
earth. Because of this Yoga, he will be happy and
prosperous right from his birth.

(a) Venus - Rahu conjunction & C (contin-
ued from sloka 5423); The native will enjoy
happiness on account of rulership of a country and
behappy after the age of 16. He will lose his mother
in the first month, during the first year of the first
dasa (i.e. within one month of birth). In the 8th
month of the 1st year, he will incur fever (or illness)
caused due to digestive deficiency. During the 2nd
year, his father will be mentally distressed and be
troubled by sickness. This period will somewhat
endanger the native and will give both good and
bad effects. The dasa of the 10th lord (Moon) and
the sub period of the 8th lord (Venus) will prove
fatal to his father when Saturn comes to Gemini in
transit and the native will be highly troubled and
will experience good and bad effects in an equal
measure. The Sth house is the house of kingdom.
‘When Saturn attains a trine from the ascendant,
strife will exist in the country due to reversals in
royal assignments. These are the effects for the

first dasa.

Notes: “Happiness after the age of 16 years”
vide sloka 5426 compels usto delink the present six
verses from Rudrasana Yoga which stipulates hap-
pinessetc. right from birth. Thus the presentslokas
are in continuity of sloka 5423 which deals with
‘Venus - Rahu conjunction & C. Itwill be clear that
Rudrasana Yoga has nothing to do with the kind of
presently mentionedeffects. Iam well aware of the
term “Cha” used in the first line of sloka 5426, But
this is the only way of reviving this vital portion.

The Istdasa referred tohere is apparently of the
Moon, The 2nd dasa is noted as that of Mars, the
3rd of Rahu, the 4th of Jupiter, the 5th of Saturn and
the like as the reader will note progressively reach-
ing sloka 5599 infra. These are all for Libra
ascendant. In between some special yogas are
discussed.

happiness in his dasa. The native will prosper and
will enjoy a great deal of happiness.

Notes: This is an independent clue, like sloka
5433. The instructions in 5434-(a) are in continu-
ation of effects mentioned earlier for Venus - Rahu
conjunction & C.

5434 - 5438, Sub-periods of Mars & Rahu:
(a) The first sub period of 2nd dasa (apparently of
Mars) will be a source of sickness. Strife will exist
inhiscountry apart from danger from theking. The
sub period of Rahu in the 2nd dasa will cause much
happiness.

(b) Irrelevant: The lord of the 5th in aspect to
Mars will destroy the king. Following this, imme-
diately there will be a change of ruler.

(©) After the age of 5, the native will be greatly
happy. He will recover his lost kingdom and will
enjoy royal authority. (4) The effects (being) stated
for Rahu’s position with a benefic’s conjunction or
aspect will bear truth. The sub period of Rabu
during the 2nd dasa will cause much happiness.
(That is, Rahu in Leo should be with a benefic to
give happiness.) Thus has to be said about the 6th
year from the time of birth.

Notes: (b) The 10th house is known as “Rajya

- sthana” whereas in some cases our textcalls the 5th
house as “Rajya sthana”.

5439 - 5441. Jupiter’s Sub-period: The sub
period of the 6th lord (Jupiter) who is strong will
give happiness in the 2nd dasa. One will enjoy
health, wealth, grains etc, robes, conveyances and
all kinds of happiness. A Brahmin ruler will be the
cause of great happiness for the subject. These
effects will occur in the 7th year in Jupiter's sub
period.

5442 - 5444¥. Saturn’s Sub-period: The 4th
lord Saturn should be in the 8th in Taurus. Then his
sub period will cause great danger in the dasa of
Mars. He will be troubled by illness like fever, boil,
ulcer etc. and be endangered through wild animals.
He will be compelled to live in another place. If
Saturn, being in Taurus, is in his own Navamsa,
much happiness will come to pass apart from one’s
maintaining sound health. He will construct anew
house and will gain lordship over villages and
lands. These results for Saturn's bhukti will mate-
rialise in the 8th year from birth.

Notes: In discussing the sub periods of Rahu,
Jupiterand Saturn, only one year each is considered
which is strange. Further some of these results are
against age factor.

5445-5448, Mercury’s sub-period: (a) Mer-
cury should be in Cancer at birth. Then his sub
period in the dasa of the 2nd lord (Mars) will
produce happiness, learning, intelligence, fame,
physical soundness, increase of prosperity and gain
of kingdom (authority).

(b) If Mercury is conjunct the Sun, then the
person will have financial gains.

(©) If Mercury is in aspect to the 10th lord, one
will gain kingdom, will celebrate auspicious func-
tions at home and will gather wealth.

Notes: Ketu’s sub period in Mars dasa is dis-
cussed in sloka 5452 etc. interrupted by Samrajya
Yoga.

5449 - 5451. Karma Samrajya Yoga: In case
ofamovable ascendant, the lord of the 10th, placed
Inanangle, in jointaspectto the lords of the 2nd and
the 11th will cause Karma Samrajya Yoga. The
native concerned will be wealthy, valorous, widely
famous, highly honoured, will take to virtuous
path, will serve a ruler, be ever libidinous and be
skilful in fulfilling royal duties. Further, he will be
happy on account of this Yoga, and will perform
good deeds. He will enjoy prosperity after the age
of 8 fora period of 16 years.

5453 -5455¥s. Venus’ Sub-period: In the 2nd
dasa, the sub period of the 8th lord (Venus) will
cause difficulties, physical unfitness, mental dis-
tress, heat and fever when Saturn comes to Leo in
transit. Further, his men will incur evils, strife and
royal enmity. If Venus is in his own Navamsa,
health and great happiness will exist. The native
will acquire learning and intelligence while his,
people will experience auspicious effects and
wealth. 10th year from birth will cause Raja Yoga.

5459 Akhanda Samrajya Yoga: If the lords of
the 1th, 9th and 2nd are in an angle from the Moon,
or if Jupiter is in the 2nd, 5th or 11th (from the
ascendant), one obtains unbroken kingdom.

5460 - 5460%. The native having Akhanda
Samrajya Yoga will be endowed with numerous
elephants, horses, chariots, palanquins and the like.
He will lord over forts etc., be an ocean of virtues,
will have a virtuous spouse, be intent upon illicit

der his rulership, be famous and righteous. He will

5461-5464. Rahu Dasa -Rahu’s Sub-period:
In the 3rd dasa, he will gain the kingdom of his
friends (i.e. will gain a good position through
friends). In the Ist sub period, he will experience
happiness, success, kingdom, marriage, beautiful
robes, omaments, great wealth provided Rahu is
related toa beneficby conjunction or aspect. These
apart, much financial gains and happiness in his
owncountry will come to pass. 23rd year will cause
happiness. These are the effects for Rahu’s sub
period.

5465 - 5470. Jupiter’s Sub-period: The sub
period of Jupiter in the dasa of Rahu will produce
Raja Yoga, when Saturn transits the sign Libra.
Further, the native will develop courage. He will
financially gain. His foes will be destroyed. He
will honour gods and Brahmins and will give
liberal donations. He will be involved in temple
festivals and will construct temples, towers, tanks
and the like. He will develop eloquence of speech,
skilful disposition, and physical splendour and will
become famous. Much opulence will accrue to him
and he will enjoy Raja Yoga effects. Auspicious
functions and association with beautiful women

will also follow. However, due to blemish of 6th
lordship, Jupiter’ ssub period can causesome amount
of distress, enmity with Brahmins, and physical
ailments to some extent. Its first half will give
happiness, while these adverse effects will be felt
in the 2nd half during the 16th year of age. Pros-
perity and happiness will occur, with the Sun’s
transit in Sagittarius, during the 16th year.

5471 - 5479, Saturn’s Sub-period: One will
gain an independent kingdom. Much happiness
will prevail with the transit of Saturn in the 11th,
apart from acquisition of royal power. Forone bom
in Rudrasana Yoga or Samrajya Yoga, this sub
period in the 3rd dasa will produce much prosper-
ity, wide fame, valour, extended kingdom, name in
many countries and acquisition of a great order.
One will exhibit skill various undertakings, will
gain eight kinds of wealth and will enjoy auspi-
ciousness and much happiness through spouse. He
will join others’ women, be libidinous, endowed
with pleasures and fortunes, be charitable and will
honour gods and brahmins. He will construct colo-
nies for Brahminsand temples. Saturn’s placement
in the 8th will however cause some distress to the
## Page 274

Chandra Kala Nadi 259

native, evils to maternal kinsfolk and physical

ailments. These effects will be due at the age of 21

in Saturn's sub period.

5480 - 5482. Chamara Yoga: The Sun in

Vargottama, the lord of the ascendant in a benefic

sign, and Mercury in the 10th from the ascendant

will produce Chamara Yoga. The effects thereof

are: One born in a royal family will become aking

whose feet will be resplendent with the brilliance

of diamonds studded in’ the crowns of millions of

prostrating kings drawn from various directions.

Further he will be long-lived, high-minded, be the

tuler of many countries, be virtuous and learned in

the Vedas.

5485 - 5490. (a) Bhagya Yoga - Libra As-
cendant: Bhagya Yoga is formedas the lords of the
11th and the 9th (the Sun and Mercury) join in
aspectto the lords of the 10th and 4th (the Moon and
Saturn). As a result, the person will be responsible
for construction of tanks, towers etc., be charitable,
happy and associated with temple festivals. Much
auspiciousness will accrue to his kinsfolk. He will
be intent upon giving donations, will worship
Brahmins and gods and will visit shrines, espe-
cially in the sub period of Mercury (in Rabu’s
dasa). He will enjoy charming women and will
listen to mythological stories.

(b) If Mercury is conjunct a malefic apart from
being related to Saturn by conjunction or aspect,
and begetting Pisces Navamsa, will lead to the
following results, during the period under discus-
sion. The subject will confront physical ailments.
More so his kinsfolk. He will be involved in
quarrels with his men. Some possibility of destruc-
tion of maternal relatives will also be there. These
effects have to be foretold for Saturn’s simultane-
ous transit in the 12th during the age of 21.

Notes: (a) Bhagya Yoga or a combination for
prosperity and fortunes can be drawn for other
ascendants also when the lords of the 9th and 11th
are together with a joint aspect of the lords of the
4th and 10th.

5491 - 54954, Sub-period of Ketu: (a) This
will be a source of much happiness. But when
Satur transits the natal Moon sign, there will be
some fear of physical sufferings followed by quar-
rels, trouble from foes and some fear for friends.

(b) Ketu in aspect to a beneficial planet will
help the person do good deeds and take to virtuous
path. He will enjoy excellent learning and enter-
tainment, will listen to mythological accounts, will,
visit pilgrim centres and will meet a powerful king.
There will accrue auspicious furtctions, apart from
much happiness on account of progeny and spouse.
One will give charities, will perform meditation
and be devoted to gods and brahmins.

(c) Should Ketu be in a malefic’s sign, one will
be somewhat sinful. He will develop illicit ties
with others’ women and will quarrel with friends
andrelatives. Good and bad results will occur apart
from Raja Yoga.

5496 - 5503¥3. Sub-period of Venus: (a) In the
3rd dasa (of Rahu) during the sub period of the 8th

lord (Venus), when Saturn moves in Capricom
during the 24th year of age of the native, he will
acquire royal kind of happiness, conveyances,
wealth and spouse. Abundant enthusiasm and
fame, gain of beautiful ornaments, and charming
articles like gems, corals and pearls. He will be
happy in hiskingdom. His kinsfolk will experience
much auspicious effects.

(b) Should Venus be together with Rabu at
birth, the sub period under study will give happi-
‘ness on account of union with others’ females. This
apart, he will acquire an excellent spouse and
women of various castes. A lot of his money will
be spent away. He will achieve many things, be
famous in many countries, and skilful in many
kinds of efforts (or jobs). He will enjoy Raja Yoga
and the blessings of the goddess of lucre. The
conjunction of Venus with Rahu will, however,
promote some quarrels on account of women,
accompanied by infamy and some fear of ill-
health. His relatives will be anguished. Involve-
ment in wicked deeds will also be possible.

(©) Jupiter’s aspect (on Venus) can produce
happiness and Raja Yoga. The person will be
courageous and enthusiastic.

5504 - 5508, Sub-period of the Sun: Should
Rahu be in the 11th (in Leo) while the 11th lord (the
Sun) is with the 9th lord (Mercury), the sub period
of the Sun (in Rahu’s dasa) will produce much
happiness. The native’s spouse, progeny and kins-
folk will enjoy health. Abundantornaments, acqui-
sition of beautiful articles, honours from the king
and much fame will follow the native. Due to
temple festivals, he will undertake journey to for-
ests and caverns. He will be courageous, and
## Page 276

Chandra Kala Nadi 261

enthusiastic and will meet a powerful king. Many
of his undertakings will attain fruition. He will
experience Raja Yogaeffects. All these will come
to pass in the 27th year.

5509 - 5512¥4, Sub-period of the Moon: (a) In
the 3rd dasa, during the sub period of the 10th lord
Moon, coinciding with Saturn's transit in Capri-
com, good and bad results will both come to pass.
The person will enjoy endless happiness and pleas-
uresinhis country. He will join others’ women and
will acquire lordship over a country or villages,
will construct tanks and gardens and will honour
gods and brahmins.

() With the Moon in the 6th from Rahu as
Mercury is conjunct the Sun, the effects for the sub
period of the Moon should be physical ailments,
mental distress, destruction of maternal relatives,
involvement in wicked deeds, trouble from en-
emies and to some extent fear of infamy possibly in
the 28th year.

5513 - 5517. Sub-period of Mars: (a) In the
3rd dasa (of Rahu) during the sub period of the 2nd

lord (Mars), physical ailments, mental distress,
quarrels, royal displeasure, evils to kinsfolk, lossof
money, grains and happiness, troubles from dis-
eases to family members, differences with rela-
tives and obstacles to one’s undertakings will
occur. These will be in the 29th year, along with
Saturn's movements in the sign Aries.

(b) Should Mars be in Indra Nadiamsa, mental
courage, great happiness, enthusiasm, leaning,
ornaments, destruction of enemies, success and a
mix of good and bad effects will come to pass
(during the sub period in question).

Thus should be described the 3rd dasa (ruled by
Rah, for a Libra native).

Notes: (b) “Indrasana Pada” isan unusual term

- not described in our text. Apparently, it can only be

interpreted as “Indra Nadiamsa” which has been
oft-occurring.

5518 - 5519. Koorma Yoga: (a) A strong
Jupiter in his own sign identical with either the 3rd
orthe 1 thas the lord of the ascendantis in the 11th,
will produce Koorma Yoga.

(b) Alternatively, a movable sign can ascend
whose lord occupies a fixed sign with fixed Nav-
amsa. The said ascendant lord should also be
related to Jupiter and Venus either by aspect or
conjunction. Here again, Koorma Yoga forms.

Notes: (a) This aphorism applies to Libra
ascendant while Jupiter is in Sagittarius (3rd) with
strength’and Venus is in Leo (11th).

The other ascendant that comes under the
purview of this Yoga is Taurus. Here, Jupiter with
strength should be in Pisces along with exalted
Venus.

(b) Another form of Koorma Yoga occurs to a
movable ascendant (Aries, Cancer and Capricom
only) whose lord should be related to Jupiter and
Venus and occupy a fixed sign (Taurus, Leo,
Scorpio or Aquarius) with one of such Navamsas.

‘The reader can well understand that this does not
apply to Libra ascendant for which Koorma Yoga
of a different kind is already listed.

5520. Effects: The subject born with Sri Koorma
Yoga (of any of the three kinds cited above) will
enjoy wide fame on the earth, will be honoured by
the ruler, attached to virtues, be primarily gentle in
disposition, ever happy, will perform gentle deeds
and be charming among men.

$521. Jupiter’s Dasa: This dasa occurring as
the 4th one will generate a Raja Yoga if Jupiter at
birth is in his Moolatrikona while Venus is in the
9th thereof (i.e. in Leo) with a benefic Navamsa.
Notes: This is one particular kind of Koorma
Yoga discussed specially for Libra ascendant in a
previous verse.

Koorma Yoga.

5523 - $529, (a) One will lose his kingdom
(positiou) in the sub period of the 8th lord (Venus)
during the middle part of Jupiter's dasa. He will
also be anguished and be subjected to great fear.
The concluding part of Jupiter's dasa will give
happiness and Raja Yoga effects.

(b) Allkinds of wealth will occurin the first sub
period of Jupiter's dasa.

(©) During the 30th year, Saturn transiting in
Aries will cause royal enmity and obstacles to
one’s undertakings. He will develop enmity with
friends, will lose his wealth and be subjected to
fear. His kinsfolk, spouse and progeny will be
anguished. The first half of Jupiter's dasa will be
distressful while the later half will be a source of
much happiness. Sdtumn’s movements in Gemini
will bless one with every kind of happiness and a
great deal of Raja Yoga, wide fame and valour.

(d) Irrelevant: Satu’ s journey in Taurus will
usher famine and will destroy the state. The entire
world will cry in grief. The native will be happy in
his country in the 31st year.

5530 - 5532. During the currency of Saturn's
sub period, the transit of Saturn in Taurus will give
much happiness to the person. He will head over
towns and villages, be honoured by army chief and
king, be intent upon helping others, will meetanew
king, and enjoy happiness on account of spouse and
progeny. His kinsfolk will also be greatly happy.
There will be auspicious functionsin his family and
he will join others’ women.

5533-5535. A malefic’ssub period in Jupiter’s
dasa will cause much fear if Saturn occupies the 6th
from Jupiter, or even the 8th from the ascendant
(Libra). Additionally, the person will be inflicted
by windy diseases, will incur enmity with other
kings, be troubled by quarrels with friends and will
lose his relatives. His wealth will decline in many
away. Good and bad effects will be equal in the
said sub period occurring in the 24th year.

5536 - $542. Mercury’s Sub-period: (a) The
sub period of Mercury in the 4th dasa (of Jupiter)
will give auspicious effects with Satum’s sojourn
in Gemini. One will be much happy due to auspi-
cious and pleasurable deeds and through spouse
and progeny. He will like to join beautiful women.
Happiness will accrue to him in his own country.
He will have abundant financial gains. He will give
charities, perfom meditation and will attain fruits
due to fulfilment of "Shatkarmas". He will visit
temples in connection with divine festivals. His
travels will prove auspicious and his undertakings
will yield expected results.

(b) Should Mercury be in the 6th or 8th from
Jupiter, in relation to a malefic by aspect or con-

junction, the sub period in question will cause
‘distress among one’s kinsfolk and circle of friends.
The native will earn enmity with another ruler.
There will be strife in his own country. His people
will be anguished and his money will be spent on
many counts.

(©) Should Mercury be aspected by the 10th
lord (Moon), Raja Yogaeffects will be experienced
in the former’s sub period including courage and
enthusiasm in the 36th year.

Notes: "Shatkarma" needsaclarifiation. Vedic
literature prescribes six kinds of duties for a Brah-
min, These are Adhyayana (studying or repeating
of the Vedas), Adhyapana (teaching of the Vedas),
Yajana (offering sacrifices), Yaajana (conducting
sacrifices for others), Dana (giving donations) and
Pratigraha (accepting gifts). ‘Thus, the native in
question will accomplish these duties and will
attain spiritual wisdom.

5543 - 5545¥s. Sub-period of Ketu: If Ketu at
birth is in the 5th house, the transit of Saturn in
Cancer will be the cause of great fear, in the sub
period of Ketuin Jupiter’ sdasa. There will be strife
in one’s country. He will develop enmity with his
relatives, will lose his position or place and be
subjected to great fear. His travels will also cause
fear. He will face quarrels, fear from enemies,
more expenditure than his income, loss of courage
and will indulge in calamitous deeds. He will incur
disorders of urine at the age of 27.

5546-5551'4, Sub-period of Venus: (a) When
‘Saturn comes to the 8th, the sub period of Venus,
in the dasa of 6th lord (Jupiter) will produce great
fear. If Venusand Rahuare together in the 8th from
the Moon, the said transit will destroy the kingdom
and wealth. His undertakings will be destroyed
suddenly and he will have no profits. His health
will decline. He will be anguished, and be worried
about many of his undertakings, be humiliated and
will face obstacles to work.

(b) Irrelevant: Saturn's movements in Leo
will usher a famine in the country. The people will
cry in distress due to drought and strife.

(c) If Venus is in own Navamsa (while being
with Rahu & C as at “a” above), the 2nd half of the
sub period will cause some happiness. One will

beget courage, show enthusiasm and will experi-’

ence good and bad effects in an equal measure after
the age of 36 and 41. (This should be independent
of the sub period in question.)

5552 - 5556%.. The Sun’s Sub-period: (a)
When Saturn moves in Virgo, the sub period of the
‘Sun in Jupiter's dasa will produce much happiness.
One's undertakings will be fruitful due to a power-
ful king. The person will acquire courage,
adventurism, happiness, royal honour, economic

gains, increased fame and agreeable talks with
virtuous men. He will have abundant gains of gold
and clothes.

(b) If the Sun is in aspect to the 10th lord
(Moon) one will gain happiness through a friendly
ruler.

(©) Should the Sun be in the 8th from the dasa
lord (Jupiter) one will display ignorance in his
undertakings. Physical troubles, mental distress,
limited happiness and an equal measure of good
and bad effects will occur.

5557 - 5566%. (a) The Moon’s Sub-period:

_During the currency of the Ist half of the sub period

of the 10th lord (Moon), Saturn’s movements in
Virgo with a malefic’s aspect will cause anguish,
destruction of matemal kinsfolk, indulgence in bad
deeds, expenditure in (unfruitful) travels, bad con-
duct, mental troubles, loss of work, indolence and
dullheadedness.

(b) The Moon joining Jupiter in Gemini Nav-
amsa will cause much happiness in the 2nd half of
## Page 280

Chandra Kala Nadi 265

the said sub period with ‘upiter and Saturn in order
journeying in the signs Gemini and Libra. The
native will enjoy a good deal of royal honours
including from a new ruler. The undertakings
aimed at by him will materialise. He will gain
wealth through a captured kingdom (i.e. will re-
cover lost wealth), enjoy abundant wealth, be
happy in his country and will gain an independent
kingdom, wide fame and valour in the 42nd year.
with the transit Sun in Gemini and Cancer. The Sun
moving in trines to these two signs will also cause
happiness. The native will receive communication
from a powerful king and will gain wealth.

(©) Should the Moon be related to Mars by
aspect or company, one will be troubled by wild
animals to some extent, in the said sub period. The
Moon aspected by 9th lord (Mercury) will increase
prosperity in her sub period. Aspected by the 11th
lord (Sun), she will produce much financial gains
in the sub period under consideration, These
effects are to be stated for the 42nd year.

5567 - 5572%. Mars sub-period: (a) Saturn
moving inLibra will be a source of much happiness
in the sub period of Mars during the dasa of Jupiter.
He will enjoy life-span, health, acquisition of
progeny and be endowed with kingdom and kins-
folk. His fame and valour will be on the increase.
He will display courage and great enthusiasm and
will beget Raja Yoga effects.

(b) Due to 2nd and 7th lordship of Mars, he will
be somewhat endangered by ailments, ulcer, heat

and fever and will lose his spouse and progeny.

(c) Mars in Indra Nadiamsa (instead of
Indrasana) will cause Raja Yoga, success in under-
takings and much prosperity.

(@) If Mars be in a malefic’s Navamsa, travels
and strife will come to pass (in his sub period).
These effects are for the 43rd year.

Notes: (c) For Indrasauia, see notes under
of sloka 5513 - 5517 supra.

5573 - 5578. Rahu’s Sub-period: (a) If Rahu
isin the 11th (Leo) at birth, his sub period in the 4th
dasa (of Jupiter) will cause much happiness. The
dasa lord’s aspect on Rabu will produce happiness
in one’s own country, apart from increased pros-
perity, great enthusiasm, acquisition of spouse and
progeny, auspicious undertakings, auspicious jour-
neys, performance of good deeds, charitability and
Raja Yoga.

(b) Rabu’s conjunction with the 8th lord Venus
(in Leo) will cause troubles, physical ailments and
some fear in the 2nd half of Rahu’s sub period (in
Jupiter's dasa) with the transit of Saturn in Libra.
The native will find other kings turning inimical to
him and be involved in strife and royal wrath. His
kinsfolk will be anguished. These are the effects
for the junction of dasa occurring at the age of 45
(i.e. for Rahu’s sub period in Jupiter's dasa which
will give way to Saturn’s dasa).

(c) In the 4th dasa (of Jupiter) good and bad
effects will take place in a balanced manner.

from the ascendant contain planets in (simultane-

5580 - 5585. (a) The native having this Yoga
will be a ruler of the earth, according to Yavanas.
‘The 5th dasa (of Saturn) as a consequence will give
Yoga of a special order. The subject will enjoy
happiness through spouse and children in his own
country. In the first half of the dasa, all-round
happiness will prevail. The 2nd half will cause
physical troubles for one born in Libra.

(b) Saturn’s sub-period: In the 5th dasa (of
Saturn) and in his own sub period, Raja Yoga
effects will occur with his transit in Sagittarius.
The native will acquire abundant wealth. His
undertakings will be fruitful and his spouse and
progeny will prosper.

(©) Physical ailments and much fear will be the
-Fesult in the said sub period, if Saturn at birth is in

aspect to Mars. Fear of untimely death, particu-
larly in in-laws’ family will exist. To overcome
this, recitation of Mrityunjaya Japa should be
undertaken.

5586-5590. Mercury’sSub-period: (a) In the
dasa of the Sth lord Saturn, the sub period of the 9th
lord (Mercury) will give happiness, all kinds of
prosperity (to self), prosperity to spouse and chil-
dren, wide fame, valour and happiness in own
country when Saturn transits Capricorn.

(b) If Saturn is in Aries Navamsa along with
Mercury, the sub period of the latter will produce
abundant fear from enemies. Other rulers will be
inimical to him. He will be financially distressed.
His relatives will be anguished. The end of the sub
period will be his source of difficulties.

(©) If Mercury is aspected by the 10th lord
(Moon), there will be Raja Yoga effects. He will
perform various kinds of charitable activities and
will ea wisdom, piety and fame. These effects
will be due in the 51st year.

in the Sth at birth; his sub period in Saturn’s dasa

will produce strife in the country and enmity with
servants. One will incur enmity with other rulers
and be subjected to fear in journey. Physical
ailments, mental distress and great fear will follow.
52nd year will give a mixture of good and bad

effects.

5593 - 5596, Sub-period of Venus: (a) In the
dasa of the Sth lord (Saturn), the sub period of the
8th lord (Venus) will give good and bad effects with
the movements of Saturn in Aquarius. Ilness,
distress, obstacles to work, loss of wealth, quarrels,
fear from foes, expenditure beyond income, evils
to kinsfolk particularly to in-laws, family quarrels
and an equal measure of good and bad effects will
come to pass.

(b) Should Venus be in his own Navamsa,
effects of Raja Yoga will be revealed.

5597 - 5599. (a) In the-dasa of Saturn, Who at
birth is placed in the 8th, and in the sub period of
the lord of ascendant and 8th (i.e. Venus), the
native will die in his 53rd year, with the southerly
course of the Sun in Leo, or Virgo, or Libra, in
bright lunar half, on a Saturday mornin,

(b) In this birth, he will be a meritorious soul
and will attain the feet of Lord Vishnu. The effects
reflected for Manohara Nadiamsa in Deva Keralam
will be truthful.

Notes: (a) “Randhra Chakra dasa Kaale” is an
interruption to the whole set of slokas so far occur-
ring, which discussed the Ist to 5th dasas in a

sequence for Libra ascendant. Hence this is re-
moved from the translation.

5601 - 56082. (a) He will have uo older co-
born if the lord of the ascendant (Mercury) isin the
11th (Aries) while the 3rd is occupied by 11th lord.
(Mars). He will have younger ones: and be honour-
able.

(b) The 4th lord (Mercury) in the 11th (Aries)
in the company of a benefic, and begetting Sagit-
tarius Navamsa, denotes birth in a street spread
from the west to east. The town concemed will
have beautiful compound with a river in the north
as the town’s omament. His birth will be in such
surroundings.

(c) If the lord of the ascendant is aspected by

see notes under
## Page 283

268 Deva Keralam

Jupiter, predict birth beside a temple due to divine
worship and the blessings of Lord Siva.

(@) Previous Birth: The lord of the 9th in the
11th along with Mercury and Venus and in aspect
to the 10th lord (Jupiter) denotes his previous birth
in a royal clan, If Satum is in Vargottama Nav-
amsa, he was an army chief or a minister. He had
eight wives, happiness and five sons. He died in the
Gist year due to dysentery. His present birth is in
Gemini’s Kamini Nadiamsa.

5609 - 5615. The present native will be charm-
ing, intelligent, eloquent, endowed with attractive
face and eyes, be fortunate, virtuous, blood-red in
complexion and handsome. He will have limited
food and sleep, will speak within limits and be
discerning. He will be inclined to serve the ruler,
will have many an attendant, be garrulous, truthful,
leaned in many ancient branches, will have a
beautiful name, be philosophical and will attain
Wisdom. He will be fond of listening to divine
stories, will honour gods and Brahmins, be knowl-
edgeable in Philosophy, addicted to joining fe-
males, devoid of paternal fortunes, and will have
self-earned wealth. Symbols will be found on his
palms and soles. He will be fond of good people, be
crafty towards the wicked and will have eight
brothers out of whom one will be long lived while
others will be destroyed. The native will enjoy Raja
Yoga effects:

5616 - 5626. (a) The 2nd lord (Moon) in
exaltation while her dispositor (Venus) is in the
lth related to a benefic by aspect or conjunction
will cause abundant financial gains.

(b) One will be replete with uninterrupted
wealth like Kubera (god of wealth) if the 4 houses
viz. the 2nd, 9th, 11thand 10th arein strength. (The
2nd line of sloka 5617 has another reading -
"Sampuma vittam satatam”. This slokaalso occurs
under S. No. 5387 supra.)

() General: One’ financial prosperity will be
in such a country denoted by the 2nd house or the
sign occupied by the 2nd lord. If Capriom be the
2nd house, he will have universal gains. Financial
prosperity can also take place in such a country
denoted by the sign having the highest bindus in
Sarva Ashtaka Varga.

(d) Gemini Ascendant (Continued): One will
locate treasure if the 11th house is occupied by
Venus who enjoys the aspect of Saturn. However,
## Page 284

Chandra Kala Nadi 269

Venus should in the process be not in his debilita-
tion Navamsa.

(e) Venus in the company of Saturn will cause
enjoyment of pleasures and proximity to the ruler.
The person will join other women, be libidinous,
will repent for his sins and will (later) conquer his
senses. He will be wise, endowed with philosophi-
cal knowledge and be famous

(® Venus in the 11th (in Aries) will give
abundant finances in 3rd dasa and 4th dasa. (The
term “Indra Yoga” is to be ignored.)

(g) Venus related to the 10th lord (Jupiter) by
aspect or conjunction will cause acquisition of
treasure.

(h) The lord of the ascendant in the 9th (orin the
9th Arudha pada) in aspect to Jupiter will produce
much prosperity. Moreover, one will be endowed
with co-born and long-lived parents.

(@ Jupiter arriving in transit in the sign identi-
cal with the Navamsa occupied by the 2nd lord
(Moon), or a trine thereof, or the sign that is
aspected by the 2nd lord, will cause financial
upliftment.

5627 - 562872. (a) Add the longitudes of the
lords of the 2nd, 11th and 10th and find out the
Navamsa indicated by the resultant sum. The sub
period of such a Navamsa lord will give abundant
financial gains in Jupiter's dasa.

(b) Venus in 11th: The 3rd and 4th dasas will
give abundant financial gains (if Venus is in the
11th, vide sloka 5623 supra). Further, the person
will enjoy wide fame and valour and will have his
undertakings fulfilled, The effects so far discussed
are for Kamini Nadiamsa, Ist half, in the ascendant
Gemini.

Notes: (a) Instead of the 2nd lord (Moon), the
lord of the ascendant (Mercury) is denoted in
another version.

5629 - 5630. (a) The 3rd house aspected or
occupied by Mars will make the subject truthful
and discerning.

ute to valour, longevity and strong mind.

5632 - 5638. (a) Birth in Gemini ascendant
while Saturn is ina movable sign with 3 or 4 A.V.
Bindus will kill younger co-born.

(b) The 3rd house in aspect to Saturn and Mars
will cause death of younger co-born. These two
planets in male signs (while aspecting the 3rd) will
kill younger brothers. If these are in female signs,
younger sisters will pass away.

(c) Should these two aspect the 7th lord Jupiter,
or the significator Venus, the native will have two
wives.

(@ Marsin the 3rd house with female Navamsa
will not allow female co-bom to live long; his
position in male Navamsa will affect male co-born
in a similar manner.

will have six or seven co-born, These are the

5639 - 5642. (a) Mars and Venus being in
Vargottama in any of the three signs, viz. Sagitta-
rius, Aries or Leo (either jointly or individually)
will give ownership of abundant wealth apart from
valour. The native will worship a deity having a
name akin to his, be firm in disposition and valor-
ous.

(b) The lord Of the 4th, Mercury, in the 11th
(Aries) in relation to Jupiter and Venus by aspect or
conjunction will bless one’s mother with longev-
ity.

(c) Should the planet signifying mother, i.e. the
Moon, be in exaltation while the owner of that sign
isin the 1 1th (Aries) and Rahu is in the 4th (Virgo),
happiness concerning mother will come to pass.

(@) The lords of the 4th and the 9th related to
Jupiter by conjunction or aspect will bring royal
honour. Further, the person will be munificent,
endowed with all kinds of wealth.

5646 - 5649. (a) Rahu in the 4th as the Moon
is with the Sun and Mercury is with Rahu will cause
long life to mother who will be virtuous, pure and
devoted to her husband.

(b) For a native of Kamini Nadiamsa, 1st half,
in Gemini ascendant, the placement of Rahu in the
4th (Virgo) will make the native a deserving son.
He will be the light of the family, and will gain
villages, lands etc. all of which will prosper. His
desires will attain fruition due to the mercy of an
emperor. He will gain horses, palanquins and such
status symbols as royal umbrella and chowrie.

5650 - 5651. (a) The lord of the Sth (Venus) in
the company of Saturn will cause wealth ending
with elephants.

(b) The Moon in “Krida Avastha” will give
happiness through sexual enjoyment. The subject
will join beautiful women and be ever profound in
disposition. He will be skilful in three languages,
be famous, and will patronize his relatives.

5652 - 5654¥2, Mother’s Death: (a) Note the
lord of the 8th from the Moon sign. During the said
planet’s dasa and in the sub period of a malefic
planet, indicate mother’s death in the thick of many
difficulties.

(b) When Saturn moves in the sign denoted by
the Navamsa occupied by the 10th lord, or ina trine
thereof, or in the sign that is aspected by the 10th
lord, mother’s death will occur.

(©) During the sub period of the 1 1th lord in the
4th dasa, with Saturn moving in Taurus and the Sun
in Libra, Scorpio or Pisces, danger to mother be
declared.

5655 - 5657. (a) Birth of a Son: Venus in the
Sth with full strength in aspect to Jupiter will ensure
acquisition of a son.

(b)Poor-feeding: The subject will be disposed
to feed the poor if the Sth is occupied by a benefic

planet.

(©) If the Sth lord is in Gopuramsa, one will be
sharp-minded and valorous.

(d@ Should Jupiter (the 10th lord) be strong, one
will be a king’s minister.

Notes: The Sth lord in the Sth may generally
be a good augury for betting progeny. However,
Venus in the Sth may be pre-disposed to give
female issues. He needs Jupiter’ s support to cause
a male issue.

and two long-lived daughters.

5659 - 5660. Jupiter’s Dasa: Fora person born
in the former half of Kamini Nadiamsa, Gemini
ascendant, Jupiter in the Sth in Paravatamsa will
produce much happiness in his dasa. The sub
period of the 9th lord (Saturn) will cause prosperity
(in Jupiter’ sdasa). The subject will constructanew
house, will acquire lordship over villages, landed
properties, conveyances, omaments and female.
offspring.

Notes: Hamsa Yoga is caused by Jupiter, only
when he is in his own sign or exaltation, identical
with an angle but not when he is in the Sth. Hence
the word “Hamsa Yoga” appearing in sloka 5659 is
erroneous and omitted from the rendering.

5661 - 5667/2. Various Yogas: (a) Having the
9th lord in the 1 1th with strength while the 10th lord
isin the Sth, the subject will gain superior convey-
ancesand abundant wealth due to royal favours. He
will be honoured by the head of a region and will
acquire elephants, horses, wealth, gold, robes,
omaments and numerous conveyances.

(b) The dispositor of Jupiter being in an angle
or a trine with reference to Jupiter and obtaining 1,
2or3 Ashtaka Varga Bindus will give headship of
family and wealth.

(©) Venus being in angle to Jupiter, identical
with the 1 1th or atrine from the ascendant, or these
two aspecting each other, will produce a king or
one equal to a king.

(@) Note the sign containing maximum number
of A.V. Bindus. That of the four directions ranging
from the east, as indicated by the said sign, will be
the source of conveyances, robes and ornaments.
One should construct a house in that direction. In
other directions, destruction of the house will
result.

(©) One will obtain such number of offspring as.
indicated by the number of A.V. bindus found in
the Sth place from Jupiter. Children born in excess
of this will only die.

(©) The number of Navamsas past by the 5th
lord, or significator (Jupiter) will indicate number
of children.

5671 - 5675¥2, (a) One will have a sound and
sturdy physique if the 6th lord Mars is in an angle
or trine from the 6th house in a friendly sign, and
obtains a benefic Navamsa.

(b) Ability to achieve an undertaking and val.
our will be given by Mars in the 3rd in Leo with
Sagittarius Navamsa.

(©) Ifthe Navamsa dispositors of the lords of the
6th, 8th and 12th are benefics, one’s foes will run
away like an elephant that has dreamt of a lion,

(@) Mars indicates urinary disorders while the
Sun rules diseases of the blood vessels. The Moon
causes windy disorders and more worries because
of diseases.

5676-5677. (a) When Satum reaches by transit
the sign identical with the sign denoted by the
Navamsa occupied by the 6th lord (Mars), one will
face much difficulties, physical ailments and great
fear.

(b) With the 8th lord (Saturn) in the 6th (Scor-
pio) while Mercury being in conjunction with Mars
(in a particular sign) and obtains Sagittarius Nav-
amsa, or when Mercury isin a Martian sign, the sub
period of Mercury will produce diseases.

5678 - 5682. If the 7th lord (Jupiter) isin the Sth
(Libra) in conjunction with or aspect to its lord
(Venus) as Mercury is ina sign of Mars (only Aries)
in the company of Venus and in aspect to 9th lord
(Saturn), the subject will enjoy longevity, will have
two wives, be the head ofa village, be a scholarand
of sacrificing disposition. He will gather prosper-
ity and be involved with exciting activities in royal
Because of this Yoga (caused by
Jupiter & C), he will be happy and prosperous right
from birth. He will have many wives (?), be
wealthy, disposed to join others’ women, will have
two wives, be honourable and associated with a
‘woman equal to spouse (i.e. will have concubine).
As a result the said female’s prosperity will in-
crease and her progeny will become prosperous.
She (i.e. the lady beyond wedlock) will be virtuous,
devoted to the native, be gentle, intelligent, highly
fortunate, of good conduct, learned, handsome and
will please the native. She will have sons and

daughters. These are the effects concerning the
female akin to spouse of the native.

5683. Yoga for 2 Wives: If the 7th lord isin the
‘Sth while the Sth lord is in the 11th (or is) in aspect
to the 7th lord, one will have to wives.

Notes: Two different yogas are implied in this
partially defective sloka.

The first one is: The 7th lord (Jupiter) is to be
in the Sth (Libra) while the Sth lord (Venus) is in
11th (Leo).

The second one is: The 7th lord (Jupiter) is to
be in the Sth (Libra) and be aspected by Venus
(from Aries). To wit, Jupiter should be aspected by
Venus.

5684 - 5686¥. (a) In case of Gemini ascendant,
Venus joining Saturn will cause association with
two women equal to spouse.

(b) Note : if the 7th lord (Jupiter) is in the Sth
(Libra) while the Sth lord (Venus) is with Saturn,
with the company or aspect of a debilitated planet.
‘As a consequence, the person’s first wife will be
issueless, irascible, foolish and will have enmity
with the native. Due to the native’s sins of previous
birth, she will incur (premature) death.

(©) Satum in the Navamsa of his debility will
cause a bad end to one’s spouse. Her death will be
in a well or due to consumption of poison.

Notes: (c) This is to be read with the Yoga
mentioned at “b” and is not independent.

5687 - 5690. (a) The native (of Gemini ascend-
ant, Kamini Nadiamsa) will lose the said spouse
after his 22nd year of age and will enjoy happiness
through his concubine. His prosperity will increase
and he will enjoy much happiness.

(b) If Venus is endowed with strength, the said
native will be fond of illicit associations with
women. He will have either four or seven wives.

(©) Should the 7th lord Jupiter be in a movable
sign with Gemini Navamsa, there will be two wives
hailing from one’s own community. The associa-
tion of Jupiter with Venus will cause the spouses
hail from other communities.

These are the effects relating to spouse.

denotes a bride from the community of traders,
‘Venusa prostitute and Saturn one from a wicked (or

5693 - 5702. (a) If the 7th lordisin full strength,
due to exaltation or retrogression, (in the above
‘Yoga involving Rahu), the person will be endowed
with much prosperity and will enjoy Raja Yoga
effects. His marriage will occur in the dasa and sub
period of Rahu. After the age of 18, Jupiter coming
in Aries, Gemini or Leo will cause marriage for a
Gemini native with Kamini Nadiamsa.

(b) The 9th house is the house of 2nd wife. If the
lord of the 9th is with a benefic, one will have two
wives. He will have a house in his own region and
be happy after the age of 25. His spouse will be
blood-red in complexion, foolish in disposition,
will please her husband to some extent and be
endowed with male and female issues - two each.
Female issues will be destroyed for the subject
native. He will obtain four children from a girl
equal to his spouse. Male issues will live long while
daughters will die premature deaths. The other
woman referred to above will be equal to the
native’s second wife, be virtuous, prosperous, de-
voted to the native and will fear him. He will gain
two daughters. One of the sons will be long-lived.
Concerning the woman akin to 2nd wife, she will
be garrulous, skilful, expedient, intelligent and
highly fortunate.

period will produce evils tohis spouse. She will fall
sick for which immediate remedies be sought.

(b) In Rahu’s sub period of Jupiter's dasa, she
will incur sickness. The native will be highly
troubled and will fall sick. To overcome this blem-
ish, prayer to Rahu be offered.

(c) Satum’s dasa, Satum’s sub period, will
destroy the other woman equal to spouse. His
daughters will be destroyed and he will be sub-
jected to great fear in this phase of time.

5710-5713. a) The person will lead agood life
if the 8th lord is in the 11th (Aries) along with
Mercury.

(b) Saturn, the lord of the 8th and the 9th,
placed in the 11th in aspect to or association with
abenefic will bless one with all kinds of happiness.

(©) Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga: Thelord of the
8th (Saturn) in debility with Vargottama Navamsa,
while its lord (Mars) is in an angle to the Moon will
give pleasures of a great order throughout one’s

life. With this Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga, the
subject will be equal to an emperor. His prosperity
will rise after 25. He will enjoy wealth and fame.
