S715 - 5728. (a) Effects: The above-men-
tioned native will prosper after the age of 16, be
exceedingly charming, saintly and will enjoy pros-
perity for a period of 16 years. In the end of this,
Yoga period (i.e. at 32), he will incur danger of
untimely death and will be physically troubled ina
war.

(b) Saturn’s conjunction with a benefic will
give much wealth, The first dasa will give a great
deal of general happiness including from parents.
Learning, intelligence, fame, wealth and grains
will increase. The 2nd dasa will give wealth to the
native and his family. He will be greatly happy,
with robes, conveyances and omaments. He will
enjoy learning, intelligence and fame. His father
etc. will be very much happy. He will earn fame in
royal service and be happy througha friendly king.
He will progress by the day like the Moon of the
bright half. He will enjoy headship, be independ-
ent, and powerful in royal service. If Rabu joins
Mandi (Gulika), a sub period ruled by a malefic in
the 2nd dasa will be a source of great danger. With
Saturn’s movement in Scorpio, the sub period of
Rahu in Rahu dasa will cause marriage. He will
undertake a journey tothe north-west to meet a
king and will gain a new job and abundant wealth.
Jupiter's sub period in this dasa will give happiness
through a friendly king, with Saturn’smovementin
Sagittarius. He will gain a better job with the help
of aBrahmin ruler, will gain fame in royal service,
will desire illegal union with women, will enjoy
much fame, valour and much financial gains. He
will perform good and charitableacts. The 3rd dasa
during Saturn’ s sub period will cause distress after
the age of 21, when Saturn will be moving in
Capricorn. He will have fear of injury, fall etc. He

will be endangered through foes, thieves and fire:

‘There will occur enmity with another king, coupled

with evils to relatives and danger in journeys. if

Saturn is conjunct a benefic, enemies will be
destroyed and success will adore the native, He

will gain happiness through a friendly king and his
undertakings will attain fruition.

$729 - 5730. Calculation of Nadiamsas: In
movable signs, there are 150 Nadiamsas (in the
order mentioned below). The order is to be re-
versed in case of immovable or fixed signs. In dual
or common signs, these commence from the 76th

Nadiamsa.

(The names of the 150 Nadiamsas are given

below as in the original.)

‘These are the Nadi(amsas) as once stated by
Lord Brahma. Without knowing the effects of the
Nadiamsa, the ascendant cannot be decided. (That
is effects based on ascendant will be futile without
the help of rising Nadiamsa.)

Notes: The way the 150 Nadiamsas should be
known have been fully explained in the preface in
Book 1. These are not being repeated here to avoid
verbosity.

Sat SYA AMT ATE FAT |
wera Tara Fatssreshrohay 143k Ut

5731. Again, without the knowledge of the sign
ascending, only untruth will arise; such predictions
will be —unfrutiful or useless. The ascendant's
‘Nadiamsa should be examined and the effects of a
horoscope be decided.

Notes: In the present sloka, the point that
Nadiamsa and ascendant are mutually complimen-
tary is driven home. Mere ascendantis not enough
without knowing the exact Nadiamsa rising in it.
In the location of the correct Nadiamsa, the accu-
racy of the ascendant is implied.

5732 - 5739. Yogas for royal status, fortunes,
illegal births, physical defects, peculiar births,
poverty, livelihood, worldy affairs, effects of ma-
jor periods and sub periods, evils due in a major
period, remedies for such evils, profession, and
world after death are to be known (through the use
of Nadiamsas) according to my preceptor. Life-
span, untimely death, ascetism, loss of position,
birth in a Brahmin's family, birth of sons, worship
of a deity, time of father's death, discarded kins-
folk, final rites to father etc. (can also be known
through Nadiamsas) according toBhargava's school
of thought. Effects due to occur to the king in
accordance with the time period (i.e. the age of
living) and the country, its well-being, alien rule,
famine, plentifulness etc. can also be studi
(through the relevant horoscope) according to the
injunctions of Lord Siva.

5740 - 57491/2: Planets Described: (a) The
‘Sun has a symmetric body. He is a king and has
othernames, viz. Dyumani and Divakara. Heis fire
(personified), fierce and causes happiness to the
world. He is the ruler of bile ("Peefa" reads as
"Pitta" in another version which is used in the
rendering), quadrupeds and the direction east. His
hue is golden.

(b) The Moon is strong in the nights. Sheis fair
in complexion and is the ruler of north-west. She
is perishable by nature (i. e. she wanes periodi-
cally), is handsome (when waxing), sagely, phleg-
matic, is watery (lit. has the form of water), has a
body subject to decrease and increase, is learned,
soft-spoken and has attractive eyes.

(©) Mars resembles fire. He is sharp and is
(known as) "Kona". The direction south is ruled by
him. He isa youth, of royal scion and has a square
body. He is known as "Vakra" (or crooked) and has
blood-red complexion. His eyes are fierce and his
belly depressed.

(@) Mercury resides in villages, is handsome,
‘courageous and has a composition of all the three
humours (viz. wind, phlegm and bile). He is
learned, dark in complexion, the lord of the north-
em direction, and fond of music and all kinds of
tastes. He is Tara's son, highly intelligent, coura-
geous, and fond of jokes.
## Page 295

280 Deva Keralam

(e) Jupiter is a Brahmin, winner of senses,
attached to performing sacrificial rites, highly phleg-
matic, sweet, knower of feelings (or truth) and very
righteous. He rules gold, is of middle age, follows
Vedic path and is the lord of north-east.

(f) The planet Venus is handsome, is formed of
water ("Jalamaya"), passionate, fond of love and
is a middle-aged female. She is a Brahmin, is the
lord of south-east, and fond of music. Her body is
resplendent. She lives in islands and has charming
eyes.

(g) Saturn is fond of Soodra females and is the
lord of the western direction. He is harmful in
disposition, lame, old and has a "stout" body.

(b) Rahu's head is like that ofa snake. Heis like
Saturn and wanders during nights (like demons).
Ketu is equal to Mars, is phlegmatic, of variegated
hue and is fikle-minded.

Notes: (b) The word "Kshara” relating to the
Moon has various meanings like medicinal salt,
molasses, perishable, mutable and dissoluble. Since
the Moon wanes in the dark fortnight, the meaning
chosen in the rendering may be treated as appropri-
ate although this quality is reflected in the words
("Kshaya Vriddhi Tanuh".)

(c) The word "Kona" for astrological purposes
has twomeanings. Itisa synonym of Saturnas well
as Mars. Most of the texts however denote only
Saturn by this term while Mars is never indicated.

(f) Although it is a tradition in English transla-
tions to use masculine pronoun "be" for Venus, in
this portion "she" is used as an exception toremain
in line with the context.

(b) Rahu is compared to Saturn and Ketu to
Mars, in line with the misused popular adage -
“Sanivadrahu kujvatketu". The comparison is
testricted to the general disposition of the two
nodes and does not allow swapping Rahu with
Saturn and Ketu with Mars for every convenient
purpose on the earth.

5750-5753. Rast Divisions: (a) Thirty degrees
constitute a Rasi (lit. ascendant).

(b) Half of it is called Hora.

(©) Drekkana is a third of the sign.

(@) Dvadasamsa is one twelfth of a sign.

(e) Trimsamsa is constituted by thirtieth parts.

(© The drekkana distribution should in order be
done thus: (to) the lords of the Ist, Sth and 9th in
case of movable signs, the lords of the 9th, Ist and
Sth in case of immovable signs, and the Sth, 9th and
the Ist (in case of dual signs).

(g) For positions in Aries, Leo and Sagittarius
count from Aries. For Capricorn, Taurusand Virgo
count from Capricorn, For Libra, Aquarius and
Gemini, count from Libra. For Cancer, Scorpio
and Pisces, count from Cancer. (Thus are the
Navamsa distribution to be understood.)

Notes: In these verses, Rasi, Hora, Drekkana,
Trimsamsa, Dvadasamsa and Navamsa calcula-
tions are casually touched. The reader is already
aware of these in a fuller measure. Yet clarifica-
tions on two points are necessary as under.

(a) The word "ascendant" should not be misin-
terpreted that the ascendant is always 30 degrees in
length irrespective of interruption by another sign,
and that no Bhava divisions be made with the help
of the meridian as taught by the likes of Parasara
and Sripathi. Our author just took some liberty and
used the word "ascendant" which means a sign.

(d) The drekkana distribution suggested is par-
tially different from what we already know. Incase
of immovable and dual signs, an unknown concept
is introduced. Serious probe is a must whether we
so far know the right thing. According to this text,
for example, in Taurus, afixed sign, the 3 decanates
are in order ruled by Saturn (Capricorn), Venus
(Taurus) and Mercury (Virgo) and not Venus (Tau-
rus), Mercury (Virgo) and Saturn (Capriom). In
case of a dual sign, for example Gemini, the three
decanates are ruled by Venus (Libra), Aquarius
(Saturn) and Gemini (Mercury) instead of tradi-
tionally known rulers Mercury (Gemini), Venus
(Libra) and Saturn (Aquarius).

5754. Bhavas: The Kendras are the ascendant,
10th, 7th and the 4th. Panapharas are those next to
Kendras, and Apokilmas are the next to Panapharas.
The 6th, 8th, 12th and the 3rd houses are trouble-
some.

5755. The sign occupied by the Moonis called
Chandra Lagna. Note the number of signs inter-
vening between the ascendant and its lord. So
many signs from the ascendant lord will be the
Arudha Lagna. If the ascendant is unaspected by
malefics but by the Moon and acendant lord, long
life will come to pass.

5756. Bhava Lagna: Commencing from the
Sun's degree (as at Sun rise), progress at the rate of
5 ghatikas (or 120 minutes) per sign till the time of
birth to get Bhava Lagna (sphuta or longitude).

5757. Chandra Ayur Lagna: Note the Moon's
Navamsa position (at Sun rise) and distribute the
birth time in ghatikas (from Sun rise) till the time
of birth at the rate of 5 ghatikas per sign to beget the
Ayur Lagna (i. e. ascendant for life-span).

5758-5759, Hora Lagna: Commencing from
the natal ascendant, distribute the time of birth in
ghatikas etc. at the rate of two ghatikas and a half
persign to get Hora Lagna. Divide the day ornight
duration by 12 and distribute in the direct order if
the birth ascendantis an odd sign, and in the reverse
order in.case of an even sign asending.
Notes: The 2nd line of sloka 5758 has another -

version thus: "Vilagnabhaak prayaati lagnam
tannama hora lagnamihochyate".

5760 - 5761. Ghatika Lagna: At the rate of
one ghatika per sign, distribute the time of birth
commencing from the Sun to obtain Nadi Lagna
(or GhatikaLagna). If the natal ascendant, Ghatika
Lagna and Hora Lagnaare all occupied by exalted
planets, or two of them be so, a Raja Yoga is
formed.

5762 - 5764. N.B. Although the caption de-

| notes the acendant prevailing at the time of com-

mencement of a dasa, nothing is given in the text in
this respect. The next line states: "Now about
yogas for opulence through the Moon sign." The
other part of the slokas which are again defective
and mutually unrelated conveys the following.)
If the lord of the Moon's Nakshatra is exalted,
or even debilitated (?), wealth will be acquired by
the person. Distribute at the rate of 5 ghatikas from
the ascendant. If the Nakshatra lord is Ketu the
## Page 297

282

distribution is to be done in the reverse order.
Except for Ketu, the calculations should be done in
ascending order while for Ketu, it should be re-
versed. If the lord of the first Nakshatra is exalted

or debilitated or is in a movable sign, Yoga for

wealth (Isvarya Yoga) should be drawn.

5765 - 5771. Shashtiamsas: (Now explained
are the 60 Shahtiamsas of a sign). (1) Ghora, (2)
Rakshasa, (3) Deva, (4) Kubera, (5) Rakshogana,
(©Kinnara, (7) Bhrashta, (8) Kulaghna, (9) Garaala,
(10) Agnisandhi, (11) Maaya, (12) Pretapurisa,
(13) Apampathi, (14) Deva, (15) Ganesa, (16)
Loka, (17) Ahi, (18) Amritamsu, (19) Chandra,
(20) Mridu, (21) Komala, (22) Padmanabha, (23)
Lakshmisa, (24) Vagisa, (25) Digambara, (26)
Deva, (27) Ardra, (28) Kalinasa, (29) Kshiteeswara,

Deva Keralam

(30) Kamalakara, (31) Mandatmaja (or Gulika),
(32) Mrityu, (33) Kaala, (34) Davagni, (35) Ghora,
(36) Yamakantaka, (37) Sudhamrita, (38)
Paripooma Chandra, (39) Visha, (40) Pradigdha,
(41) Kulanasa, (42) Vamsakshaya, (43) Utpataka,
(44) Kalaroopa, (45) Saumya, (46) Mridu, (47)
Susitala, (48) Damshtrakaraala, (49) Indumukhi,
(50) Purisri, (51) Kalagni, (52) Dandayudha, (53)
‘Nirmala, (54) Subha, (55) Asubha, (56) Atiseetala,
(57) Sudha, (58) Payodhi, (59) Bhramana and (60)
Induraka (read Indurekha). Thus should be known
in order (the various Shashtiamsas in an odd sign).
Reverse the order in case of an even sign. Planets
in beneficial Shashtiamsa will grant good effects
while the ones in an evil Shastiamsa will destroy
everything. Ifplanetsare endowed with full strength,
be in exaltation, in Moolatrikona....... (text incom-
plete).

Notes: The first line of sloka 5767 should
contain the expression "Deva + Ardra bhagau
Kalinasaa bhago" providing for Ardra Shashtiamsa
placed in between Deva and Kalinasa, as per
standard literature. But for "Ardra” shashtiamsa,
we will be short of one Shashtiamsa, or will have
to make some unscrupulopus manipulation.

Further, some of the Shashtimsas in this work
are named in a different style which may be noted.
Sometimes, asynonym is used, and yetsome times
a totally different name is given.

dispositor, strength, virtues (association etc.),
ocupation of a Bhava, dasas, Cheshtas, Avasthas,

5773. Declaration of effects (of a horosope)

without considering the Time period (i.e. relevant
age) will be futile akin to a lamp sans oil.
## Page 298

Chandra Kala Nadi

wes gare fora seared Aer:

nafinifed ferrenri aaa sree |

aeefa 8 data 3 a afdene: arefaq yor tt
5774. (In the word "Kaala" - %1@ - meaning

time), the letter "Kaa" - ®1 - is the reason for the

three worlds, while the other letter "La"- & -

stands for Great Dissolution. The Matras (one Guru

283

and two Laghus, equalling three Laghus) denote
the three Benefactors (Brahma, Vishnu and
Mahesvara). Brahma is of the nature of Kaala. i.e.
he is himself Kaala. Knowing of Kaala ever leads
to attainment of Punya or merits. One who knows
of it is the knower of Tatva, is a sage and appella-
tion of Brahma.

Finis
